# Strategy Guide: Grid Strike - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#strategy-guide-grid-strike)

Strategy Guide: Grid Strike[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#strategy-guide-grid-strike "Permanent link")

====================================================================================================================================

**Grid Strike** (`grid_strike`) is a [Controller](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/v2-strategies/controllers/)
 in Hummingbot's StrategyV2 framework that automatically places a grid of buy or sell orders within a set price range.

Unlike [basic grid strategies](https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/24240367765730)
, Grid Strike utilizes [Executors](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/v2-strategies/executors/)
 which dynamically manage each order level with configurable take profit and stop loss settings. This means every position opened by the strategy is automatically monitored and closed according to your risk parameters. It can be configured to move with the market trend —- either up or down -- letting you adapt the strategy to changing market conditions.

This guide explains how the Grid Strike strategy works and helps you decide when to use it. You'll learn how to configure and run the strategyt, as well as the key concepts and how to set it up for trending markets.

**Strategy Code**: [https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/master/controllers/generic/grid\_strike.py](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/master/controllers/generic/grid_strike.py)

Strategy Overview[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#strategy-overview "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/long-grid.png)

*   **Grid-based Order Placement:** The strategy lets you place either a **Long Grid** or a **Short Grid** that places buy/sell orders across a defined price range
*   **Automated Risk Management:** Each side utilizes Grid Executors that automatically manages the risk of each position with configurable take profit, stop loss, and time limits
*   **Exchange Flexibility:** Works on both spot and perpetual exchange connectors, including support for **one-way position mode** on perp exchanges
*   **Highly Configurable** Can be tailored for various market scenarios (trending up, trending down, or consolidating) or trading objectives (volume, liquidity, P&L, etc) by adjusting price range, order spacing, and other parameters

Running the Strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#running-the-strategy "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Recommendations for New Users

If you're new to Grid Strike, it's highly recommended to review [**Strategy Configuration**](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#strategy-configuration)
 section below before running the strategy. These sections explain the essential concepts, parameters, and setup steps that will help you understand how the strategy works and how to configure it safely for your trading goals. Taking time to learn these basics will help you avoid common mistakes and get the most out of the strategy.

### Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

Before you run Grid Strike, make sure you have Hummingbot installed, either:

*   [**Hummingbot Client**](https://hummingbot.org/client/)
    : See [Installation Guide](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
     or [Source Installation](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#source-installation)
     to get started
*   [**Hummingbot API + Dashboard**](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/)
    : See the [Hummingbot Dashboard Quickstart Guide](https://hummingbot.org/blog/hummingbot-dashboard-quickstart-guide/)
     for a guide with step-by-step setup instructions

### Create Controller Config[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#create-controller-config "Permanent link")

In the Hummingbot client, run the following command:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-0-1) create --controller-config generic.grid_strike`

![1.png](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/1.png)

Afterwards, a file `conf_generic.grid_strike_1.yml` in `/conf/controllers` will be saved with the default starting configuration.

See [Strategy Configuration](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#strategy-configuration)
 below for an in-depth guide to adjusting these parameters.

### Create Script Config[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#create-script-config "Permanent link")

Once you are satisfied with your Grid Strike configuration, add the name of the file to the [Loader Script](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/scripts/examples/#v2-controller-loader)
:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-1-1) create --script-config v2_with_controllers`

![image.png](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/image.png)

If you have an existing load script configuration file, simply modify it to add the Grid Strike configuration you just made (e.g `conf_generic.grid_strike_1.yml`):

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-2-1) markets: {} [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-2-2) candles_config: [] [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-2-3) controllers_config: [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-2-4) - conf_generic.grid_strike_1.yml [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-2-5) script_file_name: v2_with_controllers.py [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-2-6) max_global_drawdown_quote: null [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-2-7) max_controller_drawdown_quote: null`

### Run Loader Script[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#run-loader-script "Permanent link")

From the Hummingbot client interface, start the loader script with this config file:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-3-1) start --script v2_with_controllers.py --conf conf_v2_with_controllers_1.yml`

Alternatively, use this CLI command to skip password entry and automatically start the strategy:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-4-1) /bin/hummingbot_quickstart.py -p [PASSWORD] -f v2_with_controllers.py -c conf_v2_with_controllers_1.yml`

### Monitoring the Strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#monitoring-the-strategy "Permanent link")

When you start the Grid Strike strategy, it first looks at your configuration to figure out how to set up your trading grid. Once the grid is set up, the strategy begins placing orders based on the set `side` (buy or sell) within your specified price range. It places order and waits for it to fill. Whenever `order_frequency` is reached, it checks if it needs to place a new order.

Run the `status` command (or `status --live` for constant refresh) to get an in-depth overview of the strategy performance:

[![image-1.png](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/image-1.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/image-1.png)

When an order gets filled and opens a position, the Triple Barrier system immediately takes over to manage that specific trade. This system acts a personal risk manager for each filled order or position: it will automatically close the position if it reaches the `take_profit` or if it moves from the set `stop_loss` against you. This happens automatically without you having to watch the screen.

The strategy then continues this cycle: place a sell order, wait for it to fill, let the Triple Barrier manage the position until it closes, then place another sell order. This creates a continuous trading loop that tries to capture small profits repeatedly while keeping your risk controlled.

Strategy Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#strategy-configuration "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After you run `create --controller-config generic.grid_strike`, an initial config file in `/conf/controllers` will be saved with the starting configuration below:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-1) id: <controller-id> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-2) controller_name: grid_strike [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-3) controller_type: generic [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-4) total_amount_quote: '1000' [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-5) manual_kill_switch: false [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-6) candles_config: [] [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-7) initial_positions: [] [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-8) leverage: 20 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-9) position_mode: HEDGE [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-10) connector_name: binance_perpetual [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-11) trading_pair: WLD-USDT [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-12) side: 1 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-13) start_price: '0.58' [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-14) end_price: '0.95' [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-15) limit_price: '0.55' [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-16) min_spread_between_orders: '0.001' [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-17) min_order_amount_quote: '5' [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-18) max_open_orders: 2 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-19) max_orders_per_batch: 1 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-20) order_frequency: 3 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-21) activation_bounds: null [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-22) keep_position: false [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-23) triple_barrier_config:   [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-24)   open_order_type: 3  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-25)   stop_loss: null  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-26)   stop_loss_order_type: 1  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-27)   take_profit: '0.001'  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-28)   take_profit_order_type: 3  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-29)   time_limit: null  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-30)   time_limit_order_type: 1  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-5-31)   trailing_stop: null`

### Key Parameters[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#key-parameters "Permanent link")

[![](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/long-grid-order-executor.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/long-grid-order-executor.png)

Here are some of the most important settings:

| Parameter | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **total\_amount\_quote** | Your budget for the bot (e.g., 1000 USDT) |
| **connector\_name** | The exchange to trade on (e.g., `binance_perpetual`) |
| **trading\_pair** | Market you want to trade (e.g., `WLD-USDT`) |
| **side** | 1 = Buy (long), 2 = Sell (short) |
| **start\_price** | The price at which your bot starts placing orders |
| **end\_price** | The price at which your bot stops placing orders |
| **limit\_price** | If price falls below this level for Buy grids (or rises above for Sell grids), the bot stops trading and terminates positions (or moves them to `PositionHold` based on `keep_position`) |
| **min\_spread\_between\_orders** | Minimum spacing between orders (e.g., 0.001 = 0.1%) |
| **min\_order\_amount\_quote** | Smallest order size allowed by exchange (e.g. 10 for 10 USDT) |
| **order\_frequency** | How often the bot checks for new orders (in seconds) |
| **position\_mode** | For perp exchanges: `ONEWAY` or `HEDGE` |
| **keep\_position** | If true, terminated executors store the positions in `PositionHold` bucket |
| **activation\_bounds** | Controls which grid orders are actually placed based on their proximity to the current price. If set to `null`, all grid orders within your price range are placed, exposing your full capital. If set to a value like `0.005`, only orders within 0.5% of the current price are placed, while others are calculated but not exposed—acting as a risk control. For example, during a sudden price drop, only nearby orders are active, preserving capital for better entry points. |

Make sure to set the most important parameters like:

*   Exchange (`connector_name`): Use any Hummingbot CLOB connector, such as `hyperliquid` or `hyperliquid_perpetual`
*   Trading Pair (`trading_pair`): Use a valid trading pair supporte by the exchange, e.g. `BTC-USDT`
*   Budget (`total_amount_quote`): Total amount allocated to the strategy, expressed in the quote asset
*   Direction (`side` = buy or sell): Place either a Long (buy) Grid or Short (sell) order grid
*   Grid Dimensions: Start price and end price

### Triple Barrier Parameters[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#triple-barrier-parameters "Permanent link")

![](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/triple_barrier.png)

Grid Strike creates Grid Executors that implement the Triple Barrier Method, similar to [Position Executors](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/v2-strategies/executors/positionexecutor/)
. Set these parameters to define how each order is managed if it is filled:

| Setting | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| **stop\_loss** | Automatically close the trade if it moves against you |
| **take\_profit** | Close the trade once profit target is reached |
| **time\_limit** | Close the trade after a set time, win or lose |
| **order\_type** | Choose between 1 - Market, 2 - Limit, or 3 - Limit-Maker orders |

Tips & Tricks[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#tips-tricks "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Three Building Blocks of Grid Strike[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#three-building-blocks-of-grid-strike "Permanent link")

To set up Grid Strike effectively, you need to balance these three foundational considerations:

**1 - Price Range**

This is the zone where your orders will be placed, defined by **start\_price** and **end\_price**.

*   Narrow range: Best for calm markets with little movement.
    
*   Wide range: Better for volatile assets but spreads orders out more thinly.
    

Example:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-6-1) Price Range = end_price - start_price [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-6-2) Your case: $6.8 - $5.5 = $1.3 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-6-3) Price Range % = ($1.3 / $5.5) × 100 = 23.6%`

**2 - Budget**

How much money you set aside determines how many orders the bot can place, defined by **total\_amount\_quote**. Note that this is expressed in units of the quote asset, the second symbol in the trading pair. We utilize this convention across V2 strategies to help you manage budgets across multiple bot instances.

*   The bot divides your budget by the minimum allowed order size from the exchange.
*   Each order level requires money reserved upfront. If you don’t have enough funds for all orders, the bot won’t place them.

Example:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-7-1) # capital support [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-7-2) Max Orders by Budget = total_amount_quote ÷ min_order_amount_quote [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-7-3) Your case: $100 ÷ $7 = 14.28 ≈ 14 orders maximum`

**3 - Spread Levels**

This is how far apart your orders are from each other, controlled by the **min\_spread\_between\_orders** parameter:

*   **Small spread (e.g., 0.5%)** = more orders close together → captures small moves but needs more funds.
*   **Large spread (e.g., 2%)** = fewer, wider orders → targets bigger moves and uses less capital.

Example:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-8-1) Max Levels = Price Range % ÷ min_spread_between_orders [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#__codelineno-8-2) Your case: 23.6% ÷ 0.7% = 33.7 levels (theoretical max)`

Your final grid will be a balance of these three factors. The bot will create the maximum number of orders it can, respecting both your capital limit and the minimum spread you've set within your chosen price range.

### Long vs Short Grids[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/#long-vs-short-grids "Permanent link")

[![](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/long-vs-short-grids.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/long-vs-short-grids.png)

Think of Grid Strike as a way to “ride the wave” of a trend:

**Short Grid (Betting on Price Going Down)**

If you expect the price to fall, set `side` to 2 and define the expected downward price trajectory using `start_price` and `end_price`. Use `min_order_amount_quote` and `min_spread_between_orders` to space the orders in your grid.

Grid Strike places sell (short) orders at gradually lower prices. As the market moves downward, these orders are triggered one by one, building up your short position and letting you profit from the downtrend.

If you are anticipating for continuing downtrend movement, you can setup the Grid Strike with `start_price` above the `end_price` as shown below.

![Gridstrike for Short.png](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/Gridstrike_for_Short.png)

**Long Grid (Betting on Price Going Up)**

If you think the price will rise, set `side` to 1 and define the expected upward price trajectory using `start_price` and `end_price`. Similarly, use `min_order_amount_quote` and `min_spread_between_orders` to space the orders in your grid.

Grid Strike places buy (long) orders at gradually higher prices. As the price climbs, your orders are triggered, adding to your long position and helping you profit from the uptrend.

Conversely, if you are anticipating for price reversal at support level or continuing uptrend, you can setup the Grid Strike with `start_price` below the `end_price` as shown below.

![GridStrike for Long.png](https://hummingbot.org/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/GridStrike_for_Long.png)

This "averaging into a trend" approach is the key difference. A traditional grid buys low and sells high in a sideways range (fighting the momentum), whereas Grid Strike joins the momentum, adding to a winning long or short position as the trend develops in your favor.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Strategy%20Guide%3A%20Grid%20Strike+-+/blog/strategy-guide-grid-strike/)
.

Back to top

---

# Running a Trading Bot with Hummingbot Dashboard on dYdX V4 - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4)

Running a Trading Bot with Hummingbot Dashboard on dYdX V4[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4 "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![cover](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/dydx-connector-guide.png)

Welcome to the new dYdX v4 Hummingbot Connector Guide, your comprehensive resource for integrating and trading on dYdX's perpetual contracts using Hummingbot. Using the latest version of dYdX Chain based on the Cosmos SDK and Tendermint Proof-of-stake consensus protocol, dYdX v4 improves the trading experience with more seamless onboarding, enhanced security, and improved speed and performance!

In this guide, we'll walk you through the process of integrating your dYdX account into Hummingbot and running a directional algorithmic trading bot. Let's dive in!

Connecting Your MetaMask Wallet to dYdX[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#connecting-your-metamask-wallet-to-dydx "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_Note: dYdX supports multiple wallets, but for this guide, we'll use_ _MetaMask__, one of the most popular Ethereum wallets. MetaMask allows users to interact with dYdX via its browser extension or mobile app._

### Install MetaMask[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#install-metamask "Permanent link")

Ensure MetaMask is Installed and Set Up

If you haven’t already, download and install the [MetaMask](https://metamask.io/)
 browser extension or mobile app. Create a new wallet or import an existing one.

### Connect Your Wallet to dYdX[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#connect-your-wallet-to-dydx "Permanent link")

Open your web browser and go to [https://dydx.exchange/](https://dydx.exchange/)
. On the dYdX homepage, click the **"Connect Wallet"** button, typically located in the top-right corner of the page.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/1.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/1.png)

From the list of available options, select **"MetaMask"**.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/2.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/2.png)

MetaMask will open a pop-up asking you to confirm the connection.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/3.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/3.png)
  
[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/4.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/4.png)

After the wallet is connected, dYdX will prompt you to sign a message to generate the dYdX Chain Wallet.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/5.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/5.png)
  
[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/6.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/6.png)

### Deposit Funds[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#deposit-funds "Permanent link")

Once your wallet is connected, you will need to deposit USDC into your dYdX account. USDC is the required trading currency on dYdX.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/8.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/8.png)

### Get dYdX Chain Address[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#get-dydx-chain-address "Permanent link")

After connecting, locate your **dYdX Chain Address** in the wallet connection settings. Copy this address and store it securely for future reference.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/9.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/9.png)

### Get dYdX Secret Phrase[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#get-dydx-secret-phrase "Permanent link")

To export your secret passphrase, go to the wallet connection settings dropdown and click **"Export Secret Phrase"**.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/10.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/10.png)

A window will appear. Check the box to confirm that you understand the risks. There may be a short delay before the export button becomes available.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/11.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/11.png)

Once available, copy the secret phrase and store it safely in a secure location. You will need this phrase later to connect to Hummingbot.

Using dYdX with Hummingbot Client[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#using-dydx-with-hummingbot-client "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Special dYdX Build

Currently, there are dependency conflicts between the dYdX v4 connector and the other Hummingbot exchange connectors. For this reason, we maintain a dedicated build of Hummingbot that is optimized for use with dYdX v4. Below are simplified instructions to get you set up quickly, whether you're using Docker or Source.

Below, we show you how to add your dYdX credentials to the command-line Hummingbot client. If you prefer to use dYdX v4 with the new Hummingbot Dashboard, go to [Using dYdX with Dashboard](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#using-dydx-with-dashboard)
.

### Docker Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#docker-setup "Permanent link")

**Clone the Hummingbot Repository**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-0-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot`

**Edit `docker-compose.yml`**

Navigate to the `hummingbot` project directory and open the `docker-compose.yml` file using an IDE like VSCode or a text editor.

**Update the Image Line**

Find the line that starts with `image:` under the `hummingbot` service. Modify it based on whether you want the latest stable or development version:

For the latest stable version:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-1-1) image: hummingbot/hummingbot:latest_dydx`

For the development version:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-2-1) image: hummingbot/hummingbot:development_dydx`

**Launch the Docker Container**

After updating the image line, clone the repository and launch the Hummingbot Docker container:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-3-1) cd hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-3-2) docker compose up -d`

**Attach to the Hummingbot Container**

Hummingbot should now be running in the background. Use the following command to attach to the running instance:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-4-1) docker attach hummingbot`

**Proceed to Add API Keys**

Once Hummingbot is running, follow the steps in the "Add Keys to Hummingbot" section below to connect your dYdX account.

### Source Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#source-setup "Permanent link")

**Clone the Repository**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-5-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot.git`

**Install with dYdX Support**

After cloning the repo, install Hummingbot with dYdX support by running the `install` command with the `--dydx` flag:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-6-1) cd hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-6-2) ./install --dydx`

**Activate the Conda Environment and Compile**

Activate the conda environment and compile Hummingbot:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-7-1) conda activate hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-7-2) ./compile`

**Start Hummingbot**

Run the following command to launch Hummingbot:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-8-1) ./start`

### Add Keys to Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#add-keys-to-hummingbot "Permanent link")

To connect Hummingbot to dYdX's v4 perpetual market, you'll need the dYdX v4 secret phrase and chain address from above.

From within the Hummingbot client, run the following command to start the connection process:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-9-1) connect dydx_v4_perpetual`

You will be prompted to enter your credentials:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-10-1) Enter your dYdX v4 secret passphrase (24 words) >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-10-2) Enter your dYdX v4 chain address (starts with dydx) >>>`

If the credentials are correct, you'll see the following confirmation message:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-11-1) You are now connected to dydx_v4_perpetual`

To verify the connection, run the **balance** command to check if the displayed balance matches your dYdX account:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-12-1) balance`

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/balance.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/balance.png)

### Run a Strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#run-a-strategy "Permanent link")

For this example, we'll use the [**bollinger\_v1**](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/development/controllers/directional_trading/bollinger_v1.py)
 directional trading controller.

**Create a controller config**

Run the `create` command from within the Hummingbot client to configure the controller:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-13-1) create --controller-config directional_trading.bollinger_v1`

**Create the configuration:**

You will be prompted to provide various configuration parameters. Feel free to adjust the settings based on your preferences:

*   Select **dydx\_v4\_perpetual** as the name of the exchange you want to trade on.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-1) Enter the total amount in quote asset to use for trading >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-2) Enter the name of the exchange to trade on >>> dydx_v4_perpetual [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-3) Enter the trading pair to trade on >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-4) Enter the maximum number of executors per side >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-5) Set the leverage to use for trading >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-6) Enter the stop loss >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-7) Enter the take profit >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-8) Enter the time limit in seconds >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-9) Enter the order type for taking profit >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-14-10) Enter the trailing stop as activation_price, trailing delta >>>` 

*   When prompted for the connector with the candles data, make sure to select a different connector other than **dYdX** since it doesn't currently support candles feed.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-15-1) Enter the connector for the candles data, leave empty to use the same exchange as the connector: [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-15-2) Enter the trading pair for the candles data, leave empty to use the same trading pair as the connector:`

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/12.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/12.png)

*   Once the configuration is done, give the controller config a name or use the default one:

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/21.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/21.png)

**Create a script config**

*   Next we have to create the script config for the **v2\_with\_controllers** generic script to run our controller config.
    
*   To create the script config, use the following command:
    

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-16-1) create --script-config v2_with_controllers`

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/script.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/script.png)

*   Make sure to enter the file name of the controller config we created earlier

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/script2.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/script2.png)

*   Lastly, give the script config a name

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/script3.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/script3.png)

**Start the strategy**

*   To start the strategy, use the following command. Note - if your config file has a different file name then replace the config name below

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-17-1) start --script v2_with_controllers.py --conf conf_v2_with_controllers_1.yml`

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/22.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/22.png)

**Monitor the Strategy**

You can monitor your bot by checking the logs for any errors and running the **status** command to view the current order status:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-18-1) status`

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/23.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/23.png)

**Stop the Bot**

To stop the bot, use the **stop** command. Please note that the bot will need some time to create orders to close out the positions.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-19-1) stop`

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/24.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/24.png)

For more details on V2 Strategies or other available controllers, check out the Controllers section of [V2 Strategies](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/v2-strategies/controllers/)
 documentation. Please note that since dYdX only supports **one-way mode** some V2 Strategies that use Hedge mode may not work correctly with the controller.

Using dYdX with Hummingbot Dashboard[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#using-dydx-with-hummingbot-dashboard "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hummingbot Dashboard is a web-based interface for Hummingbot introduced recently in Hummingbot 2.0 that allows you to perform backtesting, visualize your portfolio, and deploy/manage multiple bots. Since each bot is a Docker instance of the standard Hummingbot client, you can use the same credentials and API keys as the CLI method above.

### Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#installation "Permanent link")

**Clone the Hummingbot Deployment Repository**

First, clone the Hummingbot deployment repository to your local machine:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-20-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api`

**Navigate to the Hummingbot API Directory**

Change your working directory to the newly cloned `hummingbot-api` folder:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-21-1) cd hummingbot-api`

**Run the Setup Script**

Run the `setup_dydx` bash script to configure your environment for dYdX:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-22-1) bash setup_dydx.sh`

This script will pull the required Docker images specified in the `docker-compose-dydx.yml` file and launch them as containers. The initial image download may take some time.

You should see output like this once the setup is complete:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-23-1) [+] Running 7/7 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-23-2) ✔ Network deploy_emqx-bridge   Created [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-23-3) ✔ Volume "deploy_emqx-data"    Created [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-23-4) ✔ Volume "deploy_emqx-log"     Created [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-23-5) ✔ Volume "deploy_emqx-etc"     Created [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-23-6) ✔ Container dashboard          Started  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-23-7) ✔ Container backend-api        Started  [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#__codelineno-23-8) ✔ Container hummingbot-broker  Started` 

**Access the Dashboard**

After the containers have started, you can access the Dashboard by navigating to either:

*   [http://localhost:8501](http://localhost:8501/)
    
*   [http://127.0.0.1:8501](http://127.0.0.1:8501/)
    

in your web browser.

Cloud Servers

If you are using a cloud server or VPS, replace `localhost` with the IP of your server. You may need to edit the firewall rules to allow inbound connections to the necessary ports.

### Adding Credentials[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#adding-credentials "Permanent link")

To trade on dYdX, you need to add your API credentials via the Dashboard. You can manage multiple accounts and API keys easily, making it convenient to switch between sub-accounts when creating bots.

*   Click on **Credentials** from the left-hand menu to open the Credentials page.
    
*   In the **Add Credentials** section:
    
*   Choose the account (e.g., `master_account`) where you'd like to add the API keys.
*   From the "Select Connector" dropdown, select **dydx\_v4\_perpetual**.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/13.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/13.png)

*   Enter your dYdX secret phrase and chain address, then click **Submit Credentials**.
    
*   If the credentials are valid, they will appear under the **Available Accounts and Credentials** section, with `dydx_v4_perpetual` listed under the respective account.
    

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/14.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/14.png)

### Portfolio Page[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#portfolio-page "Permanent link")

Once your API keys are added, you can view the tokens and balances in your account under the **Portfolio** page. You can filter by account, exchange, or token to see more details.

For example, in this screenshot, there is a balance of $77.47 USD in the account:

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/15.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/15.png)

### Generate Configs[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#generate-configs "Permanent link")

Before running a bot, you need to generate a configuration. Head over to the **Config Generator** section and select **PMM Simple** (Pure Market Making strategy).

*   You can either use default configuration settings or customize them to suit your needs.
*   Since dYdX only supports **ONEWAY** mode (not HEDGE mode), make sure to set **Position Mode** to **ONEWAY**.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/16.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/16.png)

After setting your config:

1.  Scroll down and click **Upload** to save your configuration to the BackendAPI.
2.  Note: Since dYdX doesn't provide a candles feed, backtesting for this strategy isn't supported. If you're using a strategy that requires candle data, you'll need to configure a different exchange for your candles feed.

Once uploaded, navigate to the **Deploy V2** page to launch your bot.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/17.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/17.png)

### Launching Instances[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#launching-instances "Permanent link")

On the **Deploy V2** page, you should see the configuration you just saved.

1.  Give the instance a name.
2.  Select the Hummingbot image (make sure to choose **hummingbot/hummingbot:latest\_dydx** or **hummingbot/hummingbot:development\_dydx**).
3.  For credentials, choose `master_account` or the specific account with your dYdX API keys.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/18.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/18.png)

Click **Launch Bot** to start the instance. The bot will then appear under the **Instances** page.

### Managing Instances[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#managing-instances "Permanent link")

On the **Instances** page, you can monitor all running bots (both active and stopped). This page displays key metrics such as:

*   Net PNL
*   Volume Traded
*   Liquidity Placed

It also shows active controllers and the current configuration for each bot. You can easily access error logs and general logs to monitor the bot's status.

[![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/19.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dydx-v4-with-hummingbot/19.png)

### Stopping Instances[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#stopping-instances "Permanent link")

To stop a bot:

*   Check the box next to the active controller, then click **Stop**. This will close any open positions and stop the bot safely.
*   Alternatively, you can click the stop icon in the top-right corner, which will cancel all active orders immediately.

Note: The instance name will include **hummingbot** + the current date and time. This name appears in the top-left corner under **Local Instances**. If you need to manually attach to the Docker container, use this complete name to identify the bot instance.

Known Issues[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-dashboard-on-dydx-v4/#known-issues "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Connector Only Supports One-Way Mode**  
    Currently, the dYdX connector is limited to one-way mode, meaning it does not support hedge or multi-directional trading at the same time.
    
*   **Account Sequence Mismatch**  
    This issue arises when placing multiple orders in quick succession, particularly with a low refresh interval (e.g., 5 seconds). Due to inherent delays in decentralized exchange (DEX) transactions, the dYdX API may report an "account sequence mismatch" error, even when sequence numbers appear to increment correctly. This is a known limitation, as the platform struggles to accurately track sequences under high-order volumes. If dYdX introduces bulk order functionality in the future, this could alleviate the problem. For now, the system attempts to auto-correct the sequence number and retries the order up to three times, though this can result in orders not immediately reflecting on the web interface.
    
*   **No Backtesting Available**  
    The dYdX connector currently doesn't support backtesting. For strategy backtesting, it's recommended to use a perpetual exchange like Binance Futures as the source of historical data (candles).
    
*   **Wallet Geo-Restriction Warning**  
    Be aware that dYdX enforces geo-restrictions on wallets based in certain regions. Ensure compliance with local regulations before trading on the platform.
    

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

.

# Funding Rate Arbitrage and Creating Vaults on Hyperliquid - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid)

Funding Rate Arbitrage and Creating Vaults on Hyperliquid[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================================================================================================================================

![Cover](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/cover.png)

What Are Vaults?[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#what-are-vaults "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Hyperliquid Vaults** serve as an exchange wallet where funds are used by an individual trader or automated market maker (`Vault Leader`). Anyone can deposit (`Vault Depositors`) in the vault and earn a share of the profits.

The Hyperliquidity Provider (HLP) vault, operated by Hyperliquid, runs market-making strategies on each trading pair. Anyone can provide liquidity for HLP and share in the P&L of the vault. HLP doesn’t collect any fees directly, and its P&L is shared proportionally based on each depositor’s share of the vault. HLP is community-owned.

The minimum deposit to create a vault is 100 USDC.

How Vaults Work[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#how-vaults-work "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Earn a share of the profits, or losses, of the vault.
*   In the event of profit and decided to withdraw, 10% of the total earned goes to the Leader.
*   Vault are optional. You can trade on Hyperliquid with Hummingbot directly without using a Vault.

**Let's learn how to create a vault and use it with Hummingbot!**

* * *

For Vault Leaders[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#for-vault-leaders "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Enable Trading**

Log in to [https://app.hyperliquid.xyz/](https://app.hyperliquid.xyz/)
 using your preferred wallet (Metamask). You may have to switch to the **Arbitrum** network.

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image1.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image1.png)

Go to Vaults and then click the **Enable Trading** button.

Deposit some amount of USDC to start trading. The minimum trading deposit is 0.01 USDC, but note that you will need a minimum of 100 USDC to create a vault.

**Create a Vault**

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image2.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image2.png)

Enter the name for the vault and a short description.

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image3.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image3.png)

**Important Tips**

*   Make sure to check the vault name and description as they cannot be changed later.
*   Creation of a vault requires a minimum of 100 USDC.
*   Leaders need to always keep at least 5% of the total value of that vault. This rule is in place to ensure that you, as the vault leader, have an investment in the vault's success.
*   Leaders are not allowed to withdraw money from the vault if it would reduce their ownership to less than 5%. This requirement is designed to align their interests with the vault's performance and encourage responsible management.
*   Vault leaders receive a 10% profit share for managing the vault.

### Install Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#install-hummingbot "Permanent link")

There are two main methods to install Hummingbot:

**Docker**

For most new users, we recommend installing Hummingbot using Docker.

*   [Installation Guide](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
    

**Source**

We recommend installing Hummingbot from source if you meet any of the following criteria:

*   You want to customize or extend the Hummingbot codebase.
*   You want to build new components like connectors or strategies.
*   You want to learn how Hummingbot works at a deeper technical level.

Guides for various environments:

*   [Linux](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
    
*   [Windows](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
    
*   [macOS](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
    

### Trading with Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#trading-with-hummingbot "Permanent link")

Go to the Trade page.

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image4.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image4.png)

On the upper right corner, there is a dropdown to select which account to use. Select `Vault` here.

Launch Hummingbot and in the terminal run the `connect` command to connect Hummingbot to your Hyperliquid account:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#__codelineno-0-1) connect hyperliquid_perpetual`

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image7.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image7.png)

You should get a prompt to use the Vault address - enter Yes here.

Vaults are optional

If you just want to run Hummingbot on Hyperliquid without using a Vault, answer No to this question.

Next, you'll be prompted to enter your `Vault address`. If you don't know what the Vault address is, go to your Vault page, and the address would be at the top left under the Vault name. You can click the copy icon to copy the address to the clipboard and paste it in Hummingbot.

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image8.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image8.png)

Next, start a trading strategy in Hummingbot, and your bot trades should match the trades shown under the Vault.

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image11.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image11.png)

**Closing the Vault**

On your vault’s dedicated page, click the Leader Actions dropdown and select “Close Vault”. A modal will appear to confirm that you want to close your vault.

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image5.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image5.png)

*   All positions must be closed before the vault can close. All depositors will receive their share of the vault when it is closed.
*   What happens to open positions in a vault when someone withdraws?
    *   If there is enough initial margin to keep the open positions, withdrawal does not affect the open position.
    *   If there is not enough initial margin for the open position, a proportional amount of the withdrawal is closed for all open positions. For example, if a user comprised 10% of the vault’s total deposits, 10% of all open positions would be closed when they withdraw.

* * *

For Vault Depositors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/#for-vault-depositors "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Choose a Vault**

[![Image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image6.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/image6.png)

Users just need to go to the [Vault](https://app.hyperliquid.xyz/vaults)
 page and select Vaults they want to deposit into.

[![HLP](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/hlp.png)](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-hyperliquid-vaults-with-hummingbot/hlp.png)

Click "Deposit" and specify an amount. Afterwards, funds will be deducted from your trading account and transferred into the vault. There is a 24-hour lockup period for deposits on user vaults and 4 days for HLP vaults.

Afterwards, you will share in the performance of the vault. Check the **Vault Performance** and **Your Performance** tabs for details.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Funding%20Rate%20Arbitrage%20and%20Creating%20Vaults%20on%20Hyperliquid+-+/blog/funding-rate-arbitrage-and-creating-vaults-on-hyperliquid/)
.

# Using Osmosis with Hummingbot - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#using-osmosis-with-hummingbot)

Using Osmosis with Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#using-osmosis-with-hummingbot "Permanent link")

============================================================================================================================================

![cover](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/cover.png)

Introduction[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#introduction "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

These instructions help users navigate the installation, configuration, and operation of (OSS) Hummingbot high-frequency trading bot running Automated Market Making Arbitrage or Range Automated Market Making strategies on **Osmosis DEX** over the Hummingbot Gateway.

Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Create an Osmosis (Cosmos) Wallet[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#create-an-osmosis-cosmos-wallet "Permanent link")

The Cosmos ecosystem is powered by the Inter-Blockchain Communication protocol ([IBC](https://cosmos.network/ibc/)
) which functions like an internet of blockchains. Most Cosmos wallets support all IBC chains natively, including Osmosis - though _specific networks may need to be manually added_.

Stay up to date with Osmosis-specific wallet information on the Osmosis website and/or Docs:

*   [Osmosis Website - Supported Wallets](https://osmosis.zone/)
    
*   [Osmosis Docs](https://docs.osmosis.zone/)
    

#### Leap Wallet (recommended)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#leap-wallet-recommended "Permanent link")

To ensure the ability to easily export the wallet private key, use [Leap](https://linktr.ee/leap_wallet)
. Remember that the private key exported from Leap will include a ‘0x’ prefix which must be removed.

### Fund the Wallet[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#fund-the-wallet "Permanent link")

There are multiple bridges to Cosmos available, but the easiest way is to directly deposit to Osmosis using the Osmosis DEX front end.

*   [Connecting to Osmosis](https://docs.osmosis.zone/overview/educate/getting-started/#set-up-a-wallet)
    
*   [How to Deposit Funds](https://docs.osmosis.zone/overview/educate/getting-started#deposit-funds)
    
*   [Osmosis Interface](https://app.osmosis.zone/assets)
    

Note

Please ensure your wallet has sufficient balance, including OSMO for gas fees

  

Install Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#install-hummingbot "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two main methods to install Hummingbot:

### Docker[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#docker "Permanent link")

The **recommended** method for installing Hummingbot is using Docker.

*   [Installation Guide](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
    

### Source[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#source "Permanent link")

For advanced users who are comfortable using command line interface, installing from source allows for customization and private changes.

Guides for various environments:

*   [Linux](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
    
*   [Windows](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
    
*   [MacOS](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
    

  

Add Osmosis Wallet to Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#add-osmosis-wallet-to-hummingbot "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You will need to export the private key for your designated trading wallet. It is highly advised to have a dedicated account for this purpose.

Warning

Be aware an exported private key may have an 0x at the start, which may need to be removed to fit the private key format check.

→ Before: 0x1234567890...

→ After: 1234567890...

### Export Private Key from Keplr[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#export-private-key-from-keplr "Permanent link")

Keplr is the most popular Cosmos-based wallet. However, when creating a Keplr wallet, **only users who link a Google Account are able to export their private key** directly from Keplr.

Note

For a linked Keplr-Google wallet, find instructions to export the private key [here](https://help.keplr.app/articles/how-to-view-your-private-key)
.

To get around this, export the seed instead and then restore with a different Osmosis wallet which allows exporting private key.

To get a Keplr private key without a linked Google Account:

1.  Export Keplr seed phrase. ![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/view-keplr-seed.png)
    
2.  Restore the wallet using the seed phrase, in a different Cosmos-based wallet (such as Leap wallet) which allows exporting the private key. ![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/import-seed-leap.png)
    
3.  Export the private key from the newly restored wallet.
    

Warning

Your wallet seed phrase is extremely sensitive. Keep it confidential and never share it online or with anyone you don't trust. Always ensure you're in a secure and private environment when handling your seed phrase.

  

### Export Private Key from Leap[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#export-private-key-from-leap "Permanent link")

To export a private key from Leap wallet:

1.  Open Leap wallet and then open the wallet menu.
    
2.  Scroll down to the ‘Security’ section and select ‘Export Private Key’. ![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/export-private-key-leap-1.png)
    
3.  Enter the wallet password and proceed to view the private key by hovering the cursor over the blurred area.
    
4.  Copy down the private key and _be sure to discard_ the ‘0x’ at the beginning of the displayed key. ![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/export-private-key-leap-2.png)
    

Note

Images made with Leap version 0.10.7 and Keplr version 0.12.70; in Chrome.

### Add Private Key to Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#add-private-key-to-hummingbot "Permanent link")

Run `gateway connect osmosis` and follow the prompts to add a trading wallet to the Osmosis DEX connector.

Note

Hummingbot stores both API keys and private keys on the local machine in encrypted form, with the Hummingbot client password as the key.

![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/gateway-connect-osmosis.png)

#### Gateway Connect[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#gateway-connect "Permanent link")

*   To connect to **Osmosis mainnet** run the command below

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-0-1) >>> gateway connect osmosis`

*   To connect to the **Osmosis testnet**, select _'testnet'_ instead of 'mainnet' when prompted.
    
*   You will be prompted to enter your **Osmosis private key**. If valid, you should get a message saying **The connector osmosis\_osmosis\_mainnet is now using wallet osmo**
    
*   To confirm the connection, you can run the balance command below to see if Hummingbot is able to pull the available balance from the exchange
    

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-1-1) >>> gateway balance`

Run a Strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#run-a-strategy "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To begin trading, a strategy must be configured. The two strategies supported natively by the Osmosis connector are [**amm\_arb**](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/v1-strategies/amm-arbitrage/)
 and **amm\_v3\_lp**.

### Prepare[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#prepare "Permanent link")

1.  If you haven’t already, connect a wallet to Osmosis Mainnet with `gateway connect osmosis`. Afterwards, you should be able to see your `osmosis_osmosis_mainnet` wallet when you run `gateway balance`.
    
2.  To display the balance of specific tokens in the trading wallet balance, run `gateway approve-tokens osmosis_osmosis_mainnet <TOKEN_SYMBOL_HERE>`. The token balance will be displayed the next time `gateway balance` is run.
    
3.  Ensure the added trading wallet has at least **1 OSMO** to cover trading gas costs.
    

### Create New Strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#create-new-strategy "Permanent link")

Use the `create` command to set up an **amm\_arb** or **amm\_v3\_lp** strategy. Answer the question prompts presented so that you to generate your own version of the following example configurations.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-2-1) >>> create`

#### Example AMM\_ARB Config[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#example-amm_arb-config "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-1) strategy: amm_arb [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-2) connector_1: osmosis_osmosis_mainnet [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-3) market_1: OSMO-USDC [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-4) connector_2: uniswap_polygon_mainnet [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-5) market_2: USDC-WOSMO [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-6) min_profitability: 1.0 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-7) market_1_slippage_buffer: 1.0 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-8) market_2_slippage_buffer: 1.0 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-9) concurrent_orders_submission: false [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-10) debug_price_shim: false [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-3-11) gateway_transaction_cancel_interval: 600`

#### Example AMM\_V3\_LP Config[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#example-amm_v3_lp-config "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-4-1) strategy: range_amm [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-4-2) connector: osmosis_osmosis_mainnet [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-4-3) market: OSMO-USDC [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-4-4) fee_tier: MEDIUM [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-4-5) price_spread: 1.0 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-4-6) amount: 1.0 [](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-4-7) min_profitability: 1.0`

![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/create-amm-v3-lp-osmosis.png)

### Import a Strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#import-a-strategy "Permanent link")

Run `import` to get a list of locally stored files, use the arrow keys or tab to scroll and select one. Or, you can also skip the prompt by running `import <file_name.yml>` directly.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-5-1) >>> import`

![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/import-list.png)

If successful, you will see:

![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/imported.png)

### Start a Strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#start-a-strategy "Permanent link")

To run a trading strategy:

1.  Ensure a strategy is loaded and Preliminary checks are confirmed.
    
2.  Run `start`.
    

Note

The `amm_arb` strategy will first request confirmation of selected wallets.

![image](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/run-strat.png)

### Use a Script[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#use-a-script "Permanent link")

Scripts can be created similar to strategies, by using the `create --script` command.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-6-1) >>> create --script`

### Modify Strategy or Script Config[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#modify-strategy-or-script-config "Permanent link")

Modifications can be made to strategies and scripts by manually editing the file:

1.  Locate the `hummingbot/scripts` folder and open the relevant file using any text editor or an IDE like [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/)
    .
    
2.  Make the desired changes and save.
    
3.  Restart Hummingbot and run the command below to start the bot again:
    

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-7-1) >>> start <strategy_name.yml>`

Or, if using scripts:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-8-1) start --script <script_name.py>`

  

Useful Commands[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#useful-commands "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can run the `status` command shown below or press CTRL + S to check the bot status:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#__codelineno-9-1) status`

Note

The `status` command currently has a [issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/issues/294)
 with amm\_v3\_lp strategy.

Known Issues[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#known-issues "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   V1 Strategy compatibility for amm\_arb and amm\_v3\_lp are in development.
*   The fee tier ‘LOWEST’ is not supported and will result in an error.
*   In some cases, the token pair must be in alphabetical order.
*   PoolPrice RPC may time out intermittently which results in an error 500.

Potential Improvements[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#potential-improvements "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are interested in contributing or funding a bounty for development of additional features or improvements for this connector or related strategies, please feel free to contact [Pecunia.Finance](mailto:pecuniafinancedao@protonmail.com)
.

*   V1 strategy bugs
*   V2 strategy development
*   Data Feed
*   Custom CLMM range input (strategy development - amm\_lp\_v3 or new)
*   Future Osmosis Development - [https://forum.osmosis.zone/c/site-feedback/2](https://forum.osmosis.zone/c/site-feedback/2)
    

* * *

**\[Placeholder for additional Osmosis info\]**

* * *

Additional Resources[¶](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/#additional-resources "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Osmosis Mainnet](https://app.osmosis.zone/)
    
*   [Osmosis Testnet](https://testnet.osmosis.zone/)
    
*   [Osmosis Testnet Faucet](https://faucet.testnet.osmosis.zone/)
    
*   [Osmosis Labs GitHub](https://github.com/osmosis-labs)
    

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Using%20Osmosis%20with%20Hummingbot+-+/blog/using-osmosis-with-hummingbot/)
.
