# Commands Reference - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#commands-reference)

Commands Reference[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#commands-reference "Permanent link")

================================================================================================

`/start` - Main Menu[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#start-main-menu "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Welcome screen with quick access to all features.

**Main Menu Options:** - 📊 Portfolio - 🤖 Bots - 💱 Swap - 📊 Trade - 💧 LP - 🔌 Servers - 🔑 Keys - 🌐 Gateway - ❓ Help

`/help` - Command Guide[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#help-command-guide "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Interactive help system with detailed information about each feature.

`/portfolio` - Portfolio Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#portfolio-portfolio-management "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Comprehensive portfolio dashboard with real-time data and analytics.

### Features[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#features "Permanent link")

**PnL Tracking:** - 24h, 7d, 30d performance indicators - Deposit/withdrawal detection - Historical PnL charts

**Holdings:** - Token balances across all accounts - 24h price changes - Account breakdown

**Positions:** - Perpetual positions with unrealized PnL - CLMM liquidity positions - In-range status for LP positions

**Dashboard Graphs:** - Portfolio value history - Token distribution pie chart - Account breakdown

#### Settings[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#settings "Permanent link")

Click **⚙️ Settings** to customize: - **Time Period**: 1d, 3d, 7d, 14d, 30d - **Refresh**: Force update from exchanges

### Usage Examples[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#usage-examples "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-0-1) /portfolio                    # Show portfolio dashboard [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-0-2) Click "⚙️ Settings"          # Customize time period [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-0-3) Click "🔄 Refresh"            # Update from exchanges`

`/bots` - Bot Monitoring[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#bots-bot-monitoring "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Monitor and manage all your Hummingbot trading bots.

### Features[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#features_1 "Permanent link")

**Bot List:** - All active bots with status - Health and uptime indicators - Quick access to each bot

**Bot Details:** - System metrics (CPU, Memory, Uptime)  
\- Active controllers with performance - Recent logs

**Controller Management:** - View controller details - Performance metrics (PnL, volume, win rate) - Edit parameters (Grid Strike) - Start/stop controllers - View OHLCV charts

**Actions Available:**

1.  **View Bot**
2.  Select bot from list
3.  See all controllers and metrics
    
4.  **View Controller**
    
5.  Click controller name
6.  See detailed performance
7.  View charts (Grid Strike only)
    
8.  **Edit Controller** (Grid Strike)
    
9.  Click "📝 Edit"
10.  Modify parameters field-by-field
11.  Bulk edit with `key=value` format
    
12.  **Stop/Start Controller**
    
13.  Quick stop from bot detail
14.  Start inactive controllers
    
15.  **View Logs**
    
16.  Click "📝 Logs"
17.  See recent bot activity
18.  Refresh for updates
    
19.  **Stop Bot**
    
20.  Click "🛑 Stop Bot"
21.  Confirm to archive

#### Usage Examples[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#usage-examples_1 "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-1-1) /bots                        # List all bots [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-1-2) Click bot name              # View bot details [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-1-3) Click controller name       # View controller metrics [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-1-4) Click "📝 Edit"             # Edit parameters [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-1-5) Click "📈 Chart"            # View OHLCV chart (Grid Strike) [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-1-6) Click "🛑 Stop"             # Stop controller`

* * *

### `/trade` - CEX/CLOB Trading[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#trade-cexclob-trading "Permanent link")

Unified trading interface for centralized exchanges (spot and perpetual).

#### Supported Exchanges[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#supported-exchanges "Permanent link")

*   Binance (spot + perpetual)
*   Bybit (spot + perpetual)
*   OKX, Kucoin, Kraken, Coinbase, and more

#### Trading Functions[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#trading-functions "Permanent link")

##### 1\. Order Operations[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#1-order-operations "Permanent link")

**Place Market Order:** - Click connector → select exchange - Click pair → enter trading pair (e.g., `BTC-USDT`) - Click side → select BUY/SELL - Click type → select MARKET - Click amount → enter quantity - Use `$100` for USD value (auto-converts to units) - Use `0.01` for exact token amount - Click "✅ Execute" → confirm order

**Place Limit Order:** - Same as market order, but: - Click type → select LIMIT - Click price → enter limit price - Click "✅ Execute" → submit order

**View Orders:** - Click "🔍 Orders" from trade menu - See all open orders - Filter by connector/pair - Cancel individual orders - Cancel all button

**Cancel Orders:** - From orders view, click "🗑 Cancel" next to order - Or click "Cancel All" to close all orders

##### 2\. Position Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#2-position-management "Permanent link")

**View Positions:** - Click "📊 Positions" from trade menu - See all open perpetual positions - View unrealized PnL - Check leverage and position side

**Close Position:** - From positions view, select position - Click "Close Position" - Confirm to close at market

**Position Tracking:** - Entry price and mark price - Unrealized PnL in USD and % - Leverage used - Position side (LONG/SHORT)

##### 3\. Perpetual Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#3-perpetual-configuration "Permanent link")

**Set Leverage:** - Click "⚡ Leverage" from trade menu - Select connector - Select trading pair - Enter leverage amount (1-125x depending on exchange) - Confirm to set

**Position Mode:** - Click "🔄 Position Mode" - Select One-way or Hedge mode - Applies to entire connector

**Account Switching:** - Click connector button - Select different account - All subsequent trades use selected account

#### Interactive Menu[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#interactive-menu "Permanent link")

The trade menu shows: - Current balances for selected connector - Recent orders (last 5) - Active positions (perpetual) - Real-time quote preview - Trading rules (min/max amounts)

#### Usage Examples[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#usage-examples_2 "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-1) # Market Buy [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-2) /trade [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-3) Click "🔌 Connector" → Select "binance" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-4) Click "💱 Pair" → Type "SOL-USDT" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-5) Click "📈 Side" → Ensure "BUY" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-6) Click "💰 Amount" → Type "$100" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-7) Click "✅ Execute" → Confirm [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-8)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-9) # Limit Sell [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-10) /trade   [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-11) Click "🔌 Connector" → Select "bybit_perpetual" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-12) Click "💱 Pair" → Type "BTC-USDT" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-13) Click "📉 Side" → Select "SELL" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-14) Click "📋 Type" → Select "LIMIT" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-15) Click "💰 Amount" → Type "0.01" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-16) Click "💵 Price" → Type "95000" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-17) Click "✅ Execute" → Confirm [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-18)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-19) # Set Leverage [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-20) /trade [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-21) Click "⚡ Leverage" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-22) Select connector and pair [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-23) Type "10" → 10x leverage [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-24) Confirm [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-25)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-26) # View & Cancel Orders [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-27) /trade [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-28) Click "🔍 Orders" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-29) Click "🗑 Cancel" next to order [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-2-30) Or "Cancel All"`

* * *

### `/swap` - DEX Token Swaps[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#swap-dex-token-swaps "Permanent link")

Quick token swaps on decentralized exchanges via Gateway.

#### Supported DEX Routers[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#supported-dex-routers "Permanent link")

*   **Solana**: Jupiter
*   **Ethereum**: Uniswap
*   **Base**: Uniswap
*   And more aggregators (0x, 1inch, OpenOcean)

#### Swap Operations[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#swap-operations "Permanent link")

**Get Quote:** 1. Click "🌐 Network" → Select network (e.g., Solana) 2. Click "🔌 Router" → Select DEX (e.g., jupiter) 3. Click "💱 Pair" → Enter pair (e.g., `SOL-USDC`) 4. Click "📈 Side" → Select BUY or SELL 5. Click "💰 Amount" → Enter amount 6. Click "📊 Slippage" → Set slippage % (default: 1%) 7. Click "📋 Quote" → Get real-time price

**Execute Swap:** 1. After getting quote, click "✅ Execute" 2. Review swap details 3. Confirm to submit transaction 4. Wait for blockchain confirmation

**Quick Swap:** - Repeats last swap with same parameters - Just update amount if needed - Fast execution

**Swap History:** - Click "🔍 History" - View recent swaps - See transaction status - Filter by network/connector - Click 🔗 to view on explorer

#### Features[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#features_2 "Permanent link")

*   Real-time quote preview
*   Buy/Sell spread display
*   Balance checking
*   Recent swaps display (last 5)
*   Slippage protection
*   Transaction status tracking

#### Usage Examples[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#usage-examples_3 "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-1) # Basic Swap [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-2) /swap [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-3) Click "🌐 Network" → "Solana" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-4) Click "🔌 Router" → "jupiter" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-5) Click "💱 Pair" → "SOL-USDC" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-6) Click "📈 Side" → "BUY" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-7) Click "💰 Amount" → "10" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-8) Click "📋 Quote" → Review [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-9) Click "✅ Execute" → Confirm [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-10)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-11) # View History [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-12) /swap [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-13) Click "🔍 History" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-14) View recent swaps [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-3-15) Click 🔗 for transaction details`

* * *

### `/lp` - Liquidity Pool Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#lp-liquidity-pool-management "Permanent link")

Manage CLMM (Concentrated Liquidity Market Maker) positions.

#### Features[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#features_3 "Permanent link")

**LP Positions:** - View all liquidity positions - In-range/out-of-range status - Uncollected fees - Position value and PnL

**Pool Discovery:** - Browse pools by connector - Search by network - GeckoTerminal integration - View pool analytics

**Position Management:** - Collect fees from positions - Add liquidity to pools - Remove liquidity - Close positions

#### Operations[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#operations "Permanent link")

**View Positions:** - See all active LP positions - In-range indicator (🟢/🔴) - Uncollected fees - Current value

**Collect Fees:** - Click "💰 Collect" next to position - Or "💰 Collect All" for all positions - Fees sent to wallet

**Add Liquidity:** - From pool explorer - Select pool - Enter amounts - Set price range - Confirm transaction

**Remove Liquidity:** - Select position - Click "❌ Close" or "Remove" - Specify amount to remove - Confirm transaction

**Pool Explorer:** - Click "🔍 Pool Info" - Search by network and connector - View: - Pool TVL - 24h volume - Fee tier - Token reserves - Current price

#### Usage Examples[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#usage-examples_4 "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-1) # View Positions [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-2) /lp [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-3) See all positions with fees [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-4)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-5) # Collect Fees [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-6) /lp [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-7) Click "💰 Collect" next to position [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-8) Confirm [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-9)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-10) # Explore Pools [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-11) /lp [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-12) Click "📋 Pools" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-13) Select network [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-14) Select connector [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-4-15) Browse available pools`

* * *

### `/config` - Configuration Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#config-configuration-management "Permanent link")

Complete system configuration through Telegram menus.

#### API Servers[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#api-servers "Permanent link")

Manage connections to Hummingbot Backend API instances.

**View Servers:** - Lists all configured servers - Real-time status (🟢 Online / 🔴 Offline) - Shows default server (⭐) - Connection details

**Add Server:** 1. Click "➕ Add Server" 2. Progressive form: - **Name**: Unique identifier (e.g., `main`, `prod`, `dev`) - **Host**: Server address (e.g., `localhost`, `192.168.1.100`) - **Port**: API port (default: `8000`) - **Username**: API username (default: `admin`) - **Password**: API password (default: `admin`) 3. Review and confirm 4. Server is added and tested

**Modify Server:** 1. Click server name from list 2. Click field to modify (Host, Port, User, Pass) 3. Enter new value 4. Changes saved immediately

**Delete Server:** 1. Click server name 2. Click "🗑 Delete" 3. Confirm deletion 4. Server removed from configuration

**Set Default Server:** 1. Click server name 2. Click "⭐ Set as Default" 3. Default server used for all operations

**Server Status:** - **🟢 Online**: Server reachable and authenticated - **🔴 Offline**: Cannot connect to server - **🔴 Auth Error**: Wrong credentials

#### API Keys[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#api-keys "Permanent link")

Manage exchange API credentials for trading.

**View Credentials:** - Lists all accounts - Shows connected exchanges per account - Number of credentials

**Add API Key:** 1. Click "🔑 API Keys" 2. Select account 3. Select exchange connector 4. Progressive input form: - Fields vary by exchange - Common: `api_key`, `api_secret` - Perpetual: may need `api_passphrase` 5. Credentials tested automatically 6. Success confirmation

**Delete API Key:** 1. Select account 2. Find credential to remove 3. Click "🗑 Delete \[exchange\]" 4. Confirm deletion 5. Credential removed

**Security Features:** - Secrets displayed as `****` - Input messages deleted immediately - Encrypted storage via Hummingbot API

#### Gateway Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#gateway-configuration "Permanent link")

Deploy and manage Gateway for DEX trading.

##### Deployment[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#deployment "Permanent link")

**Deploy Gateway:** 1. Click "🌐 Gateway" 2. Click "🚀 Deploy" 3. Select Docker image: - `latest` - Stable release - `development` - Latest features - Custom image 4. Gateway container deployed

**Stop Gateway:** - Click "🛑 Stop Gateway" - Container stopped - Preserves configuration

**Restart Gateway:** - Click "🔄 Restart" - Gateway restarted with same config

**View Logs:** - Click "📝 Logs" - See recent gateway activity - Troubleshoot issues

##### Wallet Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#wallet-management "Permanent link")

**Add Wallet:** 1. Click "💼 Wallets" 2. Click "➕ Add Wallet" 3. Select blockchain (Solana, Ethereum, Base, etc.) 4. Enter private key - **Security**: Message deleted immediately - Stored encrypted in Gateway 5. Wallet address confirmed

**View Wallets:** - Listed by blockchain - Shows wallet addresses - Balances (if available)

**Remove Wallet:** 1. Select blockchain 2. Select wallet address 3. Confirm removal 4. Wallet removed from Gateway

##### Network Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#network-configuration "Permanent link")

**View Networks:** - Lists all available networks - Shows RPC endpoints - Chain ID and settings

**Configure Network:** 1. Select network 2. Progressive field editing 3. Modify: - RPC URL - Chain ID - Gas settings - Timeouts 4. Changes applied to Gateway

**Supported Networks:** - Ethereum Mainnet - Base - Solana Mainnet/Devnet - BSC - Polygon - Arbitrum - And more

##### DEX Connectors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#dex-connectors "Permanent link")

**View Connectors:** - Lists all DEX connectors - Configuration status - Trading type (AMM, CLMM, Router)

**Configure Connector:** 1. Select connector 2. View current settings 3. Edit configuration fields 4. Apply changes to Gateway

**Connector Types:** - **AMM**: Constant product pools - **CLMM**: Concentrated liquidity - **Router**: Aggregated routing

##### Pool Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#pool-management "Permanent link")

**Add Custom Pool:** 1. Click "📋 Pools" 2. Select connector 3. Select network 4. Enter pool details: - Pool type - Base token - Quote token - Pool address 5. Pool added to Gateway

**Remove Pool:** 1. Browse pools by connector/network 2. Select pool 3. Click "🗑 Delete" 4. Confirm removal

**View Pools:** - Listed by connector and network - Shows token pairs - Pool addresses

##### Token Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#token-management "Permanent link")

**Add Custom Token:** 1. Click "🪙 Tokens" 2. Select network 3. Enter token details: - Token address - Symbol - Decimals - Name (optional) 4. Token added for trading

**Remove Token:** 1. Browse tokens by network 2. Select token 3. Click "🗑 Delete" 4. Confirm removal

**View Tokens:** - Listed by network - Shows all configured tokens - Symbol, address, decimals

#### Usage Examples[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#usage-examples_5 "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-1) # Add API Server [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-2) /config [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-3) Click "🔌 API Servers" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-4) Click "➕ Add Server" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-5) Name: "production" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-6) Host: "192.168.1.100" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-7) Port: "8000" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-8) Username: "admin" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-9) Password: "mypassword" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-10) Confirm [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-11)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-12) # Add Exchange Credentials [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-13) /config [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-14) Click "🔑 API Keys" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-15) Select account [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-16) Select "binance" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-17) Enter api_key [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-18) Enter api_secret [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-19) Confirm → Tested automatically [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-20)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-21) # Deploy Gateway [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-22) /config [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-23) Click "🌐 Gateway" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-24) Click "🚀 Deploy" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-25) Select "latest" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-26) Wait for deployment → Success [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-27)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-28) # Add Wallet [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-29) /config → "🌐 Gateway" → "💼 Wallets" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-30) Click "➕ Add Wallet" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-31) Select "solana-mainnet-beta" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-32) Enter private key (deleted immediately) [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-33) Wallet address confirmed [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-34)[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-35) # Configure Network [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-36) /config → "🌐 Gateway" → "🌐 Networks" [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-37) Select network [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-38) Edit RPC URL or other settings [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/usage/#__codelineno-5-39) Changes applied`

**Community Support:** - Hummingbot Discord: https://discord.gg/hummingbot - GitHub Issues: https://github.com/hummingbot/condor/issues

# Find Log Files - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/log-files/#log-files)

Log Files[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/log-files/#log-files "Permanent link")

==================================================================================

As Hummingbot is an in-progress and open-access software, logs are stored locally in your computer each time an instance is run. While the bot is active, record of status updates, results of specified checks and behaviors, as well as error tracing is encoded in the log files.

### Viewing log configurations[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/log-files/#viewing-log-configurations "Permanent link")

The way that log files are structured is contained within `conf/hummingbot_logs.yml`. For now, we request that users leave the log settings at the defaults. This makes it easier for the Hummingbot team to trace bugs and other problems that users face when logs are submitted.

### Viewing individual log files[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/log-files/#viewing-individual-log-files "Permanent link")

For users who wish to locate and submit log files, generally they are located in the `/logs` folder. Specific path or location may vary depending on the environment and how Hummingbot was installed.

*   Installed from source: `hummingbot/logs`
*   Installed via Docker: `hummingbot_files/hummingbot_logs`
*   `hummingbot_files` is the default name of the parent directory. This can be different depending on the setup when the instance was created.
*   Installed via Binary (Windows): `%localappdata%\hummingbot.io\Hummingbot\logs`
*   Installed via Binary (MacOS): `~/Library/Application\ Support/Hummingbot/Logs`

### Log file management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/log-files/#log-file-management "Permanent link")

A separate log file will now be generated daily. When a new log file is created, if there are more than 7 files, the oldest ones will be deleted in order to limit disk storage usage. The log rotation feature was added in [Hummingbot version 0.17.0](https://docs.hummingbot.io/release-notes/0.17.0/#log-file-management-data-storage)
.

If you are looking for support in handling errors or have questions about behavior reported in logs, you can find ways of contacting the team or community in our [support section](https://discord.com/invite/hummingbot)
.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Find%20Log%20Files+-+/client/log-files/)
.

# Launch/Exit Hummingbot - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#launch-and-exit-hummingbot)

Launch and Exit Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#launch-and-exit-hummingbot "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================================

This page contains information on launching and exiting the application, assuming Hummingbot is installed already on your machine.

Launch via Docker[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#launch-via-docker "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Check the list of running Docker containers

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#__codelineno-0-1) docker ps -a`

Take note of the container name and use the following command to attach to it using the command below -

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#__codelineno-1-1) docker attach [container_name]`

Tip

If no containers are running, follow the steps below to create a Hummingbot instance.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#__codelineno-2-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#__codelineno-2-2) cd hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#__codelineno-2-3) docker compose up -d`

Launch from source[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#launch-from-source "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Make sure the hummingbot conda environment is enabled.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#__codelineno-3-1) conda activate hummingbot`

In the `hummingbot` parent directory, run this command to launch the application:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#__codelineno-4-1) ./start`

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/launch-from-source.gif)

Tip

As of version **1.19.0**, use `./start` command to launch hummingbot from source. [Read more](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/issues/6513)

Exit Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/launch-exit/#exit-hummingbot "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Running the `exit` command cancels all outstanding orders and exit the Hummingbot interface. In case of errors, the command `exit -f` will force the application to close.

If you're running Hummingbot installed via binary, exiting Hummingbot by clicking the close window icon will leave your active orders open in the exchange.

Tip

You can also press the keyboard shortcut `CTRL + C` twice to exit.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Launch/Exit%20Hummingbot+-+/client/launch-exit/)
.

# Overview - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#condor-telegram-interface-for-hummingbot)

Condor: Telegram Interface for Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#condor-telegram-interface-for-hummingbot "Permanent link")

=======================================================================================================================================

Condor is a powerful Telegram bot that provides a mobile-friendly interface to interact with your Hummingbot trading infrastructure. It allows you to monitor portfolios, manage trading bots, execute trades on both centralized (CEX) and decentralized (DEX) exchanges, and configure your entire trading setup—all from Telegram.

🌟 Key Features[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#key-features "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **📊 Portfolio Dashboard** - Real-time portfolio tracking with PnL indicators, holdings breakdown, and graphical analysis
*   **🤖 Bot Monitoring** - Monitor and manage Hummingbot trading bots with live status and metrics
*   **💱 CEX/CLOB Trading** - Place orders, manage positions, and configure leverage on centralized exchanges
*   **🔄 DEX Trading** - Execute token swaps and manage liquidity positions via Gateway
*   **⚙️ Configuration** - Complete system configuration through Telegram menus
*   **🔐 Security** - User ID whitelist and credential protection

🏗️ Architecture[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#architecture "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Condor acts as a secure bridge between your Hummingbot instances and the Telegram platform. It translates user commands from Telegram into API requests that your Hummingbot instances understand.

### System Architecture[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#system-architecture "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-0-1) Telegram User      [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-0-2)      ↓ [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-0-3) Condor Bot      [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-0-4)      ↓     [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-0-5)      ├─→ Hummingbot Backend API → Trading Bots     [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-0-6)      ├─→ Gateway → DEX Protocols (Uniswap, Jupiter, etc.)     [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-0-7)      └─→ GPT-4o + MCP (future AI features)`

### Integration Points[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#integration-points "Permanent link")

#### Hummingbot Backend API[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#hummingbot-backend-api "Permanent link")

*   Portfolio data aggregation
*   Bot status and control
*   Order and position management
*   Exchange connectivity

#### Gateway[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#gateway "Permanent link")

*   DEX token swaps
*   Liquidity pool management
*   Wallet management
*   Multi-chain support

### Project Structure[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#project-structure "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-1) condor/ [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-2) ├── main.py                     # Entry point [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-3) ├── servers.py                  # Server manager [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-4) ├── handlers/                   # Command handlers [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-5) │   ├── __init__.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-6) │   ├── portfolio.py            # Portfolio dashboard [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-7) │   ├── bots/                   # Bot monitoring [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-8) │   │   ├── __init__.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-9) │   │   ├── menu.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-10) │   │   └── controller_handlers.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-11) │   ├── cex/                    # CEX/CLOB trading [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-12) │   │   ├── __init__.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-13) │   │   ├── trade.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-14) │   │   ├── orders.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-15) │   │   └── positions.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-16) │   ├── dex/                    # DEX trading [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-17) │   │   ├── __init__.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-18) │   │   ├── swap.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-19) │   │   ├── liquidity.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-20) │   │   └── pools.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-21) │   └── config/                 # Configuration [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-22) │       ├── __init__.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-23) │       ├── servers.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-24) │       ├── api_keys.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-25) │       └── gateway/            # Gateway management [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-26) │           ├── __init__.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-27) │           ├── menu.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-28) │           ├── deployment.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-29) │           ├── wallets.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-30) │           ├── connectors.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-31) │           ├── networks.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-32) │           ├── pools.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-33) │           └── tokens.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-34) ├── utils/                      # Utilities [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-35) │   ├── auth.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-36) │   ├── telegram_formatters.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-37) │   ├── portfolio_graphs.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-38) │   └── trading_data.py [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-39) └── data/                       # Persistence     [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#__codelineno-1-40)     └── condor_bot_data.pickle`

### Core Components[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#core-components "Permanent link")

1.  **Telegram Interface**: The user-facing component running on Telegram clients, handling user input and displaying interactive menus.
2.  **Command Handlers**: The logic layer that processes specific commands (e.g., `/portfolio`, `/bots`) and determines the appropriate action.
3.  **API Integration Layer**: A secure communication module that interacts with the Hummingbot Backend API using standard REST protocols.
4.  **Data Processing Utilities**: Helper functions that format raw data from Hummingbot into clear, readable messages and visualizations (charts/graphs).

🚀 Why Use Condor?[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/#why-use-condor "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Accessibility**: Manage your bots on the go without needing SSH access or complex terminal commands.
*   **Simplicity**: Abstract away technical complexities with intuitive menus and buttons.
*   **Efficiency**: Quickly react to market changes by managing positions and bots instantly.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

.

# Installation - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#installation-deployment)

Installation & Deployment[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#installation-deployment "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================

This guide outlines the steps to install and deploy Condor.

Important

**Docker is the recommended installation method** for most users as it handles all dependencies automatically. The Source/Conda method is intended for developers or advanced users who need to modify the code.

✅ Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before you begin, ensure you have the following ready:

*   **Docker** & **Docker Compose** (for Docker method).
*   **Python 3.11+** & **Conda** (for Source method).
*   A running **Hummingbot API** instance.
*   A **Telegram Bot Token** from [@BotFather](https://t.me/botfather)
    .

🐳 Option 1: Docker (Recommended)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#option-1-docker-recommended "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 1\. Clone the Repository[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#1-clone-the-repository "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-0-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/condor.git [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-0-2) cd condor`

### 2\. Configure Environment[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#2-configure-environment "Permanent link")

Run the setup script to generate your `.env` file.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-1-1) ./setup-environment.sh`

**Required Inputs:**

*   `TELEGRAM_TOKEN`: Your bot token.
    
*   `AUTHORIZED_USERS`: Comma-separated list of allowed Telegram User IDs (e.g., `123456789,987654321`).
    

### 3\. Launch Condor[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#3-launch-condor "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-2-1) docker compose up -d`

🐍 Option 2: Source (Developers)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#option-2-source-developers "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use this method if you want to run Condor directly from the source code.

### Clone the Repository[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#clone-the-repository "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-3-1) # Clone the repository [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-3-2) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/condor.git [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-3-3) cd condor`

### Create Conda Environment[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#create-conda-environment "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-4-1) conda env create -f environment.yml [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-4-2) conda activate condor`

### Configure Environment[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#configure-environment "Permanent link")

Run the setup script to generate your `.env` file.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-5-1) ./setup-environment.sh`

### Run the Bot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#run-the-bot "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-6-1) python main.py`

🛠️ Post-Installation Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#post-installation-configuration "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Getting Your Telegram User ID:** 1. Start the bot with `/start` 2. If not authorized, the bot will display your User ID 3. Add your User ID to `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_IDS`

#### 2\. API Servers Configuration (`servers.yml`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#2-api-servers-configuration-serversyml "Permanent link")

Create `servers.yml` to define your Hummingbot instances:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-1) default_server: main [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-2) servers:   [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-3)   main:    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-4)     host: localhost    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-5)     port: 8000    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-6)     username: admin    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-7)     password: admin    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-8)     enabled: true [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-9)  [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-10)   remote:    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-11)     host: 192.168.1.100    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-12)     port: 8000    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-13)     username: admin    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-14)     password: admin    [](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#__codelineno-7-15)     enabled: true`

**Note**: You can also configure servers directly through the bot's `/config` menu.

### First-Time Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/#first-time-setup "Permanent link")

1.  **Start the bot**: Send `/start` to your bot in Telegram
2.  **Verify access**: Ensure your User ID is in `TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_IDS`
3.  **Configure servers**: Use `/config` → `🔌 API Servers` to add your Hummingbot instances
4.  **Add credentials**: Use `/config` → `🔑 API Keys` to connect exchange accounts
5.  **Deploy Gateway** (optional): Use `/config` → `🌐 Gateway` to set up DEX trading

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

.

# Check Bot/Market Status - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#check-bot-and-market-status)

Check Bot and Market Status[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#check-bot-and-market-status "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================

Check bot status[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#check-bot-status "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Run `status` command or CTRL+S to show the bot's current status. The output may differ depending on the running strategy, but generally, it shows the following information:

*   Market(s) you're trading in
*   Balance or inventory of trading pair assets
*   Active or outstanding orders
*   Error messages
*   Exchange connection status
*   Paper trading mode (when enabled)

Get live bot status[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#get-live-bot-status "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `status --live` command displays the real-time status of the bot.

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/status-live.gif)

Note

Currently, this feature works on all strategies except `liquidity mining` strategy.

View market order book[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#view-market-order-book "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, the `order_book` command displays the top 5 bid/ask prices and volume of the current market, similar to how they're displayed in the exchange's order book.

**Optional arguments**

| Command Argument | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--live` | Continuously displays the order book in real-time |
| `--lines` | Number of lines to display |
| `--exchange` | Specifies the exchange when running a secondary exchange |
| `--market` | Specifies the trading pair when running a secondary exchange |

**Sample usage**

Run `order_book --live --lines 20` to show the top 20 bid/ask and volume in real-time.

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/orderbook-live-lines.gif)

View market ticker prices[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#view-market-ticker-prices "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `ticker` command displays the market prices, specifically the best bid, best ask, mid price, and last trade price.

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/ticker-command.png)

**Optional arguments**

| Command Argument | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `--live` | Displays the ticker in real-time |
| `--exchange` | Specifies the exchange when running a secondary exchange |
| `--market` | Specifies the trading pair when running a secondary exchange |

status[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#status "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get the market status of the current bot.

``[](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#__codelineno-0-1) >>>  status [](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#__codelineno-0-2)  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#__codelineno-0-3) (BTC) 0.1116 0.1274     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#__codelineno-0-12)      Current %            46.7%  53.3% [](https://hummingbot.org/client/status/#__codelineno-0-13) # Create/Delete Password - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/password/#create-and-delete-password)

Create and Delete Password[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/password/#create-and-delete-password "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================

The password in Hummingbot encrypts sensitive data such as API keys, secret keys, and wallet private keys. For security reasons, the password is only stored locally in encrypted form, and we do not have access to it.

Creating a password[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/password/#creating-a-password "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are using Hummingbot for the first time, the system will prompt you to create a password. There are no character requirements, although we recommend using a strong password for additional security.

You can click the **OK** button on the welcome screen or you can press **TAB** to navigate the selection and **ENTER** to confirm.

**Passwords are stored locally in your computer. No passwords are uploaded to any server.**

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/welcome.gif)

Deleting a password[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/password/#deleting-a-password "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The password is stored as an encrypted `.password_verification` file in the `/conf` directory within the `hummingbot` folder.

Delete the `.password_verification` file under the `hummingbot_conf` folder to reset the password. Note that the .password\_verification file is hidden so you won't be able to see it by default unless you set your system to show all hidden files. In the terminal use the `ls -a` command to list all files

Note that if you do remove the `.password_verification` file you'll also need to remove the existing `connector.yml` files under the `conf/connector` folder otherwise you'll run into an issue where the bot throws an error message and doesn't start.

This is because Hummingbot encrypts the connector files with the same password you use to login. Resetting the password by deleting the password verification file will prevent the existing connector files from being decrypted which means you'll also need to reconnect your API keys.

Use the command `sudo rm -rf .password_verification` to delete the file

![password](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/password.png)

Tip

In older versions the passwords and private keys are saved as encrypted files in `hummingbot_conf` (via Docker and binary) or `/conf` directory (installed from source). To reset your password, delete all files starting with `encrypted_` prefix. ![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/encrypted_files.png)

Warning

This will disconnect your API keys from Hummingbot. You will have to re-connect your API keys.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Create/Delete%20Password+-+/client/password/)
.

# Check Performance - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#performance-history)

Performance History[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#performance-history "Permanent link")

====================================================================================================

History command[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#history-command "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The `history` command displays the current duration of total past trades, asset inventory and value, and market trading pair performance. Run `history --verbose` to see all recent trades.

Trades are saved locally in a .csv file located in the `data` folder which you can view by running `history --verbose --days` command even after you restart Hummingbot.

Optional argument `--precision` specifies the number of decimal values.

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/history.png)

This block below shows the calculation for some of the values displayed in the history output.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-0-1) Avg price = total trade volume of quote / total trade volume of base asset [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-0-2) Hold portfolio value = (base start asset * current market price) + quote start asset [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-0-3) Current portfolio value = (base current asset * current market price) + quote current asset [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-0-4) Trade P&L = current portfolio value - hold portfolio value [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-0-5) Total P&L = trade P&L + fees paid [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-0-6) Return % = total P&L / hold portfolio value`

Tip

The Return % in the navbar at the bottom of Hummingbot client may be different from the history command output. This is because the Return % in history takes the price changes into calculation while the navbar in the bottom UI does not.

### How It Works[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#how-it-works "Permanent link")

Run the `history` command in Hummingbot to display the current duration of total past trades, asset inventory and value, market trading pair performance.

#### Sample Output[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#sample-output "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-1) >>>  history [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-2)  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-3)   Start Time: 2020-11-11 00:56:37  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-4)   Current Time: 2020-11-11 12:57:22  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-5)   Duration: 0 days 12:00:45 [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-6)  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-7)   binance / MFT-BNB [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-8)    [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-9)     Trades:                                         [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-10)                                          buy        sell       total      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-11)       Number of trades                   113          97         209      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-12)       Total trade volume (BTC)       2181335    -2133912       47423      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-13)       Total trade volume (USDT)      -217,67      210.76       -6.91      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-14)       Avg price                    0.0000998   0.0000988   0.0001457 [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-15)    [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-16)     Assets: [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-17)                                      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-18)                                       Start      Current      Change      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-19)       MFT                            155248       202671       47423      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-20)       BNB                            23.331       16.419      -6.912      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-21)       MFT/BNB price               0.0001076    0.0000809  -0.0000267      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-22)       Base asset %                    41.7%        50.0%        8.2% [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-23)    [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-24)     Performance:      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-25)       Hold portfolio value           35.890 BNB      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-26)       Current portfolio value        32.815 BNB      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-27)       Trade P&L                      -3.075 BNB      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-28)       Fees paid                      -0.428 BNB      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-29)       Total P&L                      -3.504 BNB      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#__codelineno-1-30)       Return %:                      -9.76%`

The following displays the formula for key calculations:

Note

For more details on the calculations, please see this [Google Sheet](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1zp8EmeJdiP5soR_Bu7hRjaSy65bc7lsg98J5jKanRn8/edit#gid=875477664)
.

#### Average Price[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#average-price "Permanent link")

Avg Price = Total trade volume of quote/Total trade volume of base asset.

In the [sample output](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#sample-output)
, the total avg price is 6.91/47423 = 0.0001457

This value means the average price of total MFT/BNB trades is 0.0001457

#### Hold Portfolio Value[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#hold-portfolio-value "Permanent link")

The asset value from the start to the end with no trades.

Hold portfolio value = (base start asset\*current market price)+ quote start asset

From the above example, for the Hold portfolio value is (155248\*0.0000809)+23.33=35.89

#### Current Portfolio Value[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#current-portfolio-value "Permanent link")

Current portfolio value = (base current asset\*current market price)+ quote current asset

From the above example, for the Current portfolio value is (202671\*0.0000809)+16.419=32.815

#### Trade P&L[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#trade-pl "Permanent link")

Trade P&L = Current portfolio value - Hold Portfolio value

From the above example, for the Trade P&L value is 32.815-35.89=-3.075

#### Total P&L[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#total-pl "Permanent link")

Total P&L = Trade P&L + Fees paid

From the above example, for the Total P&L is -3.075 + -0.428 = -3.504

#### Return Percentage[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/#return-percentage "Permanent link")

Return% = Total P&L/Hold portfolio value

From the above example, for the Return% is -3.075/-35.89 = -9.76%

The **Return %** (bottom navbar) matches the calculated return on **History** after the last trade, see following screenshot:

![ ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/same_return.png)

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Check%20Performance+-+/client/history/)
.

# Clock Tick Size - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/clock-tick/#clock-tick-size)

Clock tick size[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/clock-tick/#clock-tick-size "Permanent link")

==============================================================================================================

Starting with version [**1.8.0**](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/)
, the `tick_size` is now added as a variable in the ClientConfigMap, this means that you will be able to change the value of the tick size in the `conf_client.yml` file or by running `config tick_size` from within Hummingbot.

How it works[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/clock-tick/#how-it-works "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All the major components of Hummingbot, like the connectors and the strategies inherit from the [TimeIterator](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/e30406a2d41f1f9c741c29f449f477ab9ad7e4e5/hummingbot/core/time_iterator.pyx)
 class. The Clock notifies all the components involved in the strategy by calling the method `c_tick()` of the time iterators every `tick_size`.

By default, the `tick_size` (or how long it takes Hummingbot to loop through a strategy iteration) is currently set to 1 second.

How to configure Tick Size[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/clock-tick/#how-to-configure-tick-size "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

There are two ways to configure the tick size

*   From within Hummingbot, run the command `config tick_size` and enter the desired tick size in decimal form.

![](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/tick_size_a.png)

*   Stop and exit Hummingbot then edit the `conf_client.yml` file located inside the `hummingbot_conf` folder using a text editor. Look for `tick_size: 1.0` and change `1.0` to whatever tick size you want. Save the changes to the yml file then run Hummingbot again for the changes to take effect.

![](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/tick_size_b.png)

Note

Due to connector limitations, the tick size cannot be set lower than `0.1` seconds

To check what the current `tick_size` is, you can run the `config` command and check the tick\_size value under the **Global Configurations** section

![](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/tick_size_c.png)

More Resources[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/clock-tick/#more-resources "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Here's a short video where **Foundation** developer Federico shows how the tick\_size works: [https://www.loom.com/share/138d49d3ceb34da9943f114d848dbe77](https://www.loom.com/share/138d49d3ceb34da9943f114d848dbe77)

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Clock%20Tick%20Size+-+/client/global-configs/clock-tick/)
.

# Balance Limit - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#how-it-works)

Balance Limit
=============

**Updated on version [0.35.0](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/releases/)
**

Sets the amount limit on how much assets Hummingbot can use in an exchange or wallet. This can be useful when running multiple bots on different trading pairs with same tokens e.g. running a BTC-USDT pair and another bot on ETH-USDT using the same account.

How It Works[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#how-it-works "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can set how much of a particular token the bot can use by running the command `balance limit [exchange] [asset] [amount]`. You can disable this feature by editing it in the global config file and set it to -1. While setting it to 0 will initially not place any order for a specific asset until a trade is executed to accumulate the said asset.

For example:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-0-1) >>>  balance limit binance USDT 100 [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-0-2) Limit for USDT on binance exchange set to 100.0`

Run the `balance` command again or `balance limit` to confirm the limit has been applied.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-1) >>>  balance [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-2) Updating balances, please wait... [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-4) binance:      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-5)      Asset    Amount       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-6)        BNB    0.0000       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-7)        BTC    0.0000       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-8)        ETH    0.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-9)      TFUEL    0.0187     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-10)      THETA    0.5880      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-11)       USDC    0.0090      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-12)       USDT  158.8197       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-13)        XRP    0.8440       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-1-14)        XZC    0.0076`  

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-2-1) >>>  balance limit [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-2-2) Balance Limits per exchange... [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-2-4) binance     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-2-5)     Asset     Limit     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#__codelineno-2-6)      USDT  100.0000`

Example Scenario[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/balance-limit/#example-scenario "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create pure market making strategy, run `config` to view the whole configuration.

![config ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/config1.png)

Run `balance limit binance ETH 0.0513` to set the balance limit to 0.0513 ETH. Run `balance limit binance USDT 30` to set the balance limit to 30 USDT. Both ETH and USDT value is equivalent to $30.

![run balance ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/run_balance.png)

Each order is 0.0188 equivalent to $11.20

![order amount ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/order_amount.png)

Sell order gets filled. USDT available balance is now 30.1657

![sell filled ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/sell_filled.png)

Another sell order gets filled, the available balance now shows 41.2069. Plus the open buy order, the "usable" balance on USDT is now at around $52.

![sell filled2 ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/sell_filled2.png)

After the two sell orders gets filled the remaining available balance in ETH is 0.0137 equivalent to $8.17. It means that after the next `order_refresh_time` it won't create sell order because the minimum order amount is $11.

![buy order ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/buy_order.png)

![buy order1 ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/buy_order1.png)

Same process as the scenario above. After the two buy orders gets filled the remaining available balance in USDT is 7.5317 equivalent to $7.53. It means that after the next `order_refresh_time` it won't create buy order because the minimum order amount is $11.

![buy order buy ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/buy_order_buy.png)

![buy order buy1 ](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/buy_order_buy1.png)

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

.

# User Interface - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#user-interface-guide)

User Interface Guide[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#user-interface-guide "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================

![Hummingbot CLI](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/userinterface-hummingbot.gif)

The CLI is divided into five panes:

*   **Input pane (lower left)**: Where users enter commands

![Hummingbot CLI](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/input-pane.gif)

*   **Output pane (upper left)**: Prints the output of the user's commands

![Hummingbot CLI](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/output-pane.gif)

*   **Log pane (right)**: Log messages

![Log Pane](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/log-messages.gif)

*   **Top navigation bar**: Displays the status/information of the following items
    
    *   **Version:**
        
        *   Reference of Version Release (Currently at 1.13.0)
    *   **Strategy:**
        
        *   Hummingbot has different strategy configurations that can be used for trading or liquidity mining. Currently we are moving away from strategy based config in favor of scripts.
    *   **Strategy\_file:**
        
        *   Displays the current in use `strategy` or `script` file

![Top Navigation](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/top-nav.gif)

*   **Bottom navigation bar**: Displays the information of the following items
    
    *   Trades
        *   Number of trades done by the bot
        *   Total P&L
        *   Total profit & loss
        *   Return%
        *   Return percentage of assets
    *   CPU
        *   CPU usage of the computer
    *   Mem
        
        *   Memory usage of the computer
    *   Threads
        
    *   Duration
        *   Duration of the trading session

![Bottom Navigation](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/bottom-nav.gif)

Show and hide log pane[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#show-and-hide-log-pane "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The log pane on the right can be shown or hidden in two ways:

1.  Click the `log pane` button in the upper right hand corner
2.  Press CTRL + T shortcut on your keyboard

![Hide Log Pane](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/hide-log-pane.gif)

Tabs[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#tabs "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Users can now open another tab in the left pane of Hummingbot where the log pane is supposed to be upon entering a command associated with the Tabs feature. Users can now switch between the `log pane` and the new tab they have opened simulateneously.

Note

Currently, the feature only works with the `order_book` parameter.

Opening and Closing[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#opening-and-closing "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Opening a tab[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#opening-a-tab "Permanent link")

Use the tabs by simply inputting a command associated with the tabs feature.

Upon using the `order_book` command and any suffix it will open a tab automatically.

![opening tabs](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/tab-opening.png)

![showing tab](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/leftpane.png)

### Closing a tab[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#closing-a-tab "Permanent link")

Simply click on the `x` at the top right corner or inputting `parameter_name --close`

One option to close the tab is by clicking on the `x` next to `order_book`

![closing tabs](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/closing-of-tabs.png)

Alternatively, you can remove the new tab by inputting the `order_book --close` command to close the tab

![alternative closing tabs](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/name-of-parameter.png)

![closed tabs](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/closed-tabs.png)

Keyboard shortcuts[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#keyboard-shortcuts "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Keyboard Combo | Command | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Double CTRL + C | Exit | Press `CTRL + C` twice to exit the bot |
| CTRL + S/kbd> | Status | Show bot status |
| CTRL + F | Search /  <br>Hide Search | Toggle search in log pane |
| CTRL + X | Exit Config | Exit from the current configuration question |
| CTRL + A | Select All | \* Select all text |
| CTRL + Z | Undo | \* Undo action |
| Single CTRL + C | Copy | \* Copy text |
| CTRL + V | Paste | \* Paste text |
| CTRL + R | Reset Style | Set default color style |
| CTRL + T | Toggle logs | Hide / show the logs pane |

_\* Used for text edit in input pane only._

**Note about search:**

1.  Press CTRL + F to trigger display the search field
    
2.  Enter your search keyword (not case sensitive)
    
3.  Hit Enter to jump to the next matching keyword (incremental search)
    
4.  When you are done, press CTRL + F again to go back to reset
    

### Linux[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#linux "Permanent link")

| Keyboard Combo | Command |
| --- | --- |
| CTRL + C | Copy |
| SHIFT + RMB (right-mouse button) | Paste |

To highlight, hold SHIFT + LMB (left mouse button) and drag across the text you want to select.

### macOS[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#macos "Permanent link")

| Keyboard Combo | Command |
| --- | --- |
| ⌘ + C | Copy |
| ⌘ + V | Paste |

Note

To select text on macOS, you may need to enable the **Allow Mouse Reporting** option by pressing ⌘ + R or selecting **View > Allow Mouse Reporting** in the menu bar.

![allowmouse](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/allow_mouse_reporting.png)

Then you should be able to select text by holding `LMB` (left mouse button) and drag. You can also hold down ⌥ + shift to select specific lines like the image below.

![highlightmacos](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/highlight_macos.png)

When accessing Hummingbot on a Linux cloud server through `ssh` using a macOS terminal, hold down the Option ⌥ key or ⌥ + ⌘ to highlight text.

### Windows[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/user-interface/#windows "Permanent link")

| Keyboard Combo | Command |
| --- | --- |
| CTRL + C | Copy |
| CTRL + V | Paste |

To use this shortcut, check this box by doing a right-click on the title bar at the top of the Hummingbot window, then select **Properties**.

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/properties_windows.png)

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+User%20Interface+-+/client/user-interface/)
.
