# Start Strategies and Scripts - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#start-and-stop-strategy)

Start and Stop Strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#start-and-stop-strategy "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================

Starting a strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#starting-a-strategy "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After creating or importing a config file, use the `start` command to run the strategy.

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/start-command.gif)

Stop a running strategy[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#stop-a-running-strategy "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Run `stop` command to stop the running strategy. Doing this will also cancel all active orders.

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/stop-command.png)

Strategy Autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#strategy-autostart "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hummingbot can automatically start the execution of a previously configured trading strategy upon launch without needing user interaction. This feature works with both regular and headless modes.

### Docker Autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#docker-autostart "Permanent link")

#### Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

*   You have Hummingbot running via Docker Compose
*   You have already run the instance at least once and have set the password and API keys
*   You have at least one strategy configuration file that has been set up previously

#### How to Configure Docker Autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#how-to-configure-docker-autostart "Permanent link")

1.  **Stop any running containers**
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-0-1) docker compose down`
    
2.  **Modify docker-compose.yml**
    

Edit the `environment` section to include:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-1-1) environment:   [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-1-2)   - CONFIG_PASSWORD=password  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-1-3)   - CONFIG_FILE_NAME=simple_pmm_example.py  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-1-4)   - SCRIPT_CONFIG=conf_simple_pmm_example_config_1.yml  # Optional for scripts  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-1-5)   - HEADLESS_MODE=true  # Optional: Enable headless mode`

1.  **Deploy the Hummingbot container**
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-2-1) docker compose up -d`
    

This will start Hummingbot in detached mode (running in the background).

1.  **View active containers**
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-3-1) docker ps`
    

You should see your Hummingbot container running with the configured strategy.

1.  **Attach to the running container**
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-4-1) docker attach hummingbot`
    

When you attach, the strategy should already be running. To detach without stopping the container, use `Ctrl+P` followed by `Ctrl+Q`.

### Source Installation Autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#source-installation-autostart "Permanent link")

#### Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#prerequisites_1 "Permanent link")

*   You have Hummingbot installed via Source
*   You have already connected exchanges by adding API keys
*   You have at least one strategy configuration file that has been set up previously

#### How to Configure Source Autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#how-to-configure-source-autostart "Permanent link")

Use the following command:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-5-1) bin/hummingbot_quickstart.py -p CONFIG_PASSWORD -f SCRIPT_FILE_NAME [-c CONFIG_FILE_NAME] [--headless]`

**Example:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-6-1) # Regular mode with autostart [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-6-2) bin/hummingbot_quickstart.py -p mypassword -f simple_pmm_example.py -c conf_simple_pmm_example_config_1.yml [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-6-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-6-4) # Headless mode with autostart [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-6-5) bin/hummingbot_quickstart.py -p mypassword -f simple_pmm_example.py -c conf_simple_pmm_example_config_1.yml --headless`

Headless Mode[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#headless-mode "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Warning

Running any trading bots without manual supervision may incur additional risks. It is imperative that you thoroughly understand and test the strategy and parameters before deploying bots that can trade in an unattended manner.

Hummingbot can run in headless mode, which allows the bot to operate without the interactive CLI interface. This is particularly useful for deploying bots to cloud services or running multiple instances programmatically.

### Prerequisites for Headless Mode[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#prerequisites-for-headless-mode "Permanent link")

*   **MQTT must be enabled and running**: Since there's no CLI interface in headless mode, MQTT is required to control and monitor the bot
*   **Hummingbot password must be set**: The password is needed to decrypt API keys and wallets
*   **Strategy configuration must exist**: You need a pre-configured strategy or script file

### How to Run in Headless Mode[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#how-to-run-in-headless-mode "Permanent link")

#### Using Command Line Arguments[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#using-command-line-arguments "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-7-1) bin/hummingbot_quickstart.py --headless -p PASSWORD -f CONFIG_FILE_NAME [-c SCRIPT_CONFIG]`

Where:

*   `--headless`: Enables headless mode
    
*   `-p PASSWORD`: Your Hummingbot password
    
*   `-f CONFIG_FILE_NAME`: Strategy config file (`.yml`) or script file (`.py`)
    
*   `-c SCRIPT_CONFIG`: (Optional) Configuration file for scripts
    

#### Using Environment Variables[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#using-environment-variables "Permanent link")

You can also use environment variables, which is especially useful for Docker deployments:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-8-1) export HEADLESS_MODE=true [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-8-2) export CONFIG_PASSWORD=your_password [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-8-3) export CONFIG_FILE_NAME=your_strategy.yml [](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#__codelineno-8-4) export SCRIPT_CONFIG=your_script_config.yml  # Optional for scripts`

### Important Considerations for Headless Mode[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#important-considerations-for-headless-mode "Permanent link")

**MQTT is Required**: Without a CLI interface, MQTT is the only way to:

*   Monitor bot status and performance
    
*   View logs and error messages
    
*   Stop the bot or modify parameters
    
*   Receive alerts and notifications
    

**Use with Hummingbot API**: We strongly recommend using headless mode alongside the [Hummingbot API](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api)
 for:

*   Managing multiple bot instances
    
*   Real-time monitoring and control
    
*   Automated deployment and scaling
    
*   Integration with other systems
    

**Logging**: In headless mode, logs are still written to files, but you won't see them in real-time unless you're monitoring via MQTT or viewing log files directly.

### Autostart File Types[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#autostart-file-types "Permanent link")

You can auto-start either:

*   **Scripts**: Python files (`.py`) containing all strategy logic. Hummingbot looks for these in the `scripts` directory
    
*   **Strategies**: Configurable strategy templates with YAML config files (`.yml`). Hummingbot looks for these in the `conf/strategies` directory
    

### Best Practices for Unattended Trading[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/start-stop/#best-practices-for-unattended-trading "Permanent link")

1.  **Test Thoroughly**: Always test your strategies in paper trading mode before running them unattended
    
2.  **Set Appropriate Limits**: Configure kill switches, balance limits, and other safety parameters
    
3.  **Monitor Regularly**: Even in headless/autostart mode, regularly check logs and performance
    
4.  **Use MQTT/API**: Set up proper monitoring through MQTT or Hummingbot API for real-time alerts
    
5.  **Secure Your System**: Ensure your deployment environment is secure, especially when running with autostart
    

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

.

# Connectors - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/#what-are-connectors)

Connectors
==========

What are Connectors?[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/#what-are-connectors "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Connectors are packages of code that link Hummingbot's internal trading engine with real-time and historical data from different cryptocurrency exchanges and blockchains, via WebSocket and/or REST API. They standardize interactions with the idiosyncratic APIs offered by these platforms, for purposes such as gathering order book and blockchain data, as well as sending and cancelling transactions and orders.

CLOB Connectors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/#clob-connectors "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CLOB (Central Limit Order Book) connectors integrate into a CLOB exchange's WebSocket API, enabling standardized order placement/cancellation and order book data fetching from the perspective of Hummingbot strategies. These connectors work with both centralized exchanges (CEX) and decentralized exchanges (DEX) that utilize a central limit order book model.

See [CLOB Connectors](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/clob/)
 for a list of the current CLOB connectors in Hummingbot

Gateway DEX Connectors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/#gateway-dex-connectors "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway connectors establish and maintain connections to automated market maker (AMM) DEXs and other protocols on various blockchain networks, interfaces with their Javascript SDKs, and exposes standard REST API endpoints for trading and liquidity provision-related actions on these DEXs.

See [Gateway](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/)
 for comprehensive documentation about Gateway, including supported DEX connectors, API commands, and configuration.

Connector Maintenance[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/#connector-maintenance "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CLOB connectors requires ongoing maintenance: fixing bugs, addressing user issues, and keeping up with updates to both the exchange/blockchain API as wel as improvements to the Hummingbot connector standard.

Hummingbot Foundation maintains certain reference connectors to maintain an updated standard and leverages community-based developers to maintain other connectors to the same standard. We assign [Bounties](https://hummingbot.org/bounties/)
 to community developers to upgrade and fix bugs for each exchange's connectors in the codebase.

Connector Governance[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/#connector-governance "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hummingbot Foundation governance lets HBOT holders which exchanges are supported by the open source codebase.

New connectors may be contributed by community members via [New Connector Proposals](https://hummingbot.org/about/proposals/)
, which require a pull request with the connector code to the Hummingbot Github repo, along with a minimum HBOT balance to create.

For existing connectors, quarterly [Exchange Connector Polls](https://hummingbot.org/about/polls/)
 allocates HBOT bounties toward top exchanges and determines which exchanges should be included in the codebase going forward. See the **Connector Pots** tab in [HBOT Tracker](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1UNAumPMnXfsghAAXrfKkPGRH9QlC8k7Cu1FGQVL1t0M/edit?usp=sharing)
 for the current allocations for each exchange.

.

# Installation - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#hummingbot-client-installation)

Hummingbot Client Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#hummingbot-client-installation "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================================

This page provides detailed installation instructions for the Hummingbot Client, including both Docker and source installation methods.

For a quick Docker-based setup, see the [Hummingbot Client Quickstart](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/)
.

System Requirements[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#system-requirements "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| **Component** | **Specifications** |
| --- | --- |
| **Operating System** | Linux x64 or ARM (Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 10+), macOS, Windows (WSL2) |
| **Memory** | 4 GB RAM per instance |
| **Storage** | 5 GB HDD space per instance |
| **CPU** | at least 1 vCPU per instance / controller |

Choose Your Installation Method[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#choose-your-installation-method "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Method | Best For | Requirements |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[Docker](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#docker-installation)<br>** | Quick setup, isolated environment | Docker installed |
| **[Source](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#source-installation)<br>** | Developers, code modifications | Python, Anaconda |

* * *

Docker Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#docker-installation "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

macOSLinuxWindows

Install Docker Desktop from the [official Docker website](https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/mac-install/)

**Desktop Users:** Install Docker Desktop from [official site](https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/linux-install/)

**Headless Servers** (VPS like AWS EC2 or Digital Ocean):

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-0-1) curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com -o get-docker.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-0-2) sh get-docker.sh`

Prerequisites

*   Docker Desktop installed
*   WSL2 enabled
*   Ubuntu distribution installed

**Always run commands in:** Ubuntu Terminal (Start Menu → Ubuntu)

### Install and Run[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#install-and-run "Permanent link")

Clone the repository and start the container:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-1-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot.git [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-1-2) cd hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-1-3) docker compose up -d`

Attach to the running container:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-2-1) docker attach hummingbot`

You should see the Hummingbot welcome screen:

![welcome screen](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/welcome.png)

### Docker Commands[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#docker-commands "Permanent link")

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `docker compose up -d` | Start Hummingbot in background |
| `docker attach hummingbot` | Attach to running instance |
| `docker compose down` | Stop Hummingbot |
| `docker compose logs -f` | View logs |
| `docker pull hummingbot/hummingbot:latest` | Update to latest version |

### Detach Without Stopping[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#detach-without-stopping "Permanent link")

Press Ctrl + P then Ctrl + Q to detach from Docker without stopping the bot.

### Development or Older Versions[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#development-or-older-versions "Permanent link")

Edit `docker-compose.yml` and change the `image` field:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-3-1) # Development branch [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-3-2) image: hummingbot/hummingbot:development [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-3-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-3-4) # Specific version (e.g., 2.0.0) [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-3-5) image: hummingbot/hummingbot:version-2.0.0`

Then restart:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-4-1) docker compose down [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-4-2) docker compose up -d`

* * *

Source Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#source-installation "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#prerequisites_1 "Permanent link")

macOSLinuxWindows

**Install Xcode Command Line Tools:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-5-1) xcode-select --install`

**Install Anaconda:**

We recommend the full Anaconda distribution (not Miniconda) to avoid dependency conflicts.

_Graphical Installer:_ Visit [anaconda.com/download](https://www.anaconda.com/download)
 and download for your Mac type.

_Command Line (Intel):_

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-6-1) curl -O https://repo.anaconda.com/archive/Anaconda3-2024.10-1-MacOSX-x86_64.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-6-2) bash Anaconda3-2024.10-1-MacOSX-x86_64.sh`

_Command Line (Apple Silicon M1/M2/M3):_

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-7-1) curl -O https://repo.anaconda.com/archive/Anaconda3-2024.10-1-MacOSX-arm64.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-7-2) bash Anaconda3-2024.10-1-MacOSX-arm64.sh`

**Install System Packages:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-8-1) sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y && sudo apt install -y gcc build-essential`

**Install Anaconda:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-9-1) curl -O https://repo.anaconda.com/archive/Anaconda3-2025.06-0-Linux-x86_64.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-9-2) bash ~/Anaconda3-2025.06-0-Linux-x86_64.sh`

**Install WSL2 and Ubuntu:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-10-1) wsl --install -d Ubuntu`

Once installed, open Ubuntu terminal and follow the Linux instructions above.

Note

Run all commands in Ubuntu terminal, not Windows Command Prompt or PowerShell.

### Install and Run[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#install-and-run_1 "Permanent link")

Clone the repository:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-11-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot.git [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-11-2) cd hummingbot`

Install the environment and dependencies:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-12-1) ./install`

Activate the environment:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-13-1) conda activate hummingbot`

Compile the code:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-14-1) ./compile`

Launch Hummingbot:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-15-1) ./start`

![launch from source](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/launch-from-source.gif)

### Development or Older Versions[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#development-or-older-versions_1 "Permanent link")

**Development Branch:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-16-1) git checkout development [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-16-2) git pull origin development`

**Specific Version:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-17-1) git tag                    # List available versions [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-17-2) git checkout v2.1.0        # Switch to specific version`

* * *

Folder Structure[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#folder-structure "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Your Hummingbot installation contains:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-18-1) hummingbot/ [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-18-2) ├── conf/ [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-18-3) │   ├── connectors/    # Exchange API keys (encrypted) [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-18-4) │   ├── strategies/    # Strategy config files [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-18-5) │   └── scripts/       # Script config files [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-18-6) ├── logs/              # Log files [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-18-7) ├── data/              # Trade databases and CSV files [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-18-8) └── scripts/           # Custom scripts`

| Folder | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `/conf/connectors` | Exchange API keys encrypted by your [password](https://hummingbot.org/client/password/) |
| `/conf/strategies` | Strategy configs created with `create` command |
| `/conf/scripts` | Script configs created with `create --script-config` |
| `/logs` | Log files from your trading sessions |
| `/data` | SQLite databases and CSV trade history |
| `/scripts` | Add custom scripts here for use with `start` command |

* * *

Running in Background[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#running-in-background "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Docker[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#docker "Permanent link")

Press Ctrl + P then Ctrl + Q to detach without stopping.

Re-attach with:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-19-1) docker attach hummingbot`

### Source (using screen)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#source-using-screen "Permanent link")

Start a screen session:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-20-1) screen -S hummingbot`

Run Hummingbot:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-21-1) conda activate hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-21-2) ./start`

Detach: Ctrl + A then Ctrl + D

Re-attach:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-22-1) screen -r hummingbot`

List sessions:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#__codelineno-23-1) screen -ls`

* * *

Exiting Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#exiting-hummingbot "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Run the `exit` command to cancel all orders and close Hummingbot.

Use `exit -f` to force close in case of errors.

Tip

You can also press Ctrl + C twice to exit.

* * *

Gateway for DEX Trading[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#gateway-for-dex-trading "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway Required for DEX Trading

To trade on decentralized exchanges (Uniswap, PancakeSwap, Raydium, etc.), you need to install Gateway separately.

See [Gateway Installation](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/)
 for setup instructions.

* * *

Next Steps[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/#next-steps "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Basic Features](https://hummingbot.org/client/)
     - Learn the Hummingbot CLI
*   [V2 Strategy Walkthrough](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/v2-strategies/walkthrough/)
     - Create your first strategy
*   [Hummingbot FAQ](https://hummingbot.org/faq/)
     - Common questions
*   [Updating to New Versions](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/)
     - Update your installation

.

# Override Fees - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/override-fees/#override-fees)

Override Fees[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/override-fees/#override-fees "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================

By default, Hummingbot uses the default fees of the exchange. However, if you're on a VIP level getting discounts on fees, you can override this by editing the `conf_fee_overrides.yml` inside the `conf` or `hummingbot_conf` directory, depending on your installation method.

*   Installed from source: `hummingbot/conf`
*   Installed via Docker: `hummingbot_files/hummingbot_conf`
*   `hummingbot_files` is the default name of the parent directory. This can be different depending on the setup when the instance was created.

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/fees-override.png)

Note

Exit and restart Hummingbot for the changes to take effect.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Override%20Fees+-+/client/global-configs/override-fees/)
.

# Market Data Collector - Hummingbot

 

Market Data Collector
=====================

![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/market-data-table.png)

Hummingbot can collect real-time market data in the background for the trading pairs that are initialized in the running strategy.

Users can configure this feature by setting the new config variables in the `conf_client.yml` file:

*   `market_data_collection_enabled`: Enable/disable the Market Data Collection feature.
*   `market_data_collection_interval`: Set the market data collection interval in seconds (Default=60)
*   `market_data_collection_depth`: Set the order book collection depth (Default=20)

The data is stored in a SQLite DB table `MarketData` and includes:

*   Exchange
*   Trading Pair
*   Timestamp
*   Mid Price
*   Best Ask
*   Best Bid
*   Order Book

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Market%20Data%20Collector+-+/client/global-configs/data-collector/)
.

# Color Settings - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#color-settings)

Color Settings[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#color-settings "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================

Starting with version 0.45, we added new global configuration parameters that allows users to customize the client's background colors.

![New Hummingbot UI](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/new-ui-1.png)

1.  `top-pane`
2.  `output-pane`
3.  `input-pane`
4.  `bottom-pane`
5.  `log-pane`
6.  `terminal-primary`

Changing the panel colors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#changing-the-panel-colors "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To make changes to the panel colors, run `config [parameter_name]` inside the Hummingbot client. For example, the command for changing the log pane color is `config log-pane` and enter the hex code of the desired color.

Tip

You can use a hexadecimal color picker like the one here to choose colors: [https://www.w3schools.com/colors/colors\_picker.asp](https://www.w3schools.com/colors/colors_picker.asp)

Alternatively, you can edit these values in the `conf_client.yml` file located under the `hummingbot_conf` folder using a text editor.

Note

In past versions of Hummingbot (1.5.0 and below), the `conf_client.yml` file is named `conf_global.yml`

Reset colors to default[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#reset-colors-to-default "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Press `CTRL + R` while inside Hummingbot to reset the style to use its default colors.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-1) # Background color of the top pane [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-2) top-pane: '#000000' [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-4) # Background color of the bottom pane [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-5) bottom-pane: '#000000' [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-6)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-7) # Background color of the output pane [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-8) output-pane: '#282C2F' [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-9)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-10) # Background color of the input pane [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-11) input-pane: '#151819' [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-12)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-13) # Background color of the logs pane [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-14) logs-pane: '#151819' [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-15)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-16) # Terminal primary color (text) [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/color-settings/#__codelineno-0-17) terminal-primary: '#00FFE5'`

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Color%20Settings+-+/client/global-configs/color-settings/)
.

# Kill Switch - Hummingbot

 

Kill Switch
===========

Automatically stops the bot when it reaches a certain performance threshold, which can be either positive or negative. This feature uses the same performance calculation methodology as the [history](https://hummingbot.org/client/history/)
 command.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-0-1) Select your kill-switch mode (kill_switch_enabled/kill_switch_disabled)  >>> [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-0-2) At what profit/loss rate would you like the bot to stop? (e.g. -5 equals 5 percent loss) >>>`

You can always reconfigure this feature in two ways:

1.  Inside Hummingbot run command `config kill_switch_mode` and/or `config kill_switch_mode.kill_switch_rate`.
2.  Edit the `conf_client.yml` file located inside the `hummingbot_conf` folder using a text editor.

Note

In past versions of Hummingbot (1.5.0 and below), the `conf_client.yml` file is named `conf_global.yml`

Note that when the market prices changes, so does the bot's performance and may trigger the kill switch. For example, we executed 13 trades and our performance are shown below.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-1-1) kill_switch_mode:   [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-1-2)   kill_switch_rate: -5.0`

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-1) Inventory:       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-2)       Market Asset  Starting   Current  Net Delta Trade Delta  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-3)   0  binance   ETH   10.0000   11.0000     1.0000      3.0000  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-4)   1  binance  USDT  500.0000  297.1580  -202.8420   -610.6340 [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-5)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-6) Markets:       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-7)       Market     Pair Start Price       End Price  Trades Trade Value Delta  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-8)   0  binance  ETHUSDT     203.913  202.7150000000      13  -2.48900000 USDT [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-9)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-10) Performance:   [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-11)   Started: 2020-05-26 10:28:03  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-12)   Duration: 0 days 00:07:06  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-13)   Total Trade Value Delta: -2.489 USDT  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-2-14)   Return %: -0.0985 %`

After a while, the end price changed from 202.715 to 200.54 and so did our bot's performance even without making more trades. Since `kill_switch_rate` is set to `-0.3` this will stop the strategy.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-1) Inventory:       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-2)       Market Asset  Starting   Current  Net Delta Trade Delta  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-3)   0  binance   ETH   10.0000   11.0000     1.0000      3.0000  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-4)   1  binance  USDT  500.0000  297.1580  -202.8420   -610.6340 [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-5)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-6) Markets:       [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-7)       Market     Pair Start Price       End Price  Trades Trade Value Delta  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-8)   0  binance  ETHUSDT     203.913  200.5400000000      13  -9.01400000 USDT [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-9)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-10) Performance:   [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-11)   Started: 2020-05-26 10:28:03  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-12)   Duration: 0 days 02:09:13  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-13)   Total Trade Value Delta: -9.014 USDT  [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-3-14)   Return %: -0.3598 %`

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-4-1) [Kill switch triggered] [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-4-2) Current profitability is -0.003550034854458‬. Stopping the bot... [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/kill-switch/#__codelineno-4-3) kill_switch - Kill switch threshold reached. Stopping the bot...`

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

.

# Connect External Database - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#configuration-parameters)

Connect External Database
=========================

Community contibution

Contributor: [fengkiej](https://github.com/fengkiej)
 from [Rupiah Token](https://rupiahtoken.com/)

Hummingbot stores trades in a local SQLite for database by default, but it may be limiting for some cases such as sharing data to external system, in some cases user may want to use their own preferred client/server RDBMS for it.

Other RDBMS are supported on Hummingbot through SQLAlchemy, it has [included some widely used RDBMS dialects](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/13/dialects/index.html)
, i.e.:

*   PostgreSQL
*   MySQL
*   Oracle
*   Microsoft SQL Server

These dialects requires separate DBAPI driver to be installed on Hummingbot's conda environment, see [SQLAlchemy documentation](https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/13/dialects/index.html)
 for more information on appropriate DBAPI driver for each RDBMS. For example, to use PostgreSQL, `psycopg2` need to be installed. Run the following command to install it using conda:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-0-1) conda install psycopg2`

To configure RDBMS connection, we need to edit `conf_client.yml` in the `/hummingbot_conf` directory.

Note

In past versions of Hummingbot (1.5.0 and below), the `conf_client.yml` file is named `conf_global.yml`

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-1) - Advanced database options, currently supports SQLAlchemy's included dialects [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-2) - Reference: https://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/13/dialects/ [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-4) db_engine: sqlite [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-5) db_host: 127.0.0.1 [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-6) db_port: '3306' [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-7) db_username: username [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-8) db_password: password [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#__codelineno-1-9) db_name: dbname`

Configuration parameters[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#configuration-parameters "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Configuration Parameter | Possible Values |
| --- | --- |
| db\_engine | `sqlite`,`postgresql`,`mysql`,`oracle`,`mssql` |
| db\_host | any string e.g. `127.0.0.1` |
| db\_port | any string e.g. `3306` |
| db\_username | any string e.g. `username` |
| db\_password | any string e.g. `password` |
| db\_name | any string e.g. `dbname` |

SQLAlchemy dialects[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#sqlalchemy-dialects "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

It is also possible to connect with available SQLAlchemy's external dialects (e.g. Amazon Redshift). But the feature is not currently supported in Hummingbot due to its various DSN format, **use this at your own risk**.

📘 Additional Resources[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/external-db/#additional-resources "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In this [Youtube Video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LU-4Ui-KCtY)
 the Foundation's lead developer **Fede**, shows you how you can use `Docker Compose` to launch multiple instances that all save to a single Postgres database.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Connect%20External%20Database+-+/client/global-configs/external-db/)
.

Back to top

# Debugging & Testing Connectors - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#option-1-unit-test-cases)

Debugging & Testing Connectors
==============================

Note

The information below are for developers building `spot` and `perp` connectors that integrate directly into the Hummingbot client. For information on developing `gateway` connectors that use [Gateway](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/)
, see [Building Gateway Connectors](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/connectors/)
.

This section will break down some ways to debug and test the code. You are not required to use these options during your development process, but they will greatly help you in it.

As part of the QA process, you are **required to include unit test cases** for the code review process to begin. Refer to [Option 1: Unit Test Cases](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#option-1-unit-test-cases)
 to build your unit tests.

Option 1. Unit Test Cases[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#option-1-unit-test-cases "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You are required to provide at least 80% of unit-test code coverage to have your contribution accepted in the `hummingbot` repository. Examples of unit-tests can be found in the [test/integration](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/tree/master/test/)
 folder.

Unit-tests submitted for merging in the code base must not access any external servers directly. All server API communications must be mocked — refer to existing examples provided by the exchange you are basing your implementation on for guidance.

Warning

When writing unit-tests for submission with your PR, take extra care not to include any API authentication credentials.

Option 2. aiopython console[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#option-2-aiopython-console "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This option is mainly used to test for specific functions. Considering that many of the functions are asynchronous functions, it would be easier to test for these in the aiopython console. Click [here](https://aioconsole.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
 for some documentation on how to use aiopython.

Writing short code snippets to examine API responses and/or how certain functions in the code base work would help you understand the expected side-effects of these functions and the overall logic of the Hummingbot client.

### Issue a API Request[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#issue-a-api-request "Permanent link")

Below is just a short example on how to write a short asynchronous function to mimic a API request to place an order and displaying the response received.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-1) # Prints the response of a sample LIMIT-BUY Order [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-2) # Replace the URL and params accordingly. [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-4) >>> import aiohttp [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-5) >>> URL="api.test.com/buyOrder" [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-6) >>> params = { [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-7) ...     "symbol": "ZRXETH", [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-8) ...     "amount": "1000", [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-9) ...     "price": "0.001", [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-10) ...     "order_type": "LIMIT" [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-11) ... } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-12) >>> async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as client: [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-13) ...    async with client.request("POST", [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-14) ...                              url=URL, [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-15) ...                              params=params) as response: [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-16) ...        if response == 200: [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-0-17) ...            print(await response.json())`

### Calling a Class Method[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#calling-a-class-method "Permanent link")

Printing the output from `get_active_exchange_markets()` function in `OrderBookTrackerDataSource`.

In this example, we will be using BittrexAPIOrderBookDataSource:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-1) >>> from hummingbot.market.bittrex.BittrexAPIOrderBookDataSource import BittrexAPIOrderBookDataSource as b [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-2) >>> await b.get_active_exchange_markets()  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-3)                 [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-4)                  askRate baseAsset        baseVolume  ...             volume     USDVolume old_symbol [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-5) symbol                                                ... [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-6) BTC-USD    9357.49900000       BTC  2347519.11072768  ...       251.26097386  2.351174e+06    USD-BTC [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-7) XRP-BTC       0.00003330       XRP       83.81218622  ...   2563786.10102864  7.976883e+05    BTC-XRP [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-8) BTC-USDT   9346.88236735       BTC   538306.04864142  ...        57.59973765  5.379616e+05   USDT-BTC [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-9) . [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-10) . [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-11) . [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-1-12) [339 rows x 18 columns]`

Option 3. Custom Scripts[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#option-3-custom-scripts "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This option, like in Option 2, is mainly used to test specific functions. This is mainly useful when debugging how various functions/classes interact with one another.

e.g. Initializing a simple websocket connection to listen and output all captured messages to examine the user stream message when placing/cancelling an order. This is helpful when determining the exact response fields to use.

e.g. A simple function to craft the Authentication signature of a request. This together with [POSTMAN](https://www.getpostman.com/)
 can be used to check if you are generating the appropriate authentication signature for the respective requests.

### API Request: POST Order[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#api-request-post-order "Permanent link")

Below is a sample code for POST-ing a LIMIT-BUY order on Bittrex. This script not only tests the `BittrexAuth` class but also outputs the response from the API server.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-1) #!/usr/bin/env python3 [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-3) import asyncio [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-4) import aiohttp [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-5) from typing import Dict [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-6) from hummingbot.connector.exchange.bittrex.bittrex_auth import BittrexAuth [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-7)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-8) BITTREX_API_ENDPOINT = "https://api.bittrex.com/v3" [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-9)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-10) async def _api_request(http_method: str,                        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-11)                        path_url: str = None,                       [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-12)                        params: Dict[str, any] = None,                       [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-13)                        body: Dict[str, any] = None,                       [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-14)                        ):    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-15)     url = f"{BITTREX_API_ENDPOINT}{path_url}" [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-16)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-17)     auth = BittrexAuth(        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-18)         "****",        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-19)         "****"    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-20)     ) [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-21)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-22)     auth_dict = auth.generate_auth_dict(http_method, url, params, body, '') [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-23)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-24)     headers = auth_dict["headers"] [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-25)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-26)     if body:        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-27)         body = auth_dict["body"] [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-28)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-29)     client = aiohttp.ClientSession() [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-30)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-31)     async with client.request(http_method,                              [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-32)                               url=url,                              [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-33)                               headers=headers,                              [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-34)                               params=params,                              [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-35)                               data=body) as response:        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-36)         data: Dict[str, any] = await response.json()        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-37)         if response.status not in [200,201]:            [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-38)             print(f"Error occurred. HTTP Status {response.status}: {data}")        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-39)         print(data) [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-40)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-41) # POST order [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-42) path_url = "/orders" [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-43)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-44) body = {     [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-45)     "marketSymbol": "FXC-BTC",    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-46)     "direction": "BUY",    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-47)     "type": "LIMIT",    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-48)     "quantity": "1800",    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-49)     "limit": "3.17E-7",  # Note: This will throw an error    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-50)     "timeInForce": "GOOD_TIL_CANCELLED" [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-51) } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-52)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-53) loop = asyncio.get_event_loop() [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-54) loop.run_until_complete(_api_request("POST",path_url=path_url,body=body)) [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-2-55) loop.close()`

Option 4: Using Debugger Tools[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#option-4-using-debugger-tools "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This section will detail the necessary configurations/setup required to run the debugger tool from your IDE of choice.

### VS Code[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#vs-code "Permanent link")

Include the following debug configurations into the `launch.json` configuration file

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-2)   "version": "0.2.0",  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-3)   "configurations": [    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-4)     {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-5)       "name": "Python: Hummingbot Application",      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-6)       "type": "python",      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-7)       "request": "launch",      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-8)       "program": "${workspaceRoot}/bin/hummingbot.py",      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-9)       "console": "integratedTerminal"    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-10)     }  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-11)   ] [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#__codelineno-3-12) }`

By executing the `Start Debugging` command, the debugger will automatically attach itself to the Hummingbot process. The Hummingbot app will appear in the `integratedTerminal`. You may change this as desired.

### PyCharm[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/debug/#pycharm "Permanent link")

Similarly, for PyCharm, you want to set up the debug configurations, as seen in the screenshot below.

![PyCharmDebugConfiguration](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/pycharm-debug-configurations.png)

For debugging it is neccessary that `Gevent compatible` in `Python Debugger` settings is enabled. See [Stackoverflow Q&A](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/39371676/debugger-times-out-at-collecting-data)
.

Note

As of this writing, there is no way to add breakpoints/log points to any of the Cython code in VSCode or PyCharm.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Debugging%20%26%20Testing%20Connectors+-+/connectors/connectors/debug/)
.

Back to top

---

# Rate Limits - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/rate-limits-share-pct/#rate-limits-share-pct)

Rate Limits Share Pct[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/rate-limits-share-pct/#rate-limits-share-pct "Permanent link")

=====================================================================================================================================

Some exchanges impose rate limits per account. When running multiple bots using a single account, `rate_limits_share_pct` users to set a certain percentage of the total limit to each instance. When the bot is near the allocated limit, Hummingbot sends a notification as a warning so users can adjust their configuration before the account is banned.

For example, the rate limit for AscendEX is 100 requests per second. Your account will be banned for a certain period of time if you keep hitting the rate limit in 10 minutes (status code `429` or `100014`).

Setting 50% for `rate_limits_share_pct` means we want the bot to send a notification when it starts to send 50 requests per second for that specific instance.

How to use the parameter[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/rate-limits-share-pct/#how-to-use-the-parameter "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1.  Run `config rate_limits_share_pct` while the strategy is stopped
2.  Enter the percentage of API rate limit you want to allocate to the bot ![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/rate-limits-share-pct-prompt.png)
3.  Start the strategy using `start` command
4.  A notification will be displayed in the output pane when the `rate_limits_share_pct` value is about to be reached ![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/rate-limits-share-pct-message.png)

Note

You can also configure this setting while the strategy is running. However, the strategy must be restarted for the changes to take effect.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Rate%20Limits+-+/client/global-configs/rate-limits-share-pct/)
.

# Paper Trade - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#adding-exchanges)

Paper Trade
===========

This feature allows users to test Hummingbot and simulate trading strategies without risking any actual assets.

Note

Exchange APIs are not required to run the bot on paper\_trade for Pure Market making, Cross Exchange Market Making and Avellaneda Market Making.

Adding Exchanges[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#adding-exchanges "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Users can now add paper exchanges by adding the exchange of choice in `conf_client.yml`. Previously, it was only available for AscendEX, Binance, Gate io, and Kucoin. Users can find `conf_client.yml` in `hummingbot/conf/conf_client.yml`

Add the paper trade exchange, for example kraken, to conf\_client.yml:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-0-1) paper_trade:   [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-0-2)   paper_trade_exchange:    [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-0-3)     - binance    [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-0-4)     - kucoin    [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-0-5)     - ascend_ex    [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-0-6)     - gate_io    [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-0-7)     - kraken`

In the Hummingbot client, kraken\_paper\_trade should now be available when you select an exchange:

Enter your maker spot connector >>> kraken\_paper\_trade

Enabling and Disabling[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#enabling-and-disabling "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Paper trading can be enabled when creating a strategy and choosing an exchange when prompted `Enter your maker spot connector` during the creation of the strategy.

![papertrade1](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/binance_papertrade.png)

Alternatively, you can enable paper trading by inputting `config exchange` then choose the exchange that supports paper trade.

![papertrade2](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/config_exchange.png)

To choose a different connector and go live, simply choose the exchange name without the `paper_trade` suffix then do the command `stop` and `start` so the changes will reflect on your configuration.

![papertrade3](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/papertrade_binance.png)

Adding Paper Trade Balance[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#adding-paper-trade-balance "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By default, the paper trade account has the following tokens and balances which you can see when you run the `balance paper` command.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-1) >>>  balance paper [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-2) Paper account balances:     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-3)     Asset    Balance      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-4)       DAI  1000.0000      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-5)       ETH    10.0000      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-6)       ONE  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-7)      TUSD  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-8)      USDC  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-9)      USDQ  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-10)      USDT  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-11)      WETH    10.0000      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-1-12)       ZRX  1000.0000`

When adding balances, specify the asset and balance you want by running this command `balance paper [asset] [amount]`.

For example, we want to add 0.5 BTC and check our paper account balance to confirm.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-1) >>>  balance paper BTC 0.5 [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-2) Paper balance for BTC token set to 0.5 [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-4) >>>  balance paper [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-5) Paper account balances:     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-6)     Asset    Balance      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-7)       BTC     0.5000      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-8)       DAI  1000.0000      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-9)       ETH    10.0000      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-10)       ONE  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-11)      TUSD  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-12)      USDC  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-13)      USDQ  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-14)      USDT  1000.0000     [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-15)      WETH    10.0000      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/paper-trade/#__codelineno-2-16)       ZRX  1000.0000`

.

# Broker - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/installation/broker/#phase-i)

Broker
======

Hummingbot's `brokers` module allows for remote control and monitoring of multi-bot environments in a distributed context , so that bots can "live" on different machines and infrastructures (e.g. having a bot local and another bot on AWS).

To achieve this approach, there is an MQTT layer for bots to connect remotely to message brokers, as a single point of reference, using asynchronous bidirectional communication channels (push/pull). In this architecture, bots can be considered as clients to the overall environment. Bot scaling is seamless and does not require any further setup, anyone can connect any number of bots the a message broker (e.g. RabbitMQ, EMQX etc) without any other dependencies.

See the following repos for more information:

*   [Brokers](https://github.com/hummingbot/brokers)
    : Various deployment examples using Docker Compose
*   [Remote client](https://github.com/hummingbot/hbot-remote-client-py)
    : Package that implements a remote client for Hummingbot in Python.

Watch the February 2023 community call that contains a demo of this feature:

**Thanks to [klpanagi](https://github.com/klpanagi)
 and [TheHolyRoger](https://github.com/TheHolyRoger)
 for your work! 🙏**

Phase I[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/broker/#phase-i "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_Released in_ _v1.12.0_\*

*   Interface to execute remote commands: `Start` , `Stop` , `Import` , `Config strategy`, `Balance` , `Change balance limits`
*   All these commands can be called using an unified web application that also receives the following information from the bots - `Heartbeat - Status`, `PNL - History`
*   The configuration of the broker in the client should be in the `conf_client.yml` file

Phase II[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/broker/#phase-ii "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

_Released in_ _v1.14.0_\*

In this Phase, an event and data layer will be integrated into the Hummingbot codebase to support receiving and handling remote events via the message broker (MQTT), such as the case of TradingView signals.

An MQTTEventListener will be developed and integrated into the hummingbot codebase, which will provide configuration for setting the URIs of the events to listen on. Upon receiving an event, a handling callback provided by the user/developer will be executed by the MQTTEventListener, so that users operate/develop their strategy based on the input event.

*   Defines interfaces for subscribing to external topics and listening to messages through the `EEventQueueFactory`, `EEventListenerFactory`, `ETopicQueueFactory`, and `ETopicListenerFactory` classes.
*   The specification defines a base URI format for consuming external events, and URI slashes are transformed to dots for multi-broker and multi-protocol support
*   This extends the global configuration and adds the `mqtt_external_events` parameter for globally enabling/disabling external events feature for bot instances.

Future phases[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/broker/#future-phases "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

See [this Notion doc](https://www.notion.so/hummingbot-foundation/Bot-Orchestration-fcac18bd90d74b0ebca9b260617522f0)
 for an overview of the project. This is an ongoing project funded by Proposal [HIP-20](https://snapshot.org/#/hbot-ip.eth/proposal/0x23e5e5ec459daea8bcb2228b2e18bc081d4b12cb5067d7a9f9efe157cc05ce16)
.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Broker+-+/installation/broker/)
.

# Command Line Autostart - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#strategy-autostart)

Strategy Autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#strategy-autostart "Permanent link")

============================================================================================================================

Docker autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#docker-autostart "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Warning

Running any trading bots without manual supervision may incur additional risks. It is imperative that you thoroughly understand and test the strategy and parameters before deploying bots that can trade in an unattended manner.

Hummingbot can automatically start the execution of a previously configured trading strategy upon launch without needing user interaction when provided with pre-existing configuration files. This can be very useful if you wish to deploy already well-tested strategies and configurations to cloud services and have Hummingbot running automatically in the background.

### Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

*   You have Hummingbot running via Docker Compose
*   You have already run the instance at least once and have set the password and API keys
*   You have at least one strategy configuration file that has been set up previously

### How to autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#how-to-autostart "Permanent link")

**Stop any running containers**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-0-1) docker compose down`

**Modify YAML file**

Use an IDE like [VSCode](https://code.visualstudio.com/)
 to edit the `docker-compose.yml` file.

Edit or add the section that defines the environment variables:

*   The `environment:` line
    
*   The `CONFIG_PASSWORD` line: add the Hummingbot password to login
    
*   One of `CONFIG_FILE_NAME` lines: add your script OR strategy config file
    
*   Add your `SCRIPT_CONFIG` file if using a configurable script
    

The final `environment` section of the YAML file should look something like this:

    `[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-1-1)     environment:      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-1-2)       - CONFIG_PASSWORD=password      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-1-3)       - CONFIG_FILE_NAME=simple_pmm_example.py      [](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-1-4)       - SCRIPT_CONFIG=conf_simple_pmm_example_config_1.yml`

Afterwards, save the file.

You can auto-start either a Script or a Strategy:

*   [Scripts](https://docs.hummingbot.org/scripts/)
     are Python files that contain all strategy logic. If you define a `.py` file as `CONFIG_FILE_NAME`, Hummingbot assumes it's a script file and looks for the `.py` file in the `hummingbot_files/scripts` directory.
    
*   [Strategies](https://docs.hummingbot.org/strategies/)
     are configurable strategy templates. If you define a `.yml` file as `CONFIG_FILE_NAME`, Hummingbot assumes it's a strategy config file and looks for the `.yml` file in the `hummingbot_files/conf/strategies` directory.
    

**Relaunch Hummingbot**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-2-1) docker compose up -d`

When you attach to it, the strategy or script should already be running:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-3-1) docker attach hummingbot`

Source autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#source-autostart "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#prerequisites_1 "Permanent link")

*   You have Hummingbot installed via Source.
*   You have already connected exchanges by adding API keys
*   You have at least one strategy configuration file that has been set up previously

### How to autostart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#how-to-autostart_1 "Permanent link")

Running unattended Hummingbot is very similar to running Hummingbot manually. The only differences are:

*   You will read the pre-existing configuration files to the `conf` directory.
*   You will pass some parameters telling Hummingbot which strategy configuration to use and the password to decrypt your API keys and wallets.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-4-1) bin/hummingbot_quickstart.py -p CONFIG_PASSWORD -f SCRIPT_FILE_NAME -c CONFIG_FILE_NAME`

Where  
`CONFIG_PASSWORD` is the config password `SCRIPT_FILE_NAME` is the script / strategy file name  
`CONFIG_FILE_NAME` is the script / strategy config file name

Example:

Let's say you configured your Hummingbot password as a single letter **a** and you created a config for the **Simple PMM Example** script which you then want to autostart as soon as you start the bot. Here's how you would configure the autostart command -

`[](https://hummingbot.org/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/#__codelineno-5-1) bin/hummingbot_quickstart.py -p a -f simple_pmm_example_config.py -c conf_simple_pmm_example_config_1.yml`

Where:

*   `a` is the config password
    
*   `simple_pmm_example_config.py` is the script / strategy file name
    
*   `conf_simple_pmm_example_config_1.yml` is the script / strategy config file name
    

More information on strategy can be found in [Strategy](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/)
.

More information on configuration file name can be found in [Configuring Hummingbot](https://hummingbot.org/client/config-files/)
.

More information on password can be found in [Create a secure password](https://hummingbot.org/client/password/)
.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Command%20Line%20Autostart+-+/client/global-configs/strategy-autostart/)
.
