### Input Validation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#input-validation "Permanent link")

All inputs must be validated:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-1) function validateAddress(address: string, chain: string): void {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-2)   if (!isValidAddress(address, chain)) {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-3)     throw new ValidationError('Invalid address format');  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-4)   } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-5) } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-6)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-7) function validateAmount(amount: string): BigNumber {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-8)   const bn = BigNumber.from(amount);  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-9)   if (bn.lte(0)) {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-10)     throw new ValidationError('Amount must be positive');  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-11)   }  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-12)   return bn; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-14-13) }`

### Private Key Handling[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#private-key-handling "Permanent link")

Never log or expose private keys:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-1) class WalletManager {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-2)   private wallets: Map<string, Wallet> = new Map(); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-3)  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-4)   addWallet(address: string, encryptedKey: string): void {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-5)     const wallet = this.decryptWallet(encryptedKey);    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-6)     this.wallets.set(address, wallet);    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-7)     // Never log wallet or private key  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-8)   } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-15-9) }`

### Rate Limiting[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#rate-limiting "Permanent link")

Implement rate limiting for external calls:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-1) class RateLimiter {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-2)   private calls: number[] = [];  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-3)   private limit: number;  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-4)   private window: number; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-5)  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-6)   async throttle(): Promise<void> {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-7)     const now = Date.now();    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-8)     this.calls = this.calls.filter(t => t > now - this.window); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-9)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-10)     if (this.calls.length >= this.limit) {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-11)       const waitTime = this.calls[0] + this.window - now;      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-12)       await sleep(waitTime);    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-13)     } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-14)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-15)     this.calls.push(now);  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-16)   } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-16-17) }`

Testing Architecture[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#testing-architecture "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Mock Framework[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#mock-framework "Permanent link")

Create comprehensive mocks for testing:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-1) class MockSDK {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-2)   async swap(params: SwapParams): Promise<MockTransaction> {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-3)     return {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-4)       hash: '0xmock...',      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-5)       gasUsed: BigNumber.from('100000'),      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-6)       status: 'success'    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-7)     };  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-8)   } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-17-9) }`

### Test Fixtures[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#test-fixtures "Permanent link")

Maintain test fixtures for common scenarios:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-1) export const fixtures = {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-2)   tokens: {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-3)     USDC: { address: '0x...', decimals: 6, symbol: 'USDC' },    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-4)     WETH: { address: '0x...', decimals: 18, symbol: 'WETH' }  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-5)   },  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-6)   pools: {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-7)     USDC_WETH: { address: '0x...', fee: 3000 }  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-8)   } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-18-9) };`

Best Practices[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#best-practices "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 1\. Separation of Concerns[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#1-separation-of-concerns "Permanent link")

*   Keep business logic in connector class
*   Route handlers only handle HTTP concerns
*   Utils for pure functions

### 2\. Dependency Injection[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#2-dependency-injection "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-19-1) class MyDex {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-19-2)   constructor(    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-19-3)     private sdk: ISDK,    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-19-4)     private cache: ICache,    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-19-5)     private logger: ILogger  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-19-6)   ) {} [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-19-7) }`

### 3\. Immutability[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#3-immutability "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-20-1) // Good [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-20-2) const newState = { ...oldState, updated: true }; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-20-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-20-4) // Avoid [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-20-5) oldState.updated = true;`

### 4\. Async/Await Consistency[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#4-asyncawait-consistency "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-1) // Good [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-2) async function process(): Promise<Result> {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-3)   const data = await fetchData();  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-4)   return transform(data); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-5) } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-6)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-7) // Avoid mixing patterns [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-8) function process(): Promise<Result> {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-9)   return fetchData().then(transform); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-21-10) }`

### 5\. Error Context[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#5-error-context "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-1) try {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-2)   await operation(); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-3) } catch (error) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-4)   throw new ConnectorError(    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-5)     'Operation failed',    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-6)     {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-7)       originalError: error,      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-8)       context: { operation: 'swap', params }    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-9)     }  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-10)   ); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#__codelineno-22-11) }`

Resources[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/architecture/#resources "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Schema Definitions](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/tree/development/src/schemas)
    
*   [Example Implementations](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/tree/development/src/connectors)

# Quants Lab - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#quants-lab)

Quants Lab[¶](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#quants-lab "Permanent link")

==============================================================================

Quants Lab contains interactive notebooks and task schedulers for quantitative trading research and development. It provides comprehensive tools for data collection, backtesting, strategy development, and automated task management.

**GitHub Repository**: [github.com/hummingbot/quants-lab](https://github.com/hummingbot/quants-lab)

What is Quants Lab?[¶](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#what-is-quants-lab "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Quants Lab acts as a research and development platform for quantitative traders, enabling systematic strategy creation and testing. It bridges the gap between raw market data and executable trading strategies, providing a complete toolkit for quants and algorithmic traders.

Quants Lab enables quantitative traders to:

*   📊 Collect and process market data from multiple sources
*   🔍 Build custom screeners for specific trading signals or opportunities
*   🧠 Develop and backtest trading strategies
*   📣 Schedule Telegram, Discord, or email reports for automated notifications

Under the hood, Quants Lab uses the [Hummingbot Python library](https://pypi.org/project/hummingbot/)
 and is designed to be compatible with other Hummingbot repos.

Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#installation "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clone the Quants-Lab Github repo to download it to your machine, and then enter the folder:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-0-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/quants-lab.git [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-0-2) cd quants-lab`

Then, run the one-command installation script `install.sh`:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-1) ./install.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-3) [INFO] 🚀 Welcome to QuantsLab Installation! [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-4)[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-5) [INFO] This script will: [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-6) [INFO]   1. Check prerequisites (conda, docker, docker compose) [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-7) [INFO]   2. Create conda environment from environment.yml [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-8) [INFO]   3. Install QuantsLab package in development mode [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-9) [INFO]   4. Setup databases (optional) [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-10) [INFO]   5. Create .env file with defaults [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-1-11) [INFO]   6. Test the installation`

This script create a `quants-lab` Anaconda/Miniconda environment with all dependencies. Then, it sets up a MongoDB database for storage and creates a new `.env` file that contains starting environment variables.

For more information about other installation options, see the [Quants Lab Github repository](https://github.com/hummingbot/quants-lab)
.

Usage[¶](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#usage "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------

To get started, activate the `quants-lab` environment, explore available notebooks, and then customize them for your needs.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-2-1) # Activate environment [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-2-2) conda activate quants-lab [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-2-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-2-4) # Launch Jupyter notebooks [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-2-5) jupyter lab [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-2-6)[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-2-7) # Navigate to research_notebooks/ folders`

You can also create and schedule automated runs of tasks, as well as individual notebooks:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-3-1) # List available tasks [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-3-2) python cli.py list-tasks [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-3-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-3-4) # Run single task [](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#__codelineno-3-5) python cli.py trigger-task --task pools_screener --config config/pools_screener_v2.yml`

Next Steps[¶](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#next-steps "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After successful installation:

1.  Explore example notebooks in `research_notebooks/`
2.  Schedule automated runs of notebooks and tasks in `config/`
3.  Join our [Discord](https://discord.gg/hummingbot)
     for community support
4.  Create [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/hummingbot/quants-lab/issues)
     for bug reports and requested features

Tutorials[¶](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#tutorials "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Warning

The videos below demonstrate features from an pre-release version of Quants Lab. Some interfaces and functionalities may have changed in the official release.

### Hummingbot Live: Quants Lab[¶](https://hummingbot.org/quants-lab/#hummingbot-live-quants-lab "Permanent link")

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Quants%20Lab+-+/quants-lab/)
.

# Updating to New Versions - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#updating-to-new-versions)

Updating to New Versions[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#updating-to-new-versions "Permanent link")

===================================================================================================================

Hummingbot Foundation actively maintains all repositories in the Hummingbot ecosystem and ships versioned releases of Hummingbot Client and Gateway monthly. These releases work with user interfaces like Condor and MCP, add new exchange connectors and strategies, and include bug fixes and API updates.

This page shows you how to update your installation to the latest version.

Standard Updates[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#standard-updates "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For most releases where the configuration schema hasn't changed, follow these steps:

### Hummingbot API[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#hummingbot-api "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-0-1) docker compose down hummingbot-api [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-0-2) docker pull hummingbot/hummingbot-api:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-0-3) docker compose up hummingbot-api -d`

### Hummingbot Client[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#hummingbot-client "Permanent link")

**Docker:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-1-1) docker compose down [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-1-2) docker pull hummingbot/hummingbot:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-1-3) docker compose up -d`

**Source:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-2-1) cd hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-2-2) git pull origin master [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-2-3) ./install [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-2-4) ./compile [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-2-5) ./start`

### Gateway[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#gateway "Permanent link")

**Docker:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-3-1) docker compose down [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-3-2) docker pull hummingbot/gateway:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-3-3) docker compose up -d`

**Source:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-4-1) cd gateway [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-4-2) git pull origin main [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-4-3) pnpm install [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-4-4) pnpm start --dev --passphrase=PASSPHRASE`

* * *

Updates with Configuration Changes[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#updates-with-configuration-changes "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Some releases include changes to configuration file schemas. When this happens, existing configuration files are incompatible and must be removed before updating. Check the [release notes](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/)
 for your target version to see if configuration changes are required.

The main configuration schemas reside in:

*   **Hummingbot Client**: `conf/conf_client.yml` - Contains client settings like logging, color scheme, and global parameters
*   **Gateway**: `conf/` folder - Contains chain, network, and connector configurations

Back Up First

If you have custom settings you want to preserve, back up your configuration files before removing them. After updating, you can reference your backups to re-apply custom settings to the new configuration files.

### Hummingbot API[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#hummingbot-api_1 "Permanent link")

Hummingbot API uses the same `gateway-files` directory as Gateway for configuration.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-5-1) cd hummingbot-api [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-5-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-5-3) # Back up configs (optional) [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-5-4) cp -r gateway-files gateway-files.backup [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-5-5)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-5-6) # Remove legacy configs [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-5-7) rm -rf gateway-files`

**Docker**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-6-1) docker compose down hummingbot-api [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-6-2) docker pull hummingbot/hummingbot-api:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-6-3) docker compose up hummingbot-api -d`

**Source:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-7-1) git pull origin main [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-7-2) docker compose up emqx postgres -d [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-7-3) conda activate hummingbot-api && uvicorn main:app --reload`

### Hummingbot Client[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#hummingbot-client_1 "Permanent link")

**Docker:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-1) # Back up config (optional) [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-2) cp conf/conf_client.yml conf/conf_client.yml.backup [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-4) # Remove legacy config [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-5) rm conf/conf_client.yml [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-6)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-7) # Update and restart [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-8) docker compose down [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-9) docker pull hummingbot/hummingbot:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-8-10) docker compose up -d`

**Source:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-1) cd hummingbot [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-3) # Remove legacy environment [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-4) conda env remove -n hummingbot -y [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-5)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-6) # Back up config (optional) [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-7) cp conf/conf_client.yml conf/conf_client.yml.backup [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-8)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-9) # Update, reinstall, and compile [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-10) git pull origin master [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-11) ./install [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-12) ./compile [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-9-13) ./start`

### Gateway[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#gateway_1 "Permanent link")

**Docker:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-1) # Back up configs (optional) [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-2) cp -r gateway_files gateway_files.backup [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-4) # Remove legacy configs [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-5) rm -rf gateway_files [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-6)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-7) # Update and restart [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-8) docker compose down [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-9) docker pull hummingbot/gateway:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-10-10) docker compose up -d`

**Source:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-1) cd gateway [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-3) # Back up config (optional) [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-4) cp -r conf gateway_conf.backup [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-5)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-6) # Update and re-run setup [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-7) git pull origin main [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-8)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-9) # Re-run setup to generate new config [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-10) pnpm setup              # interactive setup [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-11) # or [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-12) pnpm setup:with-defaults  # use default settings [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-13)[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-14) # Start Gateway [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/#__codelineno-11-15) pnpm start --dev --passphrase=PASSPHRASE`

After updating, fresh configuration files with the correct schema will be generated automatically.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Updating%20to%20New%20Versions+-+/installation/update/)
.

# RPC Providers - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#overview)

RPC Providers
=============

Overview[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#overview "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Starting with [v2.9.0](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.9.0/)
, deep integrations with leading RPC providers like Helius and Infura are available to optimize speed and reduce latency for DEX trading.

The RPC provider controls your bot's connection to the blockchain network, which is crucial in DEX trading because it directly impacts the speed, reliability, and security of your transactions. A robust node connection ensures:

*   **Low Latency:** Faster order placement and execution with greater rate limits.
*   **High Availability:** Reliable access to blockchain data, minimizing downtime or missed trades.
*   **Accurate Data:** Up-to-date blockchain state for price feeds, balances, and transaction statuses.
*   **MEV Protection:** Direct connections to trusted nodes help reduce the risk of having your transactions front-run or sandwiched.

Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#setup "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------

When you set up Gateway initially, the standard `nodeURL` for each network uses default public RPC endpoints.

By adding an API key from a RPC provider (as shown below), you will override the default `nodeURL` for each supported network, ensuring more reliable and performant blockchain connectivity. This step is essential for optimal DEX trading performance.

Run `gateway ping` to check your current network and node connection:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-1) >>> gateway ping [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-3) Gateway service is online. [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-4) Testing network status for 2 chains...  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-5)[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-6) ethereum (mainnet): [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-7) - RPC URL: https://mainnet.infura.io/v3/<api-key> [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-8) - Current Block: 23440952 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-9) - Native Currency: ETH [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-10) - Status: ✓ Connected [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-11)[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-12) solana (mainnet-beta):  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-13) - RPC URL: https://mainnet.helius-rpc.com/?api-key=<api-key> [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-14) - Current Block: 369194830 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-15) - Native Currency: SOL [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-0-16) - Status: ✓ Connected`

Supported Providers[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#supported-providers "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Helius[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#helius "Permanent link")

[Helius](https://www.helius.dev/)
 is a leading Solana validator and infrastructure provider, offering fast, reliable, and scalable RPC endpoints and other services.

**Helius Supported Networks:**

| Chain | Network |
| --- | --- |
| solana | mainnet-beta |
| solana | devnet |

**Adding Helius API Keys:**

1.  Create a free account at [Helius](https://helius.dev/)
     to get your API key
    
2.  Run `gateway config helius update` and add the API key. Alternatively, modify the file `conf/rpc/helius.yml` and restart Gateway.
    
3.  Run `gateway config solana update` and change `rpcProvider` from `url` to `helius`. Alternatively, modify the file `conf/chains/solana.yml` and restart Gateway.
    

**Helius Configuration**

Adjust these settings in your `conf/rpc/helius.yml` file as needed for your deployment.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-1-1) helius:   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-1-2)   apiKey: YOUR_HELIUS_API_KEY  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-1-3)   useWebSocketRPC: false  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-1-4)   useSender: false  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-1-5)   regionCode: slc  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-1-6)   jitoTipSOL: 0.001`

### Infura[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#infura "Permanent link")

[Infura](https://www.infura.io/)
, a division of Metamask, is a leading RPC provider for EVM-based networks.

**Infura Supported Networks:**

| Chain | Network | Chain ID |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ethereum | mainnet | 1   |
| ethereum | arbitrum | 42161 |
| ethereum | avalanche | 43114 |
| ethereum | base | 8453 |
| ethereum | bsc | 56  |
| ethereum | celo | 42220 |
| ethereum | polygon | 137 |
| ethereum | sepolia | 11155111 |

**Adding Infura API Key:**

1.  Create a free account at [Infura](https://app.infura.io/)
     to get your API key
    
2.  Run `gateway config infura update` and add the API key. Alternatively, modify the file `conf/rpc/infura.yml` and restart Gateway.
    
3.  Run `gateway config ethereum update` and change `rpcProvider` from `url` to `infura`. Alternatively, modify the file `conf/chains/ethereum.yml` and restart Gateway.
    

**Infura Configuration:**

Adjust these settings in your `conf/rpc/infura.yml` file as needed for your deployment.

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-2-1) apiKey: YOUR_INFURA_API_KEY [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#__codelineno-2-2) useWebSocket: false`

Troubleshooting[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#troubleshooting "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#### Provider Not Connecting[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#provider-not-connecting "Permanent link")

*   Run `gateway ping` to assess connection
*   Verify API key is correct
*   Check network configuration

#### Rate Limiting[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/rpc/#rate-limiting "Permanent link")

*   Change Hummingbot `tick_size` to a higher number
*   Consider upgrading to a paid tier for better performance
*   Distribute usage across multiple API keys

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+RPC%20Providers+-+/gateway/rpc/)
.

Back to top

# Strategies & Snippets - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#available-scripts-and-strategies)

Strategies & Snippets
=====================

Gateway enables sophisticated trading strategies on decentralized exchanges through Hummingbot. This page lists available Gateway-compatible strategies/scripts along with commonly used code snippets.

Available Scripts and Strategies[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#available-scripts-and-strategies "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following table lists Gateway-compatible scripts and strategies available in the Hummingbot repository. All links point to the development branch where the latest versions are maintained.

| Name | Type | Description | Supported Schemas |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [AMM Data Feed Example](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/development/scripts/amm_data_feed_example.py) | Script | Fetches real-time price data and monitors pool reserves from AMM pools | Router, AMM, CLMM |
| [AMM Trade Example](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/development/scripts/amm_trade_example.py) | Script | Executes token swaps on AMM and CLMM pools with configurable parameters | Router, AMM, CLMM |
| [LP Manage Position](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/development/scripts/lp_manage_position.py) | Script | Manages liquidity positions including adding, removing, and collecting fees | AMM, CLMM |
| [AMM Arbitrage](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/tree/development/hummingbot/strategy/amm_arb) | V1 Strategy | Arbitrage between CEX and DEX markets | Router, AMM, CLMMuter |
| [Cross Exchange Market Making](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/tree/development/hummingbot/strategy/cross_exchange_market_making) | V1 Strategy | Market making with Gateway connector as taker market | Router, AMM, CLMM |
| [Arbitrage Controller](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/development/controllers/generic/arbitrage_controller.py) | V2 Controller | Creates ArbitrageExecutors between two markets | Router, AMM, CLMM |
| [XEMM Controller](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/blob/development/controllers/generic/xemm_controller.py) | V2 Controller | Creates XEMMExecutors between two markets | Router, AMM, CLMM |

Code Snippets[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#code-snippets "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The following code snippets demonstrate common Gateway operations in Hummingbot scripts and strategies.

### Data Feed[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#data-feed "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-0-1) amm_data_feed = AmmGatewayDataFeed(             [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-0-2)             connector="jupiter/router",            [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-0-3)             trading_pairs={"SOL-USDC","JUP-USDC"}            [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-0-4)             order_amount_in_base=Decimal("1.0")        [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-0-5)         )`

### Connect Market[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#connect-market "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-1-1) @classmethod [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-1-2) def init_markets(cls):         [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-1-3)         cls.markets = {"jupiter/router": {"SOL-USDC"}} [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-1-4)[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-1-5) def __init__(self, connectors: Dict[str, ConnectorBase]):         [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-1-6)         super().__init__(connectors)`

### Get Price[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#get-price "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-2-1) current_price = await self.connectors["jupiter/router"].get_quote_price(                     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-2-2)                     trading_pair="SOL-USDC",                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-2-3)                     is_buy=True,                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-2-4)                     amount=Decimal("1.0"),                [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-2-5)                 )`

### Get Balance[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#get-balance "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-3-1) connector = self.connectors["jupiter/router"] [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-3-2) await connector.update_balances(on_interval=False) [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-3-3) balance = connector.get_balance("SOL")`

### Place Order[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#place-order "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-4-1) connector = self.connectors["jupiter/router"] [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-4-2) order_id = connector.place_order(                     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-4-3)                     is_buy=True,                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-4-4)                     trading_pair="SOL-USDC",                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-4-5)                     amount=Decimal("1.0"),                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-4-6)                     price=current_price,                [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-4-7)                 )`

### Get LP Position Info[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#get-lp-position-info "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-5-1) position_info = await self.connectors["jupiter/router"].get_position_info(                     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-5-2)                     trading_pair="SOL-USDC",                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-5-3)                     position_address="<position-address>"                [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-5-4)                 )`

### Add Liquidity[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#add-liquidity "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-6-1) order_id = self.connectors["meteora/clmm"].add_liquidity(                     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-6-2)                     trading_pair="SOL-USDC",                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-6-3)                     price=current_price,                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-6-4)                     upper_width_pct=10.0,                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-6-5)                     lower_width_pct=10.0,                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-6-6)                     base_token_amount=0.1,                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-6-7)                     quote_token_amount=20,                [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-6-8)                 )`

### Remove Liquidity[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#remove-liquidity "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-7-1) order_id = self.connectors["meteora/clmm"].remove_liquidity(                     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-7-2)                     trading_pair="SOL-USDC",                    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-7-3)                     position_address="<position-address>"                [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/#__codelineno-7-4)                 )`

.

# Hummingbot API Quickstart - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#hummingbot-api-quickstart)

Hummingbot API Quickstart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#hummingbot-api-quickstart "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================================================

This guide walks you through deploying the Hummingbot API on a cloud server and setting up Condor for Telegram-based control.

For source installation, API endpoints, and advanced configuration, see [Hummingbot API Documentation](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/)
.

What You'll Set Up[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#what-youll-set-up "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By the end of this guide, you'll have:

*   **Hummingbot API** - REST API backend for bot management and trading (port 8000)
*   **Condor** - Telegram bot for monitoring and controlling Hummingbot API from mobile and desktop

This setup is best for managing multiple bot instances on a cloud server (AWS, Digital Ocean, etc.) with mobile access via Telegram.

Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hummingbot API is designed to run on a Linux-based cloud server for 24/7 operation. We recommend using a VPS from providers like AWS, Google Cloud, or Digital Ocean.

### System Requirements[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#system-requirements "Permanent link")

| **Component** | **Specifications** |
| --- | --- |
| **Operating System** | Linux x64 or ARM (Ubuntu 20.04+, Debian 10+) |
| **Memory** | 4 GB RAM minimum |
| **Storage** | 10 GB SSD |
| **CPU** | 2 vCPUs minimum |

### Cloud Server Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#cloud-server-setup "Permanent link")

AWS EC2Google CloudDigital Ocean

1.  Launch an EC2 instance with **Ubuntu 22.04 LTS**
2.  Choose instance type: **t3.medium** (2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM) or larger
3.  Configure security group to allow inbound traffic on ports **22** (SSH), **8000** (API), and **8501** (Dashboard)
4.  Connect via SSH:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-0-1) ssh -i your-key.pem ubuntu@your-instance-ip`
    

1.  Create a Compute Engine VM with **Ubuntu 22.04 LTS**
2.  Choose machine type: **e2-medium** (2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM) or larger
3.  Configure firewall rules to allow TCP ports **22**, **8000**, and **8501**
4.  Connect via SSH:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-1-1) gcloud compute ssh your-instance-name`
    

1.  Create a Droplet with **Ubuntu 22.04 LTS**
2.  Choose size: **Basic $24/mo** (2 vCPU, 4 GB RAM) or larger
3.  Connect via SSH:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-1) ssh root@your-droplet-ip`
    

### Install Docker[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#install-docker "Permanent link")

Once connected to your server, install Docker:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-3-1) curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com -o get-docker.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-3-2) sh get-docker.sh`

Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#installation "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clone the repository and run the setup script:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-4-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api.git [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-4-2) cd hummingbot-api [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-4-3) chmod +x setup.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-4-4) ./setup.sh`

The setup script will:

Prompt you for credentials (default: `admin`/`admin`)

Start all services

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-5-1) make deploy`

Access Your Platform[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#access-your-platform "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After setup completes, you can interact with Hummingbot API through:

| Interface | Description |
| --- | --- |
| **Condor** | Telegram bot for mobile/desktop control (setup below) |
| **MCP** | Connect AI assistants like Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini ([setup guide](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/)<br>) |
| **Swagger UI** | Interactive API docs at [http://localhost:8000/docs](http://localhost:8000/docs) |

Cloud Servers

If using a cloud server or VPS, replace `localhost` with your server's IP address. Configure firewall rules to allow inbound connections on port **8000**.

Add Condor Telegram Interface[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#add-condor-telegram-interface "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Condor](https://github.com/hummingbot/condor)
 is a Telegram bot that lets you monitor and control your Hummingbot instances from your phone. It's the recommended interface for managing bots deployed with Hummingbot API.

### Create a Telegram Bot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#create-a-telegram-bot "Permanent link")

1.  Open Telegram and search for [@BotFather](https://t.me/botfather)
    
2.  Send `/newbot` and follow the prompts to create your bot
3.  Copy the **bot token** (looks like `123456789:ABCdefGHIjklMNOpqrsTUVwxyz`)

### Get Your Telegram User ID[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#get-your-telegram-user-id "Permanent link")

You'll need your Telegram User ID to authorize yourself:

1.  Search for [@userinfobot](https://t.me/userinfobot)
     on Telegram
2.  Send `/start` - it will reply with your User ID (a number like `123456789`)

### Install Condor[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#install-condor "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-6-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/condor.git [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-6-2) cd condor [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-6-3) ./setup-environment.sh`

The setup script will prompt you for:

*   `TELEGRAM_TOKEN`: Your bot token from BotFather
*   `AUTHORIZED_USERS`: Your Telegram User ID (comma-separated for multiple users)

### Start Condor[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#start-condor "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-7-1) docker compose up -d`

### Connect to Your Bot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/#connect-to-your-bot "Permanent link")

1.  Open your bot in Telegram and send `/start`
2.  Use `/config` → **API Servers** to connect to your Hummingbot API (default: `localhost:8000`)
3.  Use `/config` → **API Keys** to add exchange credentials
4.  Use `/config` → **Gateway** to enable DEX trading (optional)

For detailed Condor usage, see [Condor Documentation](https://hummingbot.org/condor/)
.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Hummingbot%20API%20Quickstart+-+/installation/hummingbot-api/)
.

# API Routers - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#api-routers)

API Routers[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#api-routers "Permanent link")

============================================================================================

The Hummingbot API is organized into several functional routers that provide comprehensive trading and management capabilities.

🐳 Docker Management (`/docker`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#docker-management-docker "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Manage Docker containers and bot deployment lifecycle:

*   Check Docker daemon status and health
*   Pull new Docker images with async support
*   Start, stop, and remove containers
*   Monitor active and exited containers
*   Clean up exited containers
*   Archive container data locally or to S3
*   Track image pull status and progress

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /docker/running` - Check if Docker daemon is running
*   `GET /docker/available-images` - List available Docker images
*   `GET /docker/active-containers` - Get all running containers
*   `POST /docker/pull-image` - Pull new Docker images
*   `POST /docker/start-container/{name}` - Start a container
*   `POST /docker/stop-container/{name}` - Stop a container
*   `POST /docker/remove-container/{name}` - Remove container and archive data

💳 Account Management (`/accounts`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#account-management-accounts "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Create and manage trading accounts across multiple exchanges:

*   Create and delete trading accounts
*   Add/remove exchange credentials
*   List available credentials per account
*   Basic account configuration

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /accounts` - List all trading accounts
*   `POST /accounts` - Create new trading account
*   `PUT /accounts/{id}` - Update account credentials
*   `DELETE /accounts/{id}` - Delete trading account
*   `GET /accounts/{id}/balances` - Get account balances

🔌 Connector Discovery (`/connectors`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#connector-discovery-connectors "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Provides exchange connector information and configuration:

*   List available exchange connectors
*   Get connector configuration requirements
*   Retrieve trading rules and constraints
*   Query supported order types per connector

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /connectors` - List all available connectors
*   `GET /connectors/{name}` - Get connector details
*   `GET /connectors/{name}/trading-rules` - Get trading rules and limits
*   `GET /connectors/{name}/markets` - List supported trading pairs

📊 Portfolio Management (`/portfolio`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#portfolio-management-portfolio "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Centralized portfolio tracking and analytics across all accounts:

*   **Real-time Portfolio State**: Current balances across all accounts
*   **Portfolio History**: Time-series data with cursor-based pagination
*   **Token Distribution**: Aggregate holdings by token across exchanges
*   **Account Distribution**: Percentage-based portfolio allocation analysis
*   **Advanced Filtering**: Filter by account names and connectors

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /portfolio/balances` - Get aggregated portfolio balances
*   `GET /portfolio/performance` - Get portfolio performance metrics
*   `GET /portfolio/distribution` - Get token distribution analysis
*   `GET /portfolio/history` - Get historical portfolio data

💹 Trading Operations (`/trading`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#trading-operations-trading "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Enhanced with POST-based filtering and comprehensive order/trade management:

*   **Order Placement**: Execute trades with advanced order types
*   **Order Cancellation**: Cancel specific orders by ID
*   **Position Tracking**: Real-time perpetual positions with PnL data
*   **Active Orders**: Live order monitoring from connector in-flight orders
*   **Order History**: Paginated historical orders with advanced filtering
*   **Trade History**: Complete execution records with filtering
*   **Funding Payments**: Historical funding payment tracking for perpetuals
*   **Position Modes**: Configure HEDGE/ONEWAY modes for perpetual trading
*   **Leverage Management**: Set and adjust leverage per trading pair

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `POST /trading/orders` - Place new order
*   `GET /trading/orders` - List active orders
*   `DELETE /trading/orders/{id}` - Cancel order
*   `GET /trading/positions` - Get open positions
*   `GET /trading/history` - Get trade history
*   `POST /trading/close-position` - Close a position

🤖 Bot Orchestration (`/bot-orchestration`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#bot-orchestration-bot-orchestration "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Deploy, configure, and manage multiple bot instances:

*   Monitor bot status and MQTT connectivity
*   Deploy V2 scripts and controllers
*   Start/stop bots with configurable parameters
*   Stop and archive bots with background task support
*   Retrieve bot performance history
*   Real-time bot status monitoring

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /bot-orchestration/bots` - List all bot instances
*   `POST /bot-orchestration/deploy` - Deploy new bot
*   `PUT /bot-orchestration/bots/{id}/config` - Update bot configuration
*   `POST /bot-orchestration/bots/{id}/start` - Start bot
*   `POST /bot-orchestration/bots/{id}/stop` - Stop bot
*   `GET /bot-orchestration/bots/{id}/status` - Get bot status

📋 Strategy Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#strategy-management "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Manage trading strategies with two complementary systems:

### Controllers (`/controllers`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#controllers-controllers "Permanent link")

Manage V2 strategy controllers:

*   CRUD operations on controller files
*   Controller configuration management
*   Bot-specific controller configurations
*   Template retrieval for new configs

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /controllers` - List available strategy controllers
*   `POST /controllers/{name}/deploy` - Deploy strategy controller
*   `PUT /controllers/{id}/config` - Update strategy parameters

### Scripts (`/scripts`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#scripts-scripts "Permanent link")

Handle traditional Hummingbot scripts:

*   CRUD operations on script files
*   Script configuration management
*   Configuration templates

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /scripts` - List available trading scripts
*   `POST /scripts/run` - Execute trading script

📊 Market Data (`/market-data`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#market-data-market-data "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Professional market data analysis and real-time feeds:

*   **Price Discovery**: Real-time prices, funding rates, mark/index prices
*   **Candle Data**: Real-time and historical candles with multiple intervals
*   **Order Book Analysis**:
*   Live order book snapshots
*   Price impact calculations
*   Volume queries at specific price levels
*   VWAP (Volume-Weighted Average Price) calculations
*   **Feed Management**: Active feed monitoring with automatic cleanup

**Key Endpoints:** - `GET /market-data/ticker/{pair}` - Get current ticker data - `GET /market-data/orderbook/{pair}` - Get order book snapshot - `GET /market-data/candles/{pair}` - Get historical candles - `GET /market-data/trades/{pair}` - Get recent trades - `WS /market-data/stream` - Real-time market data stream

🔄 Backtesting (`/backtesting`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#backtesting-backtesting "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Run strategy backtests with historical data:

*   Run strategy backtests against historical data
*   Support for controller configurations
*   Customizable trade costs and resolution

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `POST /backtesting/run` - Start new backtest
*   `GET /backtesting/results/{id}` - Get backtest results
*   `GET /backtesting/metrics/{id}` - Get performance metrics
*   `POST /backtesting/optimize` - Run parameter optimization

📈 Archived Bot Analytics (`/archived-bots`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#archived-bot-analytics-archived-bots "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Comprehensive analysis of stopped bot performance:

*   List and discover archived bot databases
*   Performance metrics and trade analysis
*   Historical order and trade retrieval
*   Position and executor data extraction
*   Controller configuration recovery
*   Support for both V1 and V2 bot architectures

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /archived-bots` - List all archived bot databases
*   `GET /archived-bots/{bot_name}/performance` - Get bot performance metrics
*   `GET /archived-bots/{bot_name}/orders` - Get historical orders
*   `GET /archived-bots/{bot_name}/trades` - Get trade history

🌐 Gateway (`/gateway`)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#gateway-gateway "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Manage Gateway container and configure DEX trading infrastructure:

### Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#management "Permanent link")

Control Gateway container lifecycle and configuration:

*   Start, stop, and restart Gateway container
*   Check Gateway status and health
*   View Gateway logs
*   List and configure DEX connectors
*   Manage chains, networks, and pools
*   Add and manage network tokens

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /gateway/status` - Get Gateway status
*   `POST /gateway/start` - Start Gateway
*   `POST /gateway/stop` - Stop Gateway
*   `POST /gateway/restart` - Restart Gateway
*   `GET /gateway/logs` - Get Gateway logs
*   `GET /gateway/connectors` - List connectors
*   `GET /gateway/connectors/{connector_name}` - Get connector config
*   `POST /gateway/connectors/{connector_name}` - Update connector config
*   `GET /gateway/chains` - List chains
*   `GET /gateway/pools` - List pools
*   `POST /gateway/pools` - Add pool
*   `GET /gateway/networks` - List networks
*   `GET /gateway/networks/{network_id}` - Get network config
*   `POST /gateway/networks/{network_id}` - Update network config
*   `GET /gateway/networks/{network_id}/tokens` - Get network tokens
*   `POST /gateway/networks/{network_id}/tokens` - Add network token
*   `DELETE /gateway/networks/{network_id}/tokens/{token_address}` - Delete network token

### Swap Operations[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#swap-operations "Permanent link")

Execute and monitor DEX swaps on supported routers (Jupiter, 0x, etc.):

*   Get swap quotes with price impact
*   Execute swaps with slippage protection
*   Track swap transaction status
*   Search swap history with filters
*   View swap summaries and analytics

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `POST /gateway/swap/quote` - Get swap quote
*   `POST /gateway/swap/execute` - Execute swap
*   `GET /gateway/swaps/{transaction_hash}/status` - Get swap status
*   `POST /gateway/swaps/search` - Search swaps
*   `GET /gateway/swaps/summary` - Get swaps summary

### CLMM Operations[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#clmm-operations "Permanent link")

Manage concentrated liquidity market maker positions on DEXs:

*   Browse and analyze CLMM pools
*   Open liquidity positions with custom price ranges
*   Close positions and withdraw liquidity
*   Collect earned fees from positions
*   Track position-owned assets
*   View position events and history
*   Search positions with filters

**Key Endpoints:**

*   `GET /gateway/clmm/pool-info` - Get CLMM pool info
*   `GET /gateway/clmm/pools` - Get CLMM pools
*   `POST /gateway/clmm/open` - Open CLMM position
*   `POST /gateway/clmm/close` - Close CLMM position
*   `POST /gateway/clmm/collect-fees` - Collect fees from CLMM position
*   `POST /gateway/clmm/positions_owned` - Get CLMM positions owned
*   `GET /gateway/clmm/positions/{position_address}/events` - Get CLMM position events
*   `POST /gateway/clmm/positions/search` - Search CLMM positions

Authentication[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#authentication "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All API endpoints require HTTP Basic Authentication. Include your configured credentials in all requests:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#__codelineno-0-1) curl -u username:password http://localhost:8000/endpoint`

Configure username and password during setup or update them in the `.env` file.

Interactive Documentation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/#interactive-documentation "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For detailed endpoint documentation and testing:

*   **Swagger UI**: `http://localhost:8000/docs` - Interactive API testing
*   **ReDoc**: `http://localhost:8000/redoc` - Alternative documentation view

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+API%20Routers+-+/hummingbot-api/routers/)
.

# Installation - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#hummingbot-mcp-server-installation-guide)

Hummingbot MCP Server Installation Guide[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#hummingbot-mcp-server-installation-guide "Permanent link")

================================================================================================================================================

This guide shows you how to connect AI assistants to the Hummingbot API using the MCP (Model Context Protocol) server.

📋 Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Before starting, ensure you have:

1.  **Hummingbot API Server** running ([Hummingbot API Quickstart](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/)
    )
2.  **Docker** installed and running
3.  **API credentials** (username/password from setup)
4.  **An AI Assistant** - Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, or Claude Desktop

* * *

🚀 Install via CLI (Recommended)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#install-via-cli-recommended "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The simplest way to connect AI assistants using command-line tools.

### Claude Code[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#claude-code "Permanent link")

**Claude Code** is Anthropic's official CLI tool for using Claude in your terminal.

#### Quick Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#quick-setup "Permanent link")

Add the MCP server with one command:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-0-1) claude mcp add --transport stdio hummingbot -- docker run --rm -i -e HUMMINGBOT_API_URL=http://host.docker.internal:8000 -v hummingbot_mcp:/root/.hummingbot_mcp hummingbot/hummingbot-mcp:latest`

Local Hummingbot API URL

If you installed Hummingbot API from source and it's running locally outside of Docker, use `http://localhost:8000` instead of `http://host.docker.internal:8000`

#### Start Trading[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#start-trading "Permanent link")

Use natural language in your terminal:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-1-1) "Show me my portfolio balances" [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-1-2) "Create a market making strategy for ETH-USDT on Binance" [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-1-3) "What are my open positions?" [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-1-4) "Start Gateway in development mode for DEX trading"`

### Gemini CLI[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#gemini-cli "Permanent link")

![gemini](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/gemini.png)

**Gemini CLI** is Google's AI agent for your terminal.

#### Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#installation "Permanent link")

Install Gemini CLI: [Official Guide](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli#-installation)

#### Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#configuration "Permanent link")

1.  Navigate to config folder:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-2-1) cd ~/.gemini`
    
2.  Edit `settings.json`:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-3-1) nano settings.json`
    
3.  Add MCP server:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-2)   "mcpServers": {    [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-3)     "hummingbot": {      [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-4)       "command": "docker",      [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-5)       "args": [        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-6)         "run",        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-7)         "--rm",        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-8)         "-i",        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-9)         "-e", "HUMMINGBOT_API_URL=http://host.docker.internal:8000",        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-10)         "-v", "hummingbot_mcp:/root/.hummingbot_mcp",        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-11)         "hummingbot/hummingbot-mcp:latest"      [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-12)       ]    [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-13)     }  [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-14)   } [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-4-15) }`
    
4.  Verify connection:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-5-1) gemini [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-5-2) /mcp list`
    

✅ You should see `hummingbot` in the output.

### Codex CLI[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#codex-cli "Permanent link")

![codex](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/codex.png)

**Codex CLI** is OpenAI's coding agent for developers.

#### Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#installation_1 "Permanent link")

Install Codex CLI: [Official Guide](https://github.com/openai/codex)

IDE Support

These instructions also work with Codex for VS Code and Cursor. See the [IDE installation guide](https://developers.openai.com/codex/ide)
.

#### Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#configuration_1 "Permanent link")

1.  Navigate to config folder:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-6-1) cd ~/.codex`
    
2.  Edit `config.toml`:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-7-1) nano config.toml`
    
3.  Add MCP server:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-1) [mcp_servers.hummingbot] [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-2) command = "docker" [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-3) args = [   [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-4)   "run",  [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-5)   "--rm",  [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-6)   "-i",  [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-7)   "-e", "HUMMINGBOT_API_URL=http://host.docker.internal:8000",  [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-8)   "-v", "hummingbot_mcp:/root/.hummingbot_mcp",  [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-9)   "hummingbot/hummingbot-mcp:latest" [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-8-10) ]`
    
4.  Verify connection:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-9-1) codex [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-9-2) /mcp`
    

✅ Codex should detect the Hummingbot MCP server.

![codex](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/codex-1.png)

* * *

🖥️ Install via Docker MCP Catalog[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#install-via-docker-mcp-catalog "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**For users who prefer a graphical interface**, Docker Desktop provides a built-in MCP catalog.

### Setup Steps[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#setup-steps "Permanent link")

1.  Open **Docker Desktop** → **MCP Toolkit** → **Catalog**
    
2.  Search for **Hummingbot MCP Server** and click **➕ Install** ![toolkit](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/toolkit.png)
    
3.  Go to the **Configuration** tab ![config](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/config.png)
    
4.  Set environment variables:
    

| Variable | Value |
| --- | --- |
| `HUMMINGBOT_API_URL` | `http://host.docker.internal:8000` |
| `HUMMINGBOT_API_USERNAME` | `admin` (or your username) |
| `HUMMINGBOT_API_PASSWORD` | `admin` (or your password) |

1.  Click the **checkbox** to save ![save](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/save.png)

### Connect a Client[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#connect-a-client "Permanent link")

1.  Go to **MCP Toolkit** → **Clients** ![client](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/client.png)
    
2.  Choose your AI client (Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code)
    
3.  Click **Connect**
    

### Manual Connection (Alternative)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#manual-connection-alternative "Permanent link")

For clients not listed, add manually to your client config:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-2)   "mcp": {    [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-3)     "servers": {      [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-4)       "hummingbot": {        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-5)         "command": "docker",        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-6)         "args": ["mcp", "gateway", "run"],        [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-7)         "type": "stdio"      [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-8)       }    [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-9)     }  [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-10)   } [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#__codelineno-10-11) }`

* * *

✅ Testing Your Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#testing-your-setup "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once configured, test with these commands:

*   "Show me my portfolio balances"
*   "List all active trading bots"
*   "Get the current BTC-USDT price on Binance"
*   "What's my total portfolio value?"

* * *

🐛 Troubleshooting[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#troubleshooting "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Connection Issues[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#connection-issues "Permanent link")

**Problem**: MCP server won't connect

**Solutions**:

*   Ensure Hummingbot API is running: `docker ps | grep hummingbot-api`
*   Use `http://host.docker.internal:8000` not `http://localhost:8000` for local setups
*   Verify Docker is running

### Authentication Errors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#authentication-errors "Permanent link")

**Problem**: "Invalid credentials" or similar errors

**Solutions**:

*   Check username/password in `.env` file
*   Ensure credentials match what you configured during setup
*   Verify API is accessible: `curl -u admin:admin http://localhost:8000/`

### Docker Issues[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#docker-issues "Permanent link")

**Problem**: Docker-related errors

**Solutions**:

*   Restart Docker Desktop
*   Check Docker has network access
*   On Linux, ensure user is in `docker` group

* * *

🚀 Next Steps[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/#next-steps "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After successful installation:

1.  **Explore Tools**: See [MCP Tools](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/tools/)
     for available capabilities
2.  **Try Examples**: Review [Example Workflows](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#example-workflows)
    
3.  **Security**: Read [Security Guidelines](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#security-considerations)
    

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

.
