# 2.0.0 - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#hummingbot-v200-release-notes)

Hummingbot v2.0.0 Release Notes[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#hummingbot-v200-release-notes "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================================

_Released on July 3, 2024_

Introduction[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#introduction "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hummingbot 2.0 introduces a major update that revolutionizes the bot trading experience with the new Dashboard GUI. This upgrade transitions away from the traditional Hummingbot command-line interface (CLI), offering a more intuitive and visually appealing interface. Now, you can generate and backtest strategies before deploying them as Hummingbot instances, making Hummingbot 2.0 a powerful tool for both novice and experienced Hummingbot users.

| Repository | Description | GitHub Release | DockerHub Release |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Hummingbot](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot) | Core Client | [`v2.0.0`](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/releases/tag/v2.0.0) | [`version-2.0.0`](https://hub.docker.com/r/hummingbot/hummingbot/tags?name=version-2.0.0) |
| [Dashboard](https://github.com/hummingbot/dashboard) | Hummingbot Frontend | [`v2.0.0`](https://github.com/hummingbot/dashboard/releases/tag/v2.0.0) | [`version-2.0.0`](https://hub.docker.com/r/hummingbot/dashboard/tags?name=version-2.0.0) |
| [Backend-API](https://github.com/hummingbot/backend-api) | Hummingbot Backend | [`v2.0.0`](https://github.com/hummingbot/backend-api/releases/tag/v2.0.0) | [`version-2.0.0`](https://hub.docker.com/r/hummingbot/backend-api/tags?name=version-2.0.0) |
| [Gateway](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway) | DEX Middleware | [`v2.0.0`](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/releases/tag/v2.0.0) | [`version-2.0.0`](https://hub.docker.com/r/hummingbot/gateway/tags?name=version-2.0.0) |
| [Deploy](https://github.com/hummingbot/deploy) | Deployment Repo |     |     |

How to Update[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#how-to-update "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Docker[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#docker "Permanent link")

Re-run the [Deploy](https://github.com/hummingbot/deploy)
 setup script:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#__codelineno-0-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/deploy.git [](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#__codelineno-0-2) cd deploy [](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#__codelineno-0-3) bash setup.sh`

For individual images, exit running containers, pull the latest images, and restart:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#__codelineno-1-1) docker compose down [](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#__codelineno-1-2) docker pull hummingbot/hummingbot:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#__codelineno-1-3) docker compose up -d`

### Source[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#source "Permanent link")

Update the branches of each repo to this release by running:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#__codelineno-2-1) git pull origin master`

Hummingbot 2.0 Highlights[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#hummingbot-20-highlights "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Deploy Repo[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#deploy-repo "Permanent link")

[Hummingbot Deploy](https://github.com/hummingbot/deploy)
 is a dedicated repo that allows users to quickly deploy Hummingbot using the Dashboard as the front end UI. The compose file spins up containers for the [Dashboard](https://github.com/hummingbot/dashboard)
, [Backend-API](https://github.com/hummingbot/backend-api)
 as well as the **Hummingbot Broker**.

### Dashboard[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#dashboard "Permanent link")

For more info check out the [Hummingbot 2.0 Quickstart Guide](https://hummingbot.org/blog/hummingbot-dashboard-quickstart-guide/)
. Some highlights:

*   Configure Strategy V2 controllers with a visual editor:

[![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/pmm_simple.png)](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/pmm_simple.png)

*   Backtest your configurations before saving them to Backend-API

[![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/backtesting.png)](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/backtesting.png)

*   Deploy your strategy configurations using a few clicks:

[![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/deploy.png)](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/deploy.png)

*   View real-time status of all instances and their logs:

[![](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/instances.png)](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/instances.png)

New XRPL Connector (Python)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#new-xrpl-connector-python "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This PR enhances the current **XRPL** API connector by transitioning it to a fully Python-based implementation, eliminating the need for the **Hummingbot Gateway**. Users can now utilize the XRPL connector similarly to a standard CEX connector by executing the `connect XRPL` command. This upgrade significantly boosts the connector's performance and enhances stability for user trading activities.

**Pull Request:** [#7051](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7051)

**Thanks to [mlguys](https://github.com/mlguys)
 for this contribution! 🙏**

New Balancer Connector[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#new-balancer-connector "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Pull Request:** [#280](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/pull/280)
 - Added **Balancer** connector

**Thanks to [vic-en](https://github.com/vic-en)
 for this contribution! 🙏**

Other Updates[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#other-updates "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.0.0/#hummingbot "Permanent link")

     for this fix! 🙏**

     for this contribution! 🙏**
*   [#7069](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7069)
     - Fixed conversion of staking rewards to normal token (ex. ETH.F -> ETH) **Thanks to [yancong001](https://github.com/yancong001)
     for this fix! 🙏**

# Overview - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#hummingbot-api)

Hummingbot API[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#hummingbot-api "Permanent link")

==========================================================================================

**The central hub for running Hummingbot trading bots** - now with AI assistant integration via MCP (Model Context Protocol).

Repository Update

The `backend-api` has been **renamed to `hummingbot-api`**, marking a major revamp of the codebase with improvements in architecture, modularity, and developer experience.

A comprehensive RESTful API framework for managing trading operations across multiple exchanges. The Hummingbot API provides a centralized platform to aggregate all your trading functionalities, from basic account management to sophisticated automated trading strategies.

**GitHub Repository**: [github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api)

Getting Started[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#getting-started "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **[Installation Guide](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/)
    ** - Complete installation instructions for Docker and source installation
*   **[Developer Guide](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/quickstart/)
    ** - Learn how to:
    *   Add exchange credentials
    *   View portfolio balances
    *   Place your first market order

The guides include Docker setup and Python API client examples to get you trading in minutes.

What is Hummingbot API?[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#what-is-hummingbot-api "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Hummingbot API is designed to be your central hub for trading operations, offering:

*   **🤖 AI Assistant Integration**: Control your trading with natural language via MCP (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini)
*   **Multi-Exchange Account Management**: Create and manage multiple trading accounts across different exchanges
*   **Portfolio Monitoring**: Real-time balance tracking and portfolio distribution analysis
*   **Trade Execution**: Execute trades, manage orders, and monitor positions across all your accounts
*   **Automated Trading**: Deploy and control Hummingbot instances with automated strategies
*   **Strategy Management**: Add, configure, and manage trading strategies in real-time
*   **Complete Flexibility**: Build any trading product on top of this robust API framework

Whether you're building a trading dashboard, implementing algorithmic strategies, or creating a comprehensive trading platform, the Hummingbot API provides all the tools you need.

Architecture[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#architecture "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    graph TB
        subgraph "Clients"
            direction LR
            CUSTOM[Custom Apps]
            CONDOR[Condor<br/>Telegram Bot]
            AI[AI Agents]
        end
    
        subgraph "Hummingbot API"
            direction LR
            API["FastAPI<br/>Server<br/>"]
            PG[(PostgreSQL<br/>Database)]
            MQTT[EMQX<br/>Message Broker]
        end
    
        subgraph "Bots"
            BOTS[Hummingbot<br/>Instances]
        end
    
        subgraph "Exchanges"
            EX[Binance, OKX,<br/>Hyperliquid, etc.]
        end
    
        %% Client connections using API Client
        CONDOR -->|Hummingbot API Client| API
    
        %% Bot connections
        BOTS <-->|Commands & Updates| MQTT
    
        %% Exchange connections
        BOTS <-->|Trade & Data| EX
        API <-->|Trade & Data| EX
    
        %% Apply theme colors
        classDef clientStyle stroke:#5FFFD7,stroke-width:3px
        classDef apiStyle stroke:#00B1BB,stroke-width:3px
        classDef botsStyle stroke:#E549FF,stroke-width:3px
    
        class CONDOR clientStyle
        class API,PG,MQTT apiStyle
        class BOTS botsStyle

### Key Components[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#key-components "Permanent link")

*   **Server Infrastructure**:
    *   FastAPI server providing RESTful API with HTTP Basic Authentication
    *   PostgreSQL database for storing trading data, account info, and historical performance
    *   EMQX message broker for real-time communication with bot instances
*   **Exchange Connectors**: Built-in connectors for major CEXs and DEXs - trading and data fetching is accessible directly through the Hummingbot API or via bots that it deploys
*   **Bot Instances**: Individual Hummingbot containers connected to different exchanges
*   **Docker Management**: Orchestrates multiple Hummingbot container instances

🎯 Ways to Interact with Hummingbot API[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#ways-to-interact-with-hummingbot-api "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For new users, we strongly recommend using **Condor** for a mobile-friendly Telegram experience. For developers and advanced users, the **Swagger UI** and **MCP** provide more direct access to the API.

### 1\. 📱 Condor - Telegram Interface (Recommended for New Users)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#1-condor-telegram-interface-recommended-for-new-users "Permanent link")

**Mobile and desktop control via Telegram**

*   **Best for**: Users who want to monitor and control bots on the go
*   **Advantages**:
*   Access from any device with Telegram
*   Real-time notifications and alerts
*   Quick commands for common operations
*   No web browser required
*   **Setup**: See [Condor Installation](https://hummingbot.org/condor/installation/)
    
*   **Access**: Your personal Telegram bot

### 2\. 🔧 Swagger UI - API Documentation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#2-swagger-ui-api-documentation "Permanent link")

**Interactive REST API documentation and testing**

*   **Best for**: Developers and power users who want full control
*   **Advantages**:
*   Complete API access - all endpoints available
*   Direct endpoint testing
*   Integration development
*   No additional setup required
*   **Setup**: Automatically available after running setup
*   **Access**: [http://localhost:8000/docs](http://localhost:8000/docs)
    

### 3\. 🤖 MCP - AI Assistant[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#3-mcp-ai-assistant "Permanent link")

**Natural language trading commands through Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini**

*   **Best for**: Users who prefer conversational interaction
*   **Advantages**:
*   Natural language commands
*   Full access to all API features
*   Contextual help and explanations
*   Complex multi-step operations made simple
*   Progressive credential setup with `setup_connector` tool
*   **Setup**: See [MCP Installation Guide](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/)
    
*   **Examples**:
*   First-time: "Set up my Solana wallet" → Guides through credential setup
*   Trading: "What's the price to swap 0.01 SOL for USDC? Execute the trade"

Use Cases[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#use-cases "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Hummingbot API enables various trading applications:

*   **Custom OEMS**: Build your own trading order execution management system spanning multiple exchanges
*   **Trading Dashboards**: Build custom chat, web, and mobile interfaces for controlling bots
*   **AI-Powered Trading**: Integrate with LLMs for agentic trading workflows
*   **Risk Management Tools**: Build systems for monitoring and managing trading operations
*   **Market Data Feeds**: Create real-time price and historical candles feeds for use with different applications

API Endpoints[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#api-endpoints "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Hummingbot API is organized into functional routers covering:

*   🐳 **Docker Management** - Container lifecycle and orchestration
*   💳 **Account Management** - Multi-exchange account configuration
*   🔌 **Connector Discovery** - Exchange connector information
*   📊 **Portfolio Management** - Real-time portfolio tracking and analytics
*   💹 **Trading Operations** - Order execution and position management
*   🤖 **Bot Orchestration** - Deploy and manage trading bots
*   📋 **Strategy Management** - Controllers and scripts
*   📊 **Market Data** - Real-time and historical market data
*   🔄 **Backtesting** - Strategy testing with historical data
*   📈 **Archived Bot Analytics** - Historical bot performance analysis
*   🌐 **Gateway** - Gateway container and DEX infrastructure management
*   🔄 **Gateway Swaps** - DEX swap execution and monitoring
*   💧 **Gateway CLMM** - Concentrated liquidity position management

For detailed endpoint documentation, see the **[API Routers Guide](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/routers/)
**.

System Dependencies[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#system-dependencies "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The platform includes these essential services:

### PostgreSQL Database[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#postgresql-database "Permanent link")

Stores all trading data including:

*   Orders and trade history
*   Account states and balances
*   Positions and funding payments
*   Performance metrics

**Note:** The database is automatically initialized using environment variables. The included `init-db.sql` serves as a safety net.

### EMQX Message Broker[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#emqx-message-broker "Permanent link")

Enables real-time communication with trading bots:

*   Receives live updates from running bots
*   Sends commands to control bot execution
*   Handles real-time data streaming

Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#configuration "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Environment Variables[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#environment-variables "Permanent link")

Key configuration options available in `.env`:

*   **CONFIG\_PASSWORD**: Encrypts API keys and credentials
*   **USERNAME/PASSWORD**: API authentication credentials
*   **BROKER\_HOST/PORT**: EMQX message broker settings
*   **DATABASE\_URL**: PostgreSQL connection string
*   **ACCOUNT\_UPDATE\_INTERVAL**: Balance update frequency (minutes)
*   **AWS\_API\_KEY/AWS\_SECRET\_KEY**: S3 archiving (optional)
*   **BANNED\_TOKENS**: Comma-separated list of tokens to exclude
*   **LOGFIRE\_TOKEN**: Observability and monitoring (production)

### Bot Instance Structure[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#bot-instance-structure "Permanent link")

Each bot maintains its own isolated environment:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-0-1) bots/instances/hummingbot-{name}/ [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-0-2) ├── conf/           # Configuration files [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-0-3) ├── data/           # Bot databases and state [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-0-4) └── logs/           # Execution logs`

Authentication[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#authentication "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The API uses HTTP Basic Authentication:

*   Configure username and password during setup
*   Include credentials in the Authorization header for all requests
*   Example: `Authorization: Basic <base64-encoded-credentials>`

API Client[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#api-client "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

A modern, asynchronous Python client is available for interacting with the Hummingbot API. This client is used by [Condor](https://hummingbot.org/condor/)
 and other interfaces as the interface layer for all API communications.

*   **GitHub**: [hummingbot-api-client](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api-client)
    
*   **PyPI**: [pypi.org/project/hummingbot-api-client](https://pypi.org/project/hummingbot-api-client/)
    

### Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#installation "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-1-1) pip install hummingbot-api-client`

### Usage Example[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#usage-example "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-1) from hummingbot_api_client import HummingbotAPIClient [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-3) # Initialize client [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-4) client = HummingbotAPIClient(     [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-5)     base_url="http://localhost:8000",    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-6)     username="your-username",    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-7)     password="your-password" [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-8) ) [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-9)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-10) # Get portfolio data [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-11) portfolio = await client.get_portfolio() [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-12)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-13) # Execute a trade [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-14) order = await client.create_order(     [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-15)     connector="binance",    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-16)     trading_pair="BTC-USDT",    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-17)     order_type="limit",    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-18)     side="buy",    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-19)     amount=0.001,    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-20)     price=50000 [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#__codelineno-2-21) )`

Related Resources[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/#related-resources "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Condor](https://hummingbot.org/condor/)
     - Telegram interface for Hummingbot API
*   [API Client Documentation](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api-client)
     - Python client library
*   [Hummingbot Client](https://hummingbot.org/client/)
     - Core trading engine

Was this page helpful?

.

# 2.1.0 - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#hummingbot-v210-release-notes)

Hummingbot v2.1.0 Release Notes[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#hummingbot-v210-release-notes "Permanent link")

===============================================================================================================================

_Released on October 28, 2024_

Introduction[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#introduction "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Repository | Description | GitHub Release | DockerHub Release |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Hummingbot](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot) | Core Client | [`v2.1.0`](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/releases/tag/v2.1.0) | [`version-2.1.0`](https://hub.docker.com/r/hummingbot/hummingbot/tags?name=version-2.1.0) |
| [Dashboard](https://github.com/hummingbot/dashboard) | Hummingbot Frontend | [`v2.1.0`](https://github.com/hummingbot/dashboard/releases/tag/v2.1.0) | [`version-2.1.0`](https://hub.docker.com/r/hummingbot/dashboard/tags?name=version-2.1.0) |
| [Backend-API](https://github.com/hummingbot/backend-api) | Hummingbot Backend | [`v2.1.0`](https://github.com/hummingbot/backend-api/releases/tag/v2.1.0) | [`version-2.1.0`](https://hub.docker.com/r/hummingbot/backend-api/tags?name=version-2.1.0) |
| [Gateway](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway) | DEX Middleware | [`v2.1.0`](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/releases/tag/v2.1.0) | [`version-2.1.0`](https://hub.docker.com/r/hummingbot/gateway/tags?name=version-2.1.0) |
| [Deploy](https://github.com/hummingbot/deploy) | Deployment Repo |     |     |

How to Update[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#how-to-update "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Docker[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#docker "Permanent link")

Re-run the [Deploy](https://github.com/hummingbot/deploy)
 setup script:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#__codelineno-0-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/deploy.git [](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#__codelineno-0-2) cd deploy [](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#__codelineno-0-3) bash setup.sh`

For individual images, exit running containers, pull the latest images, and restart:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#__codelineno-1-1) docker compose down [](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#__codelineno-1-2) docker pull hummingbot/hummingbot:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#__codelineno-1-3) docker compose up -d`

### Source[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#source "Permanent link")

Update the branches of each repo to this release by running:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#__codelineno-2-1) git pull origin master`

Hummingbot 2.1.0 Highlights[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#hummingbot-210-highlights "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### CEX Connector Improvements[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#cex-connector-improvements "Permanent link")

*   The `bybit_perpetual` connector has been upgraded to use the latest v5 API version, aligning it with the spot connector in [#7196](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7196)
    . **Thanks to [tomasgaudino](https://github.com/tomasgaudino)
     for this fix! 🙏**
    
*   Enhanced `coinbase_advanced_trade` connector with improved reliability and performance in [#7220](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7220)
    . **Thanks to [isreallee82](https://github.com/isreallee82)
     for this contribution! 🙏**
    

### V2 Framework Enhancements[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#v2-framework-enhancements "Permanent link")

Several core improvements have been made to the V2 Framework:

*   Enhanced market data provider for better price feed accuracy [#7214](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7214)
    
*   Improved candle feed boundaries handling for more reliable data fetching [#7224](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7224)
     **Thanks to [tomasgaudino](https://github.com/tomasgaudino)
     for this fix! 🙏**
*   Upgraded PositionExecutor component with increased max retries for better trade execution [#7244](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7244)
    
*   Improved stop loss computation [#7200](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7200)
    

### Updated Dexalot Connector[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#updated-dexalot-connector "Permanent link")

This release introduces a fully Python-based implementation of the [Dexalot](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/dexalot/)
 connector, removing the Gateway dependency in [#7219](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7219)
. Users can now:

*   Connect directly using `connect dexalot` command
*   Experience improved performance and stability
*   Trade more efficiently with reduced latency

### New DEX Connectors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#new-dex-connectors "Permanent link")

*   We added support for ETCSwap on Ethereum Classic, expanding DEX trading options for users in [#340](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/pull/340)
    .
    
*   Based on approval from community voting, the Tegro exchange connector was added in [#7148](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7148)
    .
    

Other Updates[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#other-updates "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Hummingbot Repository[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#hummingbot-repository "Permanent link")

*   [#7134](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7134)
     - Added server time sync for [Gate.io](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/gate-io/)
     **Thanks to [Jbekker](https://github.com/Jbekker)
     for this fix! 🙏**
*   [#7255](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7255)
     - Fixed refreshing of the listenkey for MEXC **Thanks to [Jbekker](https://github.com/Jbekker)
     for this fix! 🙏**
*   [#7261](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7261)
     - Fixed issue with pandas:`The frame.append method is deprecated` **Thanks to [isreallee82](https://github.com/isreallee82)
     for this fix! 🙏**
*   [#7197](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/pull/7197)
     - Removed connectors that didn't pass the prior epoch's polls

### Gateway Repository[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#gateway-repository "Permanent link")

*   [#362](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/pull/362)
     - Removed connectors that didn't pass the prior epoch's polls

### Dashboard Repository[¶](https://hummingbot.org/release-notes/2.1.0/#dashboard-repository "Permanent link")

*   [#181](https://github.com/hummingbot/dashboard/pull/181)
     - Fixed wrong parameter name **Thanks to [mlguys](https://github.com/mlguys)
     for this fix! 🙏**
*   [#183](https://github.com/hummingbot/dashboard/pull/183)
     - Added error handling for dates

# Overview - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#hummingbot-mcp-server)

Hummingbot MCP Server[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#hummingbot-mcp-server "Permanent link")

=============================================================================================

The Hummingbot Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server enables AI assistants like Claude and Gemini to interact with Hummingbot for automated cryptocurrency trading across multiple exchanges.

**GitHub Repository**: [github.com/hummingbot/mcp](https://github.com/hummingbot/mcp)

Overview[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#overview "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------

The MCP Server acts as a bridge between AI language models and the Hummingbot trading platform, enabling programmatic interaction with cryptocurrency trading infrastructure. This allows AI assistants to manage trading operations, analyze portfolios, and execute strategies on behalf of users.

What is Model Context Protocol (MCP)?[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#what-is-model-context-protocol-mcp "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Model Context Protocol is an open standard that enables AI assistants to securely interact with external systems and data sources. In the context of Hummingbot, MCP allows AI models to:

*   Access real-time trading data
*   Execute trading operations
*   Manage portfolios across multiple exchanges
*   Analyze market conditions
*   Monitor trading performance

Key Features[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#key-features "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Hummingbot MCP Server provides the following capabilities:

### 🏦 Account Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#account-management "Permanent link")

*   Connect and manage multiple exchange accounts
*   Retrieve account information and credentials
*   Monitor account status across exchanges

### 💰 Portfolio Tracking[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#portfolio-tracking "Permanent link")

*   View real-time balance across all connected exchanges
*   Track portfolio performance and P&L
*   Analyze asset distribution and allocation

### 📊 Order Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#order-management "Permanent link")

*   Place buy and sell orders on any supported exchange
*   Cancel existing orders
*   Monitor order status and execution
*   View order history and fills

### 📈 Position Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#position-management "Permanent link")

*   Track open positions for derivatives trading
*   Monitor position sizes and unrealized P&L
*   Close positions programmatically

### 🔍 Market Data Access[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#market-data-access "Permanent link")

*   Retrieve real-time price data
*   Access order book information
*   Get historical pricing data
*   Monitor funding rates for perpetual contracts

### 📉 Funding Rates Monitoring[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#funding-rates-monitoring "Permanent link")

*   Track funding rates across perpetual exchanges
*   Identify arbitrage opportunities
*   Monitor funding rate changes over time

### 🌐 Gateway & DEX Trading[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#gateway-dex-trading "Permanent link")

*   Manage Gateway container lifecycle (start, stop, restart)
*   Configure chains, networks, tokens, and wallets
*   Execute swaps on DEX routers (Jupiter, 0x)
*   Discover and analyze CLMM (concentrated liquidity) pools
*   Open and manage liquidity positions
*   Collect fees from active positions

### 🔄 Multiple Server Support[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#multiple-server-support "Permanent link")

*   Connect to multiple Hummingbot API instances simultaneously
*   Manage trading across different environments (production, staging, dev)
*   Switch between servers seamlessly in conversation
*   Unified interface for multi-instance portfolio management

Architecture[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#architecture "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

    graph TB
        subgraph "AI Assistants"
            CLAUDE[Claude CLI]
            GEMINI[Gemini CLI]
        end
    
        subgraph "MCP Server"
            MCP[Hummingbot<br/>MCP Server]
        end
    
        subgraph "Hummingbot Infrastructure" 
            API[Hummingbot API<br/>Server]
            BOTS[Trading Bots]
        end
    
        subgraph "Exchanges"
            EX[Binance, OKX,<br/>Hyperliquid, etc.]
        end
    
        %% AI to MCP connections
        CLAUDE -->|MCP Protocol| MCP
        GEMINI -->|MCP Protocol| MCP
    
        %% MCP to Hummingbot API
        MCP -->|REST API| API
    
        %% API to infrastructure
        API <--> BOTS
        BOTS <--> EX
        API <--> EX
    
        %% Styling
        classDef aiStyle stroke:#5FFFD7,stroke-width:3px
        classDef mcpStyle stroke:#E549FF,stroke-width:3px  
        classDef hbStyle stroke:#00B1BB,stroke-width:3px
    
        class CLAUDE,GEMINI aiStyle
        class MCP mcpStyle
        class API,BOTS hbStyle

Getting Started[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#getting-started "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ready to build AI trading agents with Hummingbot? Follow these steps:

1.  **[Install the MCP Server](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/)
    ** - Set up development or production environment
2.  **[Explore Available Tools](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/tools/)
    ** - Learn about trading and portfolio management capabilities
3.  **Configure your AI assistant** - Connect Claude, Gemini, or other MCP-compatible AI
4.  **Start trading conversations** - Let AI manage your portfolio automatically

Quick Overview[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#quick-overview "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The MCP server provides comprehensive trading capabilities through these tool categories:

### 🏦 **Account & Portfolio Management**[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#account-portfolio-management "Permanent link")

Monitor balances, track performance, and analyze portfolio allocation across all connected exchanges.

### 📊 **Order & Position Management**[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#order-position-management "Permanent link")

Execute trades, manage orders, and control positions programmatically with AI oversight.

### 📈 **Market Data & Analysis**[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#market-data-analysis "Permanent link")

Access real-time prices, funding rates, and order book data for informed decision making.

Usage with AI Assistants[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#usage-with-ai-assistants "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The Hummingbot MCP Server works with various AI assistants. See the **[Installation Guide](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/)
** for detailed setup instructions for:

*   **Claude Code** (CLI) - Recommended for developers
*   **Claude Desktop** - GUI application
*   **Gemini CLI** - Google's AI assistant
*   **Codex CLI** - OpenAI's coding assistant

### Example Trading Conversation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#example-trading-conversation "Permanent link")

Once configured, you can interact naturally with your AI assistant:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-0-1) You: Show me my portfolio balances across all exchanges [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-0-2) AI Assistant: I'll check your portfolio balances using the Hummingbot MCP server...`

Example Workflows[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#example-workflows "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Portfolio Analysis[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#portfolio-analysis "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-1-1) AI: "What's my current portfolio worth and how is it distributed?" [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-1-2) MCP: Retrieves balances across all exchanges and calculates total value [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-1-3) AI: Provides detailed breakdown with recommendations`

### Automated Trading[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#automated-trading "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-2-1) AI: "Buy 0.1 BTC when the price drops below $45,000" [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-2-2) MCP: Monitors prices and executes order when conditions are met [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-2-3) AI: Confirms execution and provides trade summary`

### Risk Management[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#risk-management "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-3-1) AI: "Close all positions with unrealized losses over 5%" [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-3-2) MCP: Analyzes open positions and closes those meeting criteria [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-3-3) AI: Reports actions taken and updated portfolio status`

Related Videos[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#related-videos "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Building AI Crypto Trading Agents with Claude Code & Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#building-ai-crypto-trading-agents-with-claude-code-hummingbot "Permanent link")

### AMA: Hummingbot API + MCP[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#ama-hummingbot-api-mcp "Permanent link")

### Introduction to Hummingbot MCP Server[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#introduction-to-hummingbot-mcp-server "Permanent link")

Security Considerations[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#security-considerations "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **API Credentials**: Store credentials securely and never commit them to version control
*   **Network Access**: Ensure MCP server can only be accessed by authorized AI assistants
*   **Trading Limits**: Set appropriate position sizes and risk limits in your strategies
*   **Monitoring**: Regularly monitor AI trading activity and set up alerts for unusual behavior

Development[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#development "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Contributing[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#contributing "Permanent link")

The Hummingbot MCP Server is open source. Contributions are welcome!

1.  Fork the repository
2.  Create a feature branch
3.  Make your changes
4.  Add tests for new functionality
5.  Submit a pull request

### Custom Tools[¶](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#custom-tools "Permanent link")

You can extend the MCP server by adding custom tools:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-4-1) @mcp.tool() [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-4-2) async def my_custom_tool(parameter: str) -> str:     [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-4-3)     """Custom tool description"""    [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-4-4)     # Your implementation here    [](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/#__codelineno-4-5)     return result`

.

# Overview - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#what-is-gateway)

Overview
========

What is Gateway?[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#what-is-gateway "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Hummingbot Gateway is a Typescript-based API server that standardizes interactions with blockchain networks and decentralized exchanges (DEXs). It acts as a middleware layer, providing a unified interface for performing actions like checking balances, executing trades, and managing wallets across different protocols.

Gateway is a companion service to the Python-based [Hummingbot client](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot)
, exposing standardized REST API endpoints for trading and liquidity-related functionality on DEXs. This enables Hummingbot to run strategies that operate across both centralized (CEX) and decentralized exchanges seamlessly.

In This Section[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#in-this-section "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **[Installation & Setup](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/)
    **: Complete installation guide for source and Docker
*   **[Configuration](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/configuration/)
    **: How to configure chains, connectors, and settings
*   **[Commands](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/commands/)
    **: Comprehensive reference of all Gateway-related Hummingbot commands
*   **[Chains](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/)
    **: Detailed information about supported chains
*   **[DEX Connectors](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/connectors/)
    **: Guide to all supported DEX in Gateway
*   **[Strategies & Scripts](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/strategies/)
    **: Using Gateway with Hummingbot strategies

Key Features[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#key-features "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Standardized REST API**: Consistent endpoints for interacting with blockchains (Ethereum, Solana) and DEXs
*   **DEX SDK Integration**: Interfaces with TypeScript/JavaScript DEX SDKs to provide standardized endpoints across different protocols
*   **Connector Sub-Types**: Router (DEX aggregators), AMM (V2-style pools), and CLMM (V3-style concentrated liquidity)
*   **Modular Architecture**: Clear separation of concerns with distinct modules for chains, connectors, configuration, and wallet management
*   **Hardware Wallet Support**: Built-in support for hardware wallets and encrypted storage for regular wallets
*   **Extensible**: Easily extended with new chains and connectors

### Connector Schemas[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#connector-schemas "Permanent link")

*   **Router**: DEX aggregators that find optimal swap routes across multiple liquidity sources, maximizing execution quality by splitting trades across multiple pools and protocols
*   **AMM** (Automated Market Maker): Traditional V2-style constant product pools using the x\*y=k formula, where liquidity is distributed uniformly across the entire price range, making it simpler but less capital efficient
*   **CLMM** (Concentrated Liquidity Market Maker): V3-style pools that allow liquidity providers to concentrate their capital within custom price ranges, dramatically improving capital efficiency and enabling better pricing for traders

For detailed implementation guides and examples for each schema, see [DEX Connectors](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/connectors/)
.

Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#installation "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway can be installed alongside [Hummingbot](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot)
 to enable trading on AMM DEXs, or as a standalone API server. For detailed installation instructions, see [Installation & Setup](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/)
.

When running Gateway in `DEV` mode, access the interactive Swagger API documentation at: [http://localhost:15888/docs](http://localhost:15888/docs)

Architecture[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#architecture "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway follows a modular architecture with clear separation of concerns:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-1) /src [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-2) ├── chains/               # Blockchain-specific implementations [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-3) │   └── {chain}/         # Each blockchain (ethereum, solana, etc.) [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-4) ├── connectors/           # DEX-specific implementations [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-5) │   ├── {dex}/           # Each DEX connector directory [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-6) │   │   ├── router-routes/   # DEX aggregator operations [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-7) │   │   ├── amm-routes/      # AMM pool operations [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-8) │   │   └── clmm-routes/     # Concentrated liquidity operations [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-9) ├── services/             # Core services (config, logging, tokens) [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-10) ├── schemas/              # API request/response schemas [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-11) ├── templates/            # Base classes and interfaces for connectors [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-12) ├── tokens/               # Token lists and metadata [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-13) ├── pools/                # Liquidity pool configurations [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#__codelineno-0-14) └── wallet/               # Wallet management`

Governance and Maintenance[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#governance-and-maintenance "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Like other connectors, Gateway DEX connectors require ongoing maintenance: fixing bugs, addressing user issues, and keeping up with updates to both the exchange/blockchain API as well as improvements to the Hummingbot connector standard.

Hummingbot Foundation maintains certain reference connectors as the standard and utilizes a community-based maintenance process. We assign [Bounties](https://hummingbot.org/bounties/)
 to community developers to upgrade and fix bugs for each exchange's connectors in the codebase.

Each quarter, [Exchange Connector Polls](https://hummingbot.org/about/polls/)
 allocates HBOT bounties toward the top CEX connectors and determines which exchange connectors should be included in the codebase going forward. This process also determines which blockchains and networks that Gateway supports.

See the **Connector Pots** tab in [HBOT Tracker](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1UNAumPMnXfsghAAXrfKkPGRH9QlC8k7Cu1FGQVL1t0M/edit?usp=sharing)
 for the current allocations for each exchange.

Contributing[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#contributing "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway is part of the open source Hummingbot project. Ways to contribute:

*   **Build new connectors**: See [DEX Connectors](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/)
     for implementation guide
*   **File issues**: Report bugs at [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/issues)
    
*   **Submit pull requests**: Contribute code at [GitHub](https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway/pulls)
    
*   **Edit documentation**: Improve docs at [GitHub](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/)
    
*   **Vote in polls**: Participate in [polls](https://snapshot.org/#/hbot.eth)
     to vote on which DEXs are supported

History[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/#history "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------

For more information about Gateway's history and architecture decisions, see:

*   [Hummingbot Gateway V2 Architecture - Part 1](https://hummingbot.org/blog/hummingbot-gateway-architecture---part-1/)
    
*   [Hummingbot Gateway V2 Architecture - Part 2](https://hummingbot.org/blog/hummingbot-gateway-architecture---part-2/)

# Chains - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#ethereum)

Chains
======

Gateway provides standardized access to multiple blockchain networks, enabling wallet management, transaction execution, and node RPC interactions. Each chain integration is customized to handle the specific requirements and features of that blockchain.

Gateway currently supports the following blockchain architectures:

Ethereum[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#ethereum "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway's Ethereum integration supports the Ethereum mainnet and all EVM-compatible Layer 1 and Layer 2 blockchains as **networks**. These networks share the same basic architecture, allowing for unified handling of wallets, transactions, and smart contract interactions.

| Network | Description | Chain ID | Native Token |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| mainnet | Ethereum Mainnet | 1   | ETH |
| arbitrum | Arbitrum One | 42161 | ETH |
| optimism | Optimism | 10  | ETH |
| base | Base | 8453 | ETH |
| polygon | Polygon | 137 | MATIC |
| bsc | BNB Chain | 56  | BNB |
| avalanche | Avalanche C-Chain | 43114 | AVAX |
| celo | Celo | 42220 | CELO |
| sepolia | Sepolia (Testnet) | 11155111 | ETH |

### Chain Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#chain-configuration "Permanent link")

Each chain and network can be configured in Gateway through YAML configuration files:

*   Template: `/src/templates/chains/ethereum.yml`
*   User Configs Location: `/conf/chains/ethereum.yml`

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-0-1) defaultNetwork: mainnet [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-0-2) defaultWallet: '<ethereum-wallet-address>'`

### Network Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#network-configuration "Permanent link")

*   Template: `/src/templates/chains/ethereum.yml`
*   User Configs : `/conf/chains/ethereum/mainnet.yml`

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-1-1) chainID: 1 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-1-2) nodeURL: https://eth.llamarpc.com [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-1-3) nativeCurrencySymbol: ETH [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-1-4) minGasPrice: 0.1`

### API Endpoints[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#api-endpoints "Permanent link")

All EVM chains share the same API structure:

*   `GET /chains/ethereum/status` - Chain connection and block status
*   `GET /chains/ethereum/tokens` - Token information
*   `POST /chains/ethereum/balances` - Wallet balances
*   `POST /chains/ethereum/allowances` - Token allowances
*   `POST /chains/ethereum/approve` - Approve token spending
*   `POST /chains/ethereum/wrap` - Wrap native token
*   `POST /chains/ethereum/unwrap` - Unwrap to native token
*   `POST /chains/ethereum/poll` - Poll transaction status
*   `POST /chains/ethereum/estimate-gas` - Estimate transaction gas

Solana[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#solana "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway's Solana integration provides access to the Solana blockchain and other networks that utilize the Solana Virtual Machine.

| Network | Description | Native Token |
| --- | --- | --- |
| mainnet-beta | Solana Mainnet | SOL |
| devnet | Solana Devnet (Testnet) | SOL |

### Chain Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#chain-configuration_1 "Permanent link")

Each chain and network can be configured in Gateway through YAML configuration files:

*   Template: `/src/templates/chains/solana.yml`
*   User Configs Location: `/conf/chains/solana.yml`

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-2-1) defaultNetwork: mainnet-beta [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-2-2) defaultWallet: '<solana-wallet-address>'`

### Network Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#network-configuration_1 "Permanent link")

*   Template: `/src/templates/chains/solana.yml`
*   User Configs: `/conf/chains/solana/mainnet-beta.yml`

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-3-1) nodeURL: "https://api.mainnet-beta.solana.com" [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-3-2) commitment: "confirmed" [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-3-3) skipPreflight: false [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-3-4) preflightCommitment: "confirmed" [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-3-5) maxFee: 0.01 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-3-6) priorityFee: 0.00001`

### API Endpoints[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#api-endpoints_1 "Permanent link")

All Solana networks share the same API structure:

*   `GET /chains/solana/status` - Chain connection and slot status
*   `GET /chains/solana/tokens` - SPL token information
*   `POST /chains/solana/balances` - Wallet SOL and token balances
*   `POST /chains/solana/poll` - Poll transaction status
*   `POST /chains/solana/estimate-gas` - Estimate transaction fees

Chain Schema[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#chain-schema "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway implements a standardized schema for chain operations across all supported blockchains. These schemas define the structure of requests and responses for common blockchain operations.

### Status Check[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#status-check "Permanent link")

Returns chain connection status and current block/slot information.

**Request Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-4-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-4-2)   "network": "string (optional)" // Network identifier (e.g., "mainnet", "mainnet-beta") [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-4-3) }`

**Response Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-5-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-5-2)   "chain": "string",           // Chain name (e.g., "ethereum", "solana")  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-5-3)   "network": "string",         // Network identifier  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-5-4)   "rpcUrl": "string",          // Current RPC endpoint  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-5-5)   "currentBlockNumber": 12345, // Current block number or slot  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-5-6)   "nativeCurrency": "string"   // Native token symbol (e.g., "ETH", "SOL") [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-5-7) }`

### Token Information[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#token-information "Permanent link")

Retrieves token metadata including addresses and decimals.

**Request Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-6-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-6-2)   "network": "string (optional)",           // Network identifier  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-6-3)   "tokenSymbols": "string | string[] (optional)" // Single symbol or array of symbols/addresses [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-6-4) }`

**Response Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-2)   "tokens": [    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-3)     {      [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-4)       "symbol": "string",   // Token symbol      [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-5)       "address": "string",  // Token contract address      [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-6)       "decimals": 6,        // Token decimals      [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-7)       "name": "string"      // Token full name    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-8)     }  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-9)   ] [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-7-10) }`

### Balance Query[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#balance-query "Permanent link")

Fetches wallet balances for native and specified tokens.

**Request Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-8-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-8-2)   "network": "string (optional)",      // Network identifier  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-8-3)   "address": "string (optional)",      // Wallet address to query  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-8-4)   "tokens": ["string"] (optional)",    // Array of token symbols or addresses  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-8-5)   "fetchAll": false                    // Fetch all tokens in wallet, not just those in token list [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-8-6) }`

**Response Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-9-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-9-2)   "balances": {    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-9-3)     "TOKEN_SYMBOL": 1234.56  // Token symbol/address as key, balance as number  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-9-4)   } [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-9-5) }`

### Transaction Polling[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#transaction-polling "Permanent link")

Polls the status of a submitted transaction.

**Request Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-10-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-10-2)   "network": "string (optional)",         // Network identifier  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-10-3)   "signature": "string",                  // Transaction signature/hash  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-10-4)   "tokens": ["string"] (optional)",       // Token symbols/addresses for balance change calculation  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-10-5)   "walletAddress": "string (optional)"    // Wallet address for balance change calculation [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-10-6) }`

**Response Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-2)   "currentBlock": 12345,              // Current block number  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-3)   "signature": "string",              // Transaction signature  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-4)   "txBlock": 12340 | null,            // Block where transaction was included  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-5)   "txStatus": 0 | 1 | -1,             // 0=PENDING, 1=CONFIRMED, -1=FAILED  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-6)   "fee": 0.001 | null,                // Transaction fee paid  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-7)   "tokenBalanceChanges": {            // Optional: token balance changes    [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-8)     "TOKEN": 100.5                    // Change amount for each token  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-9)   },  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-10)   "txData": {} | null,                // Additional transaction data  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-11)   "error": "string (optional)"        // Error message if failed [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-11-12) }`

### Gas/Fee Estimation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#gasfee-estimation "Permanent link")

Estimates transaction fees for the network.

**Request Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-12-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-12-2)   "network": "string (optional)"  // Network identifier [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-12-3) }`

**Response Schema:**

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-13-1) {   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-13-2)   "feePerComputeUnit": 0.000001,  // Fee per compute unit or gas unit  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-13-3)   "denomination": "string",       // Unit denomination ("lamports" for Solana, "gwei" for Ethereum)  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-13-4)   "computeUnits": 200000,          // Default compute units/gas limit used for calculation  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-13-5)   "feeAsset": "string",            // Native currency symbol (ETH, SOL, etc.)  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-13-6)   "fee": 0.002,                    // Total estimated fee using default limits  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-13-7)   "timestamp": 1234567890          // Unix timestamp of estimate [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-13-8) }`

### Transaction Status Enum[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#transaction-status-enum "Permanent link")

All chains use a standardized transaction status enum:

*   `0` = **PENDING**: Transaction submitted but not yet confirmed
*   `1` = **CONFIRMED**: Transaction successfully confirmed on-chain
*   `-1` = **FAILED**: Transaction failed or was rejected

Adding New Networks[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#adding-new-networks "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Gateway's modular architecture makes it easy to add support for new EVM- and SVM-based blockchain networks.

1.  **Create network configuration file:**
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-14-1) # /conf/chains/ethereum/mynetwork.yml [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-14-2) nodeURL: "https://rpc.mynetwork.com" [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-14-3) chainId: 12345 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-14-4) nativeCurrencySymbol: "MYT" [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/chains/#__codelineno-14-5) minGasPrice: 0.1`
    
2.  **Add token list:** Create `/conf/tokens/ethereum/mynetwork.json` with supported tokens
    
3.  **Update connectors** Update each supported connector's configuration file (i.e. `uniswap.config.ts`) to include the new network

# CLOB Connectors - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/clob/#clob-connector-types)

CLOB Connectors
===============

CLOB (Central Limit Order Book) connectors integrate into a CLOB exchange's WebSocket API, enabling standardized order placement/cancellation and order book data fetching from the perspective of Hummingbot strategies. These connectors work with both centralized exchanges (CEX) and decentralized exchanges (DEX) that utilize a central limit order book model.

Each connector is customized for a particular exchange's idiosyncrasies to enable this level of standardization, so they should ideally have a maintainer, whose role is to ensure consistent performance by fixing bugs, incorporating API updates, and other ongoing work.

### CLOB Connector Types[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/clob/#clob-connector-types "Permanent link")

Currently, Hummingbot supports two CLOB connector standards, each which define how the code encapsulated in a connector folder should offer standardized API endpoints and hook into the Hummingbot client.

*   **CLOB Spot**: WebSocket-based connectors to an exchange's spot order book-based markets. Each connector is a folder in the [`hummingbot/connector/exchange`](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/tree/master/hummingbot/connector/exchange)
     folder.
    
*   **CLOB Perp**: WebSocket-based connectors to an exchange's perpetual futures order book-based markets. Each connector is a folder in the [`hummingbot/connector/derivative`](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot/tree/master/hummingbot/connector/derivative)
     folder. By convention, these connector names end in `_perpetual`.
    

These connector standards allow users to create [Strategies](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/)
 and [Scripts](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/scripts/)
 that can operate on different spot and perpetual markets without modification.

### Current CLOB Connectors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/clob/#current-clob-connectors "Permanent link")

Here are the CLOB connectors in the codebase for the current [Epoch](https://hummingbot.org/about/epochs/)
. Note that the Foundation prioritizes fixes for connectors from exchanges that are [sponsors or partners](https://hummingbot.org/about/sponsors/)
, so they tend to be more reliable and better maintained.

| Exchange | Foundation Partner | Spot | Perp | Connector Guide |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [Binance](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/binance/) | ✓   | ✓   | ✓   | [Guide](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-binance-with-hummingbot/) |
| [Bitmart](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/bitmart/) | ✓   | ✓   | ✓   |     |
| [Derive](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/derive/) | ✓   | ✓   | ✓   | [Guide](https://hummingbot.org/blog/running-a-trading-bot-with-hummingbot-on-derive/) |
| [dYdX](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/dydx/) | ✓   |     | ✓   | [Guide](https://hummingbot.org/blog/announcing-the-new-dydx-v4-connector-in-hummingbot/) |
| [Gate.io](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/gate-io/) | ✓   | ✓   | ✓   |     |
| [HTX](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/htx/) | ✓   | ✓   |     |     |
| [Kucoin](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/kucoin/) | ✓   | ✓   | ✓   |     |
| [OKX](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/okx/) | ✓   | ✓   | ✓   |     |
| [XRPL](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/xrpl/) | ✓   | ✓   |     | [Guide](https://hummingbot.org/blog/-using-xrp-ledger-with-hummingbot/) |
| [AscendEx](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/ascendex/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [BingX](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/bing_x/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [Bitstamp](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/bitstamp/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [Bitrue](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/bitrue/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [Bitget](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/bitget/) | ✓   | ✓   | ✓   |     |
| [Bybit](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/bybit/) |     | ✓   | ✓   |     |
| [BTC Markets](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/btc-markets/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [Coinbase](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/coinbase/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [Cube](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/cube/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [Dexalot](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/dexalot/) |     | ✓   | ✓   | [Guide](https://hummingbot.org/blog/using-dexalot-with-hummingbot/) |
| [Kraken](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/kraken/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [MEXC](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/mexc/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [NDAX](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/ndax/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [Vertex](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/vertex/) |     | ✓   |     |     |
| [Foxbit](https://hummingbot.org/exchanges/foxbit/) |     | ✓   |     |     |

### Building CLOB Connectors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/clob/#building-clob-connectors "Permanent link")

The Notion templates below summarize the file and functionalities needed to build the latest spot and perpetual connectors standards and support V2 Strategies:

*   [Spot Connector v2.1 Notion Template](https://hummingbot-foundation.notion.site/Spot-Connector-v2-1-1cc43830938445c9974f43ef861d59f1)
    : Use this template to build `CLOB spot` connectors that conform
*   [Perp Connector v2.1 Notion Template](https://hummingbot-foundation.notion.site/Perp-Connector-v2-1-57d8391eb54c40929f77067355fd551e)
    : Use this template to build `CLOB perp` connectors that conform

See [Building Connectors](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/connectors/build/)
 for more information.

If the exchange is not yet supported by Hummingbot, you can submit a governance proposal for it to be included. New connectors may be contributed by community members via [New Connector Proposals](https://hummingbot.org/about/proposals/)
, which require a pull request with the connector code to the Hummingbot Github repo, along with a minimum HBOT balance to create.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+CLOB%20Connectors+-+/connectors/clob/)
.
