# Hummingbot Client Quickstart - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#hummingbot-client-quickstart)

Hummingbot Client Quickstart[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#hummingbot-client-quickstart "Permanent link")

======================================================================================================================================

This guide walks you through installing the Hummingbot Client using Docker, the simplest method for most users.

For source installation or detailed configuration options, see [Client Installation](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/)
.

What You'll Set Up[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#what-youll-set-up "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

By the end of this guide, you'll have:

*   **Hummingbot Client** - CLI-based trading bot for centralized exchanges (CEX)
*   **Gateway** (optional) - Middleware for trading on decentralized exchanges (DEX) like Uniswap, PancakeSwap, and Raydium

This setup is best for running a single bot instance on your local machine or learning how Hummingbot works.

Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Install Docker on your system:

macOSLinuxWindows

Install Docker Desktop from the [official Docker website](https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/mac-install/)

**Desktop Users:** Install Docker Desktop from [official site](https://docs.docker.com/desktop/install/linux-install/)

**Headless Servers** (VPS like AWS EC2 or Digital Ocean):

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-0-1) curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com -o get-docker.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-0-2) sh get-docker.sh`

Prerequisites

*   Docker Desktop installed
*   WSL2 enabled
*   Ubuntu distribution installed

**Always run commands in:** Ubuntu Terminal (Start Menu → Ubuntu)

Step 1: Clone the Repository[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#step-1-clone-the-repository "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-1-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot.git [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-1-2) cd hummingbot`

Step 2: Start Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#step-2-start-hummingbot "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-2-1) docker compose up -d`

This downloads the latest Hummingbot image and starts it in the background.

Step 3: Attach to Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#step-3-attach-to-hummingbot "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-3-1) docker attach hummingbot`

You should see the Hummingbot welcome screen:

![welcome screen](https://hummingbot.org/assets/img/welcome.png)

Step 4: Set Your Password[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#step-4-set-your-password "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

On first launch, you'll be prompted to create a password. This password encrypts your exchange API keys and other sensitive data.

Step 5: Connect an Exchange[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#step-5-connect-an-exchange "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Use the `connect` command to add your exchange API keys:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-4-1) connect binance`

Follow the prompts to enter your API key and secret.

Common Commands[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#common-commands "Permanent link")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Command | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `connect [exchange]` | Add exchange API keys |
| `balance` | View your balances |
| `create` | Create a new strategy |
| `start` | Start a strategy |
| `stop` | Stop the current strategy |
| `exit` | Exit Hummingbot |

Managing Your Instance[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#managing-your-instance "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Detach Without Stopping[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#detach-without-stopping "Permanent link")

Press Ctrl + P then Ctrl + Q to return to your terminal while keeping Hummingbot running.

### Re-attach[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#re-attach "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-5-1) docker attach hummingbot`

### Stop Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#stop-hummingbot "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-6-1) docker compose down`

### Update to Latest Version[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#update-to-latest-version "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-7-1) docker compose down [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-7-2) docker pull hummingbot/hummingbot:latest [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-7-3) docker compose up -d`

Gateway for DEX Trading[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#gateway-for-dex-trading "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To trade on decentralized exchanges like Uniswap, PancakeSwap, or Raydium, you can enable Gateway alongside Hummingbot. The Docker Compose file includes Gateway configuration that's commented out by default.

### Enable Gateway[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#enable-gateway "Permanent link")

Edit `docker-compose.yml` and uncomment the Gateway-related lines:

  `[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-1)   gateway:    [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-2)     restart: always    [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-3)     container_name: gateway    [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-4)     image: hummingbot/gateway:latest    [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-5)     ports:      [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-6)       - "15888:15888"    [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-7)     volumes:      [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-8)       - "./gateway_files/conf:/home/gateway/conf"      [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-9)       - "./gateway_files/logs:/home/gateway/logs"      [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-10)       - "./certs:/home/gateway/certs"    [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-11)     environment:      [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-12)       - GATEWAY_PASSPHRASE=admin      [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-8-13)       - DEV=true`

The `GATEWAY_PASSPHRASE` is used to encrypt your wallet private keys. Change `admin` to a secure passphrase.

### Start Both Services[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#start-both-services "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-9-1) docker compose up -d`

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-10-1) [+] Running 3/3  [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-10-2)  ✔ Network hummingbot_default  Created [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-10-3)  ✔ Container hummingbot        Started [](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-10-4)  ✔ Container gateway           Started`

### Verify Gateway Connection[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#verify-gateway-connection "Permanent link")

Attach to Hummingbot:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#__codelineno-11-1) docker attach hummingbot`

After setting your password, you should see **Gateway: ONLINE** in the upper right corner.

Development Mode

By default, Gateway runs in development mode (`DEV=true`) which uses HTTP for easier setup. For production environments requiring HTTPS, set `DEV=false` and ensure certificates are properly configured. See [Gateway Installation](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/)
 for details.

Next Steps[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#next-steps "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [Basic Features](https://hummingbot.org/client/)
     - Learn the Hummingbot CLI commands
*   [Connect to Exchanges](https://hummingbot.org/client/connect/)
     - Add your exchange credentials
*   [Create a Strategy](https://hummingbot.org/strategies/)
     - Start trading
*   [Updating to New Versions](https://hummingbot.org/installation/update/)
     - Keep your installation current

Need More Options?[¶](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-client/#need-more-options "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

For source installation, development setup, or advanced configuration, see the detailed [Client Installation](https://hummingbot.org/client/installation/)
 page.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Hummingbot%20Client%20Quickstart+-+/installation/hummingbot-client/)
.

Back to top

---

# Installation - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#hummingbot-api-installation)

Hummingbot API Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#hummingbot-api-installation "Permanent link")

=================================================================================================================================

**The central hub for running Hummingbot trading bots** - now with AI assistant integration via MCP (Model Context Protocol).

The Hummingbot API provides a comprehensive trading platform with three ways to interact:

1.  **🤖 MCP (AI Assistant)** - Control your trading with Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini using natural language
2.  **📊 Dashboard** - Visual web interface for bot management and monitoring
3.  **🔧 Swagger UI** - Full REST API access for developers and power users

Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#prerequisites "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Docker** and Docker Compose installed
*   **Git** for cloning the repository
*   Python 3.10+ and Conda (for source installation only)
*   Exchange API keys (can be added after installation)

Quick Start (Docker - Recommended)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#quick-start-docker-recommended "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Clone the repository and run the setup script:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-0-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api.git [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-0-2) cd hummingbot-api [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-0-3) make setup [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-0-4) make deploy`

### Setup Process[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#setup-process "Permanent link")

The script will prompt you for:

**Credentials** (required): - Config password (for encrypting bot credentials) - API username and password

### What Gets Installed[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#what-gets-installed "Permanent link")

**Core services** (always installed):

*   ✅ **Hummingbot API** (port 8000) - REST API backend
*   ✅ **PostgreSQL** - Database for trading data
*   ✅ **EMQX** - Message broker for real-time communication
*   ✅ **Swagger UI** (port 8000/docs) - API documentation

### After Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#after-setup "Permanent link")

**1\. Access Swagger UI** (Default)

The API documentation is immediately available:

*   URL: [http://localhost:8000/docs](http://localhost:8000/docs)
    
*   Use the username/password you configured
*   Test all API endpoints directly

**2\. Connect AI Assistant** (Optional)

After setup, you can connect AI assistants to control Hummingbot with natural language.

See the **[MCP Installation Guide](https://hummingbot.org/mcp/installation/)
** for complete instructions on connecting:

*   **Claude Code** (recommended) - One-line CLI setup
*   **Gemini CLI** - Google's AI terminal agent
*   **Codex CLI** - OpenAI's coding assistant
*   **Claude Desktop** - GUI application

Install from Source (for Developers)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#install-from-source-for-developers "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you're developing or contributing to Hummingbot API, you can install from source.

### 1\. Clone and setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#1-clone-and-setup "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-1-1) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-1-2) cd hummingbot-api [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-1-3) make setup`

The setup script will prompt you for:

**Credentials** (required): - Config password (for encrypting bot credentials) - API username and password

### 2\. Install dependencies[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#2-install-dependencies "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-2-1) make install`

This will:

*   Create a conda environment named `hummingbot-api`
*   Activate the environment
*   Install all required dependencies
*   Set up pre-commit hooks

### 3\. Start the API in development mode[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#3-start-the-api-in-development-mode "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-3-1) docker compose up emqx postgres -d [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-3-2) conda activate hummingbot-api && uvicorn main:app --reload`

This starts the Broker and Postgres DB containers and runs the API using `uvicorn` with auto-reload enabled for development.

The API will be accessible at `http://localhost:8000`.

Install Python Client[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#install-python-client "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The [Hummingbot API Client](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api-client)
 is a Python library that provides a convenient interface for interacting with the Hummingbot API.

### Install via pip[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#install-via-pip "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-4-1) pip install hummingbot-api-client`

### Basic usage[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#basic-usage "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-1) import asyncio [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-2) from hummingbot_api_client import HummingbotAPIClient [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-4) # Create client instance [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-5) client = HummingbotAPIClient(     [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-6)     base_url="http://localhost:8000",    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-7)     username="admin",    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-8)     password="admin" [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-9) ) [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-10)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-11) # Use the client [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-12) async def main():     [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-13)     accounts = await client.list_accounts()    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-14)     print(accounts) [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-15)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-5-16) asyncio.run(main())`

Verify Installation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#verify-installation "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Once installed, you can verify the API is running:

### Check API health[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#check-api-health "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-6-1) curl http://localhost:8000/health`

### Access API documentation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#access-api-documentation "Permanent link")

Open your browser and navigate to:

*   Interactive API docs: `http://localhost:8000/docs`
*   Alternative API docs: `http://localhost:8000/redoc`

Configuration[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#configuration "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The installation creates a `.env` file with your configuration. You can modify these settings:

*   `API_USERNAME` and `API_PASSWORD`: API authentication credentials
*   `DATABASE_URL`: PostgreSQL connection string
*   `MQTT_HOST`, `MQTT_PORT`: EMQX message broker settings
*   `HUMMINGBOT_IMAGE`: Docker image to use for bots

Troubleshooting[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#troubleshooting "Permanent link")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Database Connection Issues[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#database-connection-issues "Permanent link")

If you encounter PostgreSQL database connection errors (such as "role 'hbot' does not exist" or "database 'hummingbot\_api' does not exist"), use the automated fix script:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-7-1) chmod +x fix-database.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-7-2) ./fix-database.sh`

This script will: 1. Check if PostgreSQL is running 2. Verify that the `hbot` user and `hummingbot_api` database exist 3. Automatically fix any missing configuration 4. Test the connection to ensure everything works

#### "role 'postgres' does not exist" Error[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#role-postgres-does-not-exist-error "Permanent link")

If you see errors like `FATAL: role "postgres" does not exist` in the PostgreSQL logs:

**Cause**: The PostgreSQL container is configured to create only the `hbot` user (via `POSTGRES_USER=hbot`). The default `postgres` superuser is NOT created. This error occurs when something tries to connect using the default `postgres` username.

**Solutions**:

1.  **Always specify the correct user** when connecting:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-8-1) # Correct - use hbot user [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-8-2) docker exec -it hummingbot-postgres psql -U hbot -d hummingbot_api [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-8-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-8-4) # Incorrect - tries to use 'postgres' user (doesn't exist) [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-8-5) docker exec -it hummingbot-postgres psql`
    
2.  **If you need the postgres superuser** (not recommended), you can create it:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-9-1) docker exec -it hummingbot-postgres psql -U hbot -d postgres -c "CREATE ROLE postgres WITH SUPERUSER LOGIN PASSWORD 'your-password';"`
    
3.  **Complete database reset** (⚠️ deletes all data):
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-10-1) docker compose down -v [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-10-2) ./setup.sh`
    

#### Manual Database Verification[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#manual-database-verification "Permanent link")

If you prefer to check manually:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-1) # Check if containers are running [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-2) docker ps | grep -E "hummingbot-postgres|hummingbot-broker" [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-4) # Check PostgreSQL logs [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-5) docker logs hummingbot-postgres [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-6)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-7) # Verify database connection (use hbot user, not postgres) [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-8) docker exec -it hummingbot-postgres psql -U hbot -d hummingbot_api [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-9)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-10) # List all database users [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-11-11) docker exec -it hummingbot-postgres psql -U hbot -d postgres -c "\du"`

#### "database 'hbot' does not exist" During Setup[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#database-hbot-does-not-exist-during-setup "Permanent link")

If you see this error during `./setup.sh`:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-12-1) ⚠️  Database initialization may be incomplete. Running manual initialization... [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-12-2) psql: error: connection to server on socket "/var/run/postgresql/.s.PGSQL.5432" failed: FATAL:  database "hbot" does not exist [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-12-3) ❌ Failed to initialize database.`

**Cause**: The setup script tried to connect to a database named `hbot` (the username) instead of `hummingbot_api` (the actual database name). This was a bug in older versions of setup.sh.

**Solution**:

1.  **Update setup.sh**: Pull the latest version with the fix:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-13-1) git pull origin main`
    
2.  **Or manually fix the database**:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-14-1) # The database already exists, just verify it [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-14-2) docker exec hummingbot-postgres psql -U hbot -d postgres -c "\l" [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-14-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-14-4) # You should see 'hummingbot_api' in the list [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-14-5) # Test connection [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-14-6) docker exec hummingbot-postgres psql -U hbot -d hummingbot_api -c "SELECT version();"`
    
3.  **If database doesn't exist**, run the fix script:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-15-1) chmod +x fix-database.sh [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-15-2) ./fix-database.sh`
    

**Prevention**: This issue is fixed in the latest version of setup.sh. The script now correctly specifies `-d postgres` when running manual initialization.

#### Complete Database Reset[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#complete-database-reset "Permanent link")

If you need to start fresh (⚠️ this will delete all data):

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-16-1) # Stop all containers and remove volumes [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-16-2) docker compose down -v [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-16-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-16-4) # Restart setup [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-16-5) ./setup.sh`

### EMQX Broker Issues[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#emqx-broker-issues "Permanent link")

If bots can't connect to the broker:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-1) # Check EMQX status [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-2) docker logs hummingbot-broker [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-4) # Restart EMQX [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-5) docker compose restart emqx [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-6)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-7) # Access EMQX dashboard (if needed) [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-8) # http://localhost:18083 [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-17-9) # Default credentials: admin/public`

### Port Conflicts[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#port-conflicts "Permanent link")

If port `8000` is already in use on your system, you can change it by modifying the configuration depending on your setup:

#### **Docker**[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#docker "Permanent link")

Update the `ports` mapping in your `docker-compose.yml` file to use a different external port. For example, to use port `8001` instead:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-18-1) services:   [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-18-2)   hummingbot-api:    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-18-3)     ports:      [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-18-4)       - "8001:8000"  # Maps local port 8001 to container's port 8000`

#### **Running from Source**[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#running-from-source "Permanent link")

Edit the `./run.sh` script to include the `--port` flag in the `uvicorn` command. For example, to run on port `8001`:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-19-1) if [[ "$1" == "--dev" ]]; then     [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-19-2)     echo "Running API from source..."    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-19-3)     # Start dependencies and launch API with uvicorn    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-19-4)     docker compose up emqx postgres -d    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-19-5)     source "$(conda info --base)/etc/profile.d/conda.sh"    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-19-6)     conda activate hummingbot-api    [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-19-7)     uvicorn main:app --reload --port 8001 [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-19-8) fi`

Make sure the new port you choose is not already in use.

### Common Issues[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#common-issues "Permanent link")

**Issue**: API won't start - "Database connection failed" - **Solution**: Run `./fix-database.sh` to repair the database configuration

**Issue**: Bot containers won't start - **Solution**: Check Docker daemon is running and you have sufficient resources

**Issue**: Can't access API at localhost:8000 - **Solution**: Verify the API container is running: `docker ps | grep hummingbot-api`

**Issue**: Authentication fails - **Solution**: Check your USERNAME and PASSWORD in the `.env` file

**Issue**: Old bot data causing conflicts - **Solution**: Clean up old volumes: `docker compose down -v` (⚠️ deletes data)

### Development Issues[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#development-issues "Permanent link")

For source installation issues:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-20-1) # Clean conda environment [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-20-2) make uninstall [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-20-3) make install [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-20-4)[](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-20-5) # Check logs [](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#__codelineno-20-6) make run`

Support & Documentation[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#support-documentation "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **API Documentation**: Available at [http://localhost:8000/docs](http://localhost:8000/docs)
     when running
*   **Detailed Examples**: Check the `CLAUDE.md` file for comprehensive API usage examples
*   **Issues**: Report bugs and feature requests through the project's issue tracker
*   **Database Troubleshooting**: Use `./fix-database.sh` for automated fixes

Next Steps[¶](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/installation/#next-steps "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

After installation, proceed to the [Developer Guide](https://hummingbot.org/hummingbot-api/quickstart/)
 to learn how to:

*   Add exchange credentials
*   View your portfolio
*   Place your first order

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Installation+-+/hummingbot-api/installation/)
.

Back to top

---

# Installation & Setup - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#install-with-docker)

Installation & Setup
====================

Hummingbot Gateway is an API/CLI client that exposes standardized REST endpoints to interact with blockchain networks and decentralized exchanges (DEXs). It provides a language-agnostic approach to interacting with these protocols through a unified interface.

New: Manage Gateway via Hummingbot API

**If you installed Hummingbot using the [Hummingbot API Quickstart](https://hummingbot.org/installation/hummingbot-api/)
**, you can manage Gateway directly through the API - no separate installation needed!

The Hummingbot API includes the `manage_gateway_container` tool that handles Gateway lifecycle:

*   **Start Gateway**: Via MCP with "Start Gateway in development mode with passphrase 'admin'"
*   **Check Status**: "Check Gateway status"
*   **Restart/Stop**: Manage Gateway without manual Docker commands

See the [Hummingbot API documentation](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-api)
 for more details on Gateway management through the API.

The methods below are for **standalone Gateway installation** (without Hummingbot API).

There are two main ways to install Gateway standalone:

1.  **Docker Installation** (Recommended for most users)
2.  **Source Installation** (For developers and advanced users)

Install with Docker[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#install-with-docker "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This assumes that you want to use Gateway alongside Hummingbot to enable DEX trading. The Docker process enables seamless communication between the two services.

1 - Navigate to your Hummingbot directory

2 - Edit `docker-compose.yml` and uncomment the Gateway-related lines:

  `[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-1)   gateway:   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-2)    restart: always   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-3)    container_name: gateway   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-4)    image: hummingbot/gateway:latest   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-5)    ports:     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-6)      - "15888:15888"   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-7)    volumes:     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-8)      - "./gateway-files/conf:/home/gateway/conf"     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-9)      - "./gateway-files/logs:/home/gateway/logs"     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-10)      - "./certs:/home/gateway/certs"   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-11)    environment:     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-12)      - GATEWAY_PASSPHRASE=admin     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-0-13)      - DEV=true`

3 - Start both services

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-1-1) docker compose up -d [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-1-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-1-3) [+] Running 3/3  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-1-4)  ✔ Network hummingbot_default  Created                                                                                                                                              0.0s [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-1-5)  ✔ Container hummingbot        Started                                                                                                                                              0.2s [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-1-6)  ✔ Container gateway           Started`

4 - Attach to Hummingbot

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-2-1) docker attach hummingbot`

After setting your password, you should see `Gateway: 🟢 ONLINE` in the upper right corner.

Note

By default, Gateway runs in development mode (`DEV=true`) which uses HTTP for easier setup. For production environments requiring HTTPS, set `DEV=false` and ensure certificates are properly configured.

Install from Source[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#install-from-source "Permanent link")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can install Gateway on a standalone basis and then link it to Hummingbot manually. These instructions assume that you have already installed Hummingbot on the machine where you are installing Gateway, either from source or via Docker. See [Installation](https://hummingbot.org/installation/)
 for how to install Hummingbot.

### Install Prerequisites[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#install-prerequisites "Permanent link")

Install the following dependencies:

*   [NodeJS](https://nodejs.org/)
     (v20.0.0 or higher)
*   [pnpm](https://pnpm.io/)
     package manager

Tip

The new version of Gateway uses pnpm instead of npm because it efficiently handles dependencies with a disk space-saving approach. Since Gateway imports multiple libraries with redundant dependencies, pnpm creates a single content-addressable storage for all packages, significantly reducing installation size and improving performance.

From CLI:

First, install NodeJS 20+ using the `sudo` administrator prefix:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-3-1) # For Ubuntu 20+ [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-3-2) sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y curl [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-3-3) curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | sudo -E bash - [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-3-4) sudo apt install -y nodejs`

Afterwards, install pnpm:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-4-1) sudo npm install -g pnpm`

### Install and Setup Gateway[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#install-and-setup-gateway "Permanent link")

1.  Clone the Gateway repo and navigate into the folder:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-5-1) # Clone repository [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-5-2) git clone https://github.com/hummingbot/gateway.git [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-5-3) cd gateway`
    
2.  Install and build Javascript dependencies defined in `package.json`:
    
    `[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-6-1) pnpm install [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-6-2) pnpm build`
    

### Run Setup Script[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#run-setup-script "Permanent link")

The `gateway-setup.sh` script, located in the root Gateway directory, copies the default Gateway [configuration](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/configuration/)
 files from `/src/templates` to `/conf/` folder.

Run the script:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-7-1) pnpm run setup`

The script will prompt you to select which configurations to update:

*   `server.yml` - Gateway server configuration
*   `chains/` - Chain and network configurations
*   `connectors/` - DEX connector configurations
*   `tokens/` - Token lists for each chain/network
*   `pools/` - Pool lists for each DEX connector

For a fresh installation, select all options. The script will preserve any existing wallet configurations and `defaultWallet` settings.

### Optional: Generate Certificates[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#optional-generate-certificates "Permanent link")

Note

Certificate generation is optional. By default, Gateway runs in development mode (HTTP) which doesn't require certificates. You only need certificates if you want to run Gateway in production mode (HTTPS).

If you want to enable HTTPS mode for secure communication:

1.  Start your Hummingbot [client](https://hummingbot.org/client/)
     and run `gateway generate-certs`
2.  Enter a secure passphrase when prompted
3.  Note the **certs\_path** where certificates are stored
4.  When running `pnpm run setup`, choose to link certificates and provide the path

To connect Hummingbot to Gateway running in HTTPS mode, set `gateway_use_ssl: true` in Hummingbot's `conf_client.yml`.

Running Gateway[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#running-gateway "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Development vs Production Modes[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#development-vs-production-modes "Permanent link")

Gateway can run in one of two modes:

**Development Mode (HTTP)** - Default

*   Started with `--dev` flag or by default
*   Exposes HTTP (unencrypted) REST endpoints
*   Interactive Swagger docs at [http://localhost:15888/docs](http://localhost:15888/docs)
    
*   Shows 🔴 indicator in logs
*   Compatible with Hummingbot when `gateway_use_ssl: false` in `conf_client.yml`

**Production Mode (HTTPS)** - Optional

*   Started without `--dev` flag
*   Exposes HTTPS (encrypted) REST endpoints
*   Requires SSL certificates
*   Shows 🟢 indicator in logs
*   Compatible with Hummingbot when `gateway_use_ssl: true` in `conf_client.yml`

Note

HTTPS is no longer required to connect to Hummingbot. By default, both Gateway and Hummingbot are configured to use HTTP for easier setup. You can change the `gateway_use_ssl` setting in Hummingbot's `conf_client.yml` to switch between HTTP and HTTPS modes.

### Development Mode (Default)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#development-mode-default "Permanent link")

For development mode (HTTP), which is now the default and works with Hummingbot:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-8-1) pnpm start --passphrase=<PASSPHRASE> --dev`

Or simply:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-9-1) pnpm start --passphrase=<PASSPHRASE>`

The passphrase is required for endpoints that handle wallet operations.

If the server has started successfully, you should see:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-1) bigint: Failed to load bindings, pure JS will be used (try npm run rebuild?) [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-3) ╔██████╗  █████╗ ████████╗███████╗██╗    ██╗ █████╗ ██╗   ██╗ [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-4) ██╔════╝ ██╔══██╗╚══██╔══╝██╔════╝██║    ██║██╔══██╗╚██╗ ██╔╝ [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-5) ██║  ███╗███████║   ██║   █████╗  ██║ █╗ ██║███████║ ╚████╔╝  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-6) ██║   ██║██╔══██║   ██║   ██╔══╝  ██║███╗██║██╔══██║  ╚██╔╝   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-7) ╚██████╔╝██║  ██║   ██║   ███████╗╚███╔███╔╝██║  ██║   ██║     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-8)  ╚═════╝ ╚═╝  ╚═╝   ╚═╝   ╚══════╝ ╚══╝╚══╝ ╚═╝  ╚═╝   ╚═╝ [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-9) 2025-04-04 10:09:59 | info |    ⚡️ Gateway version 2.8.0 starting at http://localhost:15888 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-10) 2025-04-04 10:09:59 | info |    Checking for processes using port 15888... [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-11) 2025-04-04 10:09:59 | info |    No process found using port 15888 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-12) 2025-04-04 10:09:59 | info |    🔴 Running in development mode with (unsafe!) HTTP endpoints [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-13) 2025-04-04 10:09:59 | info |    Read token file from conf/lists/solana.json, content length: 619791 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-14) 2025-04-04 10:09:59 | info |    Parsed token count: 3859 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-15) 2025-04-04 10:09:59 | info |    Loaded 3859 tokens for mainnet-beta [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-10-16) 2025-04-04 10:09:59 | info |    📓 Documentation available at http://localhost:15888/docs`

### Production Mode (Optional)[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#production-mode-optional "Permanent link")

For production mode (HTTPS), which requires SSL certificates:

1.  Ensure SSL certificates are properly configured (see "Optional: Generate Certificates" section above)
2.  Set `gateway_use_ssl: true` in Hummingbot's `conf_client.yml`
3.  Run Gateway without the `--dev` flag:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-11-1) pnpm start --passphrase=<PASSPHRASE> --prod`

If the server has started successfully, you should see:

`[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-1) bigint: Failed to load bindings, pure JS will be used (try npm run rebuild?) [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-2)[](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-3) ╔██████╗  █████╗ ████████╗███████╗██╗    ██╗ █████╗ ██╗   ██╗ [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-4) ██╔════╝ ██╔══██╗╚══██╔══╝██╔════╝██║    ██║██╔══██╗╚██╗ ██╔╝ [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-5) ██║  ███╗███████║   ██║   █████╗  ██║ █╗ ██║███████║ ╚████╔╝  [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-6) ██║   ██║██╔══██║   ██║   ██╔══╝  ██║███╗██║██╔══██║  ╚██╔╝   [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-7) ╚██████╔╝██║  ██║   ██║   ███████╗╚███╔███╔╝██║  ██║   ██║     [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-8)  ╚═════╝ ╚═╝  ╚═╝   ╚═╝   ╚══════╝ ╚══╝╚══╝ ╚═╝  ╚═╝   ╚═╝ [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-9) 2025-04-04 10:12:32 | info |    ⚡️ Gateway version 2.8.0 starting at https://localhost:15888 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-10) 2025-04-04 10:12:32 | info |    Checking for processes using port 15888... [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-11) 2025-04-04 10:12:32 | info |    No process found using port 15888 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-12) 2025-04-04 10:12:32 | info |    🟢 Running in secured mode with behind HTTPS endpoints [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-13) 2025-04-04 10:12:33 | info |    Read token file from conf/lists/solana.json, content length: 619791 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-14) 2025-04-04 10:12:33 | info |    Parsed token count: 3859 [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-15) 2025-04-04 10:12:33 | info |    Loaded 3859 tokens for mainnet-beta [](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#__codelineno-12-16) 2025-04-04 10:12:33 | info |    📓 Documentation available at https://localhost:15888/docs`

### Connecting to Hummingbot[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#connecting-to-hummingbot "Permanent link")

Once Gateway is running, go back to your Hummingbot client or restart it if you have exited. In the upper right corner, you should see **GATEWAY: 🟢 ONLINE** if your Hummingbot client is successfully connected to Gateway.

If you see **GATEWAY: OFFLINE**, check that:

1.  Gateway is running on port 15888
2.  The `gateway_use_ssl` setting in Hummingbot's `conf_client.yml` matches your Gateway mode (false for HTTP, true for HTTPS)
3.  If using HTTPS, certificates are properly configured in both Gateway and Hummingbot

Interactive Swagger Docs[¶](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/installation/#interactive-swagger-docs "Permanent link")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

![swagger](https://hummingbot.org/gateway/swagger.png)

Gateway provides interactive API documentation through Swagger UI when running in development mode. This interface allows you to:

1.  Browse all available API endpoints
2.  Test API calls directly from your browser
3.  View request/response schemas
4.  Execute live API calls

To access the Swagger documentation:

1.  Start Gateway in development mode using `pnpm start --passphrase=<PASSPHRASE> --dev`
2.  Open your browser and navigate to [http://localhost:15888/docs](http://localhost:15888/docs)
    
3.  You'll see the Swagger UI interface with all available endpoints grouped by category

Each endpoint in the documentation displays detailed information (method, path, description, parameters, request/response examples) and allows you to test API calls directly by filling in parameters and viewing the server's response.

The documentation is automatically generated from the Gateway route files, ensuring it's always up to date with the latest API changes.

Was this page helpful?

Thanks for your feedback!

Thanks for your feedback! Help us improve this page by [filing an issue](https://github.com/hummingbot/hummingbot-site/issues/new?title=[Feedback]+Installation%20%26%20Setup+-+/gateway/installation/)
.

# Testing Gateway Connectors - Hummingbot

 

[Skip to content](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#testing-gateway-connectors)

Testing Gateway Connectors[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#testing-gateway-connectors "Permanent link")

=========================================================================================================================================

Overview[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#overview "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Comprehensive testing is crucial for Gateway connectors to ensure reliability, security, and compatibility. This guide covers testing strategies, tools, and best practices for Gateway connector development.

Testing Requirements[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#testing-requirements "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

All Gateway connectors must meet these minimum requirements:

*   **Code Coverage**: ≥75% overall coverage
*   **Unit Tests**: All public methods tested
*   **Integration Tests**: All API endpoints tested
*   **Error Cases**: All failure scenarios covered
*   **Performance**: Response times under load tested

Test Structure[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#test-structure "Permanent link")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Directory Organization[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#directory-organization "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-1) test/ [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-2) ├── connectors/ [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-3) │   └── mydex/ [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-4) │       ├── mydex.test.ts           # Unit tests [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-5) │       ├── mydex.integration.test.ts # Integration tests [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-6) │       └── fixtures/                # Test data [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-7) │           ├── tokens.json [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-8) │           ├── pools.json [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-9) │           └── transactions.json [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-10) ├── mocks/ [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-11) │   └── mydex/ [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-12) │       ├── sdk.mock.ts            # SDK mocks [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-13) │       └── responses.mock.ts      # API response mocks [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-14) └── utils/     [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-15)     ├── test-helpers.ts            # Shared utilities    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-0-16)     └── test-constants.ts          # Common test data`

Unit Testing[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#unit-testing "Permanent link")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Basic Test Structure[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#basic-test-structure "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-1) import { MyDex } from '../../../src/connectors/mydex/mydex'; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-2) import { MockSDK } from '../../mocks/mydex/sdk.mock'; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-3) import { fixtures } from './fixtures'; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-4)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-5) describe('MyDex Connector', () => {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-6)   let connector: MyDex;  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-7)   let mockSDK: MockSDK; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-8)  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-9)   beforeEach(() => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-10)     mockSDK = new MockSDK();    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-11)     connector = new MyDex('ethereum', 'mainnet', mockSDK);  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-12)   }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-13)  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-14)   afterEach(() => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-15)     jest.clearAllMocks();  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-16)   }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-17)  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-18)   describe('initialization', () => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-19)     it('should initialize with correct chain and network', () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-20)       expect(connector.chain).toBe('ethereum');      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-21)       expect(connector.network).toBe('mainnet');    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-22)     }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-23)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-24)     it('should load configuration correctly', () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-25)       expect(connector.config).toBeDefined();      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-26)       expect(connector.config.allowedSlippage).toBe(1.0);    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-27)     });  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-28)   }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-1-29) });`

### Testing Swap Operations[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#testing-swap-operations "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-1) describe('swap operations', () => {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-2)   describe('quote', () => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-3)     it('should return valid quote for token swap', async () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-4)       const quote = await connector.quote({        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-5)         base: fixtures.tokens.USDC,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-6)         quote: fixtures.tokens.WETH,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-7)         amount: '1000000', // 1 USDC        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-8)         side: 'SELL'      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-9)       }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-17)      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-18)       expect(Number(quote.expectedOut)).toBeGreaterThan(0);      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-19)       expect(quote.priceImpact).toBeLessThan(0.1);    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-20)     }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-21)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-22)     it('should handle buy side quotes', async () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-23)       const quote = await connector.quote({        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-24)         base: fixtures.tokens.USDC,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-25)         quote: fixtures.tokens.WETH,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-26)         amount: '1000000000000000', // 0.001 WETH        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-27)         side: 'BUY'      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-28)       }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-29)      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-30)       expect(quote.expectedOut).toBeDefined();    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-31)     }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-32)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-33)     it('should throw on insufficient liquidity', async () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-34)       mockSDK.setLiquidity(0); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-35)      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-36)       await expect(connector.quote({        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-37)         base: fixtures.tokens.USDC,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-38)         quote: fixtures.tokens.WETH,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-39)         amount: '999999999999999999',        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-40)         side: 'SELL'      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-41)       })).rejects.toThrow('Insufficient liquidity');    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-42)     });  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-43)   }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-44)  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-45)   describe('trade', () => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-46)     it('should execute swap successfully', async () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-47)       const tx = await connector.trade({        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-48)         wallet: fixtures.wallet,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-49)         base: fixtures.tokens.USDC,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-50)         quote: fixtures.tokens.WETH,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-51)         amount: '1000000',        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-52)         side: 'SELL',        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-53)         slippage: 0.01      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-54)       }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-55)      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-56)       expect(tx).toMatchObject({        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-57)         hash: expect.stringMatching(/^0x/),        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-58)         gasUsed: expect.any(String),        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-59)         status: 'success'      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-60)       });    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-61)     }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-62)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-63)     it('should respect slippage settings', async () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-64)       const spy = jest.spyOn(mockSDK, 'swap'); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-74)      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-75)       expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalledWith(        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-76)         expect.objectContaining({          [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-77)           minAmountOut: expect.any(String)        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-78)         })      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-79)       );    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-80)     });  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-81)   }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-2-82) });`

   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-25)     });  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-26)   }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-27)  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-28)   describe('removeLiquidity', () => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-29)     it('should remove liquidity from pool', async () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-30)       const tx = await connector.removeLiquidity({        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-31)         wallet: fixtures.wallet,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-32)         pool: fixtures.pools.USDC_WETH,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-33)         liquidity: '1000000000000000000',        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-34)         slippage: 0.01      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-3-35)       }); ### API Endpoint Testing[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#api-endpoint-testing "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-1) import request from 'supertest'; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-2) import { app } from '../../../src/app'; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-3)[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-4) describe('MyDex API Endpoints', () => {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-5)   describe('POST /connectors/mydex/router/quote-swap', () => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-6)     it('should return swap quote', async () => {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-7)       const response = await request(app)        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-8)         .post('/connectors/mydex/router/quote-swap')        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-9)         .send({          [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-10)           chain: 'ethereum',          [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-11)           network: 'mainnet',          [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-4-12)           connector: 'mydex', [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-11)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-12)     const start = Date.now();    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-13)     const results = await Promise.all(requests);    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-14)     const duration = Date.now() - start; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-15)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-16)     expect(results).toHaveLength(100);    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-17)     expect(duration).toBeLessThan(5000); // 5 seconds for 100 requests  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-18)   }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-19)  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-20)   it('should cache appropriately', async () => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-21)     const spy = jest.spyOn(mockSDK, 'getPool'); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-22)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-23)     // First call    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-24)     await connector.getPoolInfo('USDC-WETH');    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-25)     expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-26)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-27)     // Second call should use cache    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-28)     await connector.getPoolInfo('USDC-WETH');    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-29)     expect(spy).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(1); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-30)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-31)     // After cache expiry    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-8-32)     jest.advanceTimersByTime(31000); ### Memory Testing[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#memory-testing "Permanent link")

`[](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-1) describe('Memory management', () => {   [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-2)   it('should not leak memory on repeated operations', async () => {    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-3)     const initialMemory = process.memoryUsage().heapUsed; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-4)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-5)     for (let i = 0; i < 1000; i++) {      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-6)       await connector.quote({        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-7)         base: fixtures.tokens.USDC,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-8)         quote: fixtures.tokens.WETH,        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-9)         amount: '1000000',        [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-10)         side: 'SELL'      [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-11)       });    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-12)     } [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-13)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-14)     global.gc(); // Force garbage collection    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-15)     const finalMemory = process.memoryUsage().heapUsed;    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-16)     const memoryIncrease = finalMemory - initialMemory; [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-17)    [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-18)     expect(memoryIncrease).toBeLessThan(10 * 1024 * 1024); // Less than 10MB  [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-19)   }); [](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#__codelineno-9-20) });`

Error Testing[¶](https://hummingbot.org/connectors/gateway-connectors/testing/#error-testing "Permanent link")
